    Add latency measurement and statistics for the audio bridge path: - End-to-end latency (radio → bridge → sounddevice) - Buffer utilization - Expose via /api/v1/bridge endpoint and web UI

    Split from #106 MVP.

    ✅ #111: Audio bridge: standalone CLI mode (without web UI)¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P3

    Add icom-lan audio-bridge subcommand that runs bridge + rigctld without web UI.

    icom-lan audio-bridge --rx-device "BlackHole 2ch" --tx-device "BlackHole 16ch"

    Useful for headless servers where web UI is not needed.

    Split from #106 MVP.

    ⬜ #112: Audio bridge: Linux/Windows platform support¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P3

    Currently macOS only (BlackHole + CoreAudio). Add support for: - Linux: PipeWire virtual device / PulseAudio null-sink - Windows: VB-Cable / WASAPI loopback

    Split from #106 MVP.

    ✅ #113: feat: Toast/notification system for backend → frontend messages¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P3

    Summary¶

    Web UI needs a notification/toast system for backend → frontend messages. Currently there's no way to communicate errors, status changes, or confirmations to the user.

    Use Cases¶

    • Connection errors: "Radio disconnected, reconnecting in 60s..."
    • DX cluster status: "Connected to dxc.nc7j.com" / "DX cluster disconnected"
    • Bridge status: "Audio bridge started (BlackHole 2ch → BlackHole 16ch)"
    • Warnings: "civ_port=0, radio session not ready"
    • Confirmations: User-dismissible dialogs ("Are you sure?")

    Requirements¶

    Toast Notifications (non-blocking)¶

    • Appear in corner (top-right or bottom-right)
    • Auto-dismiss after timeout (5-10s)
    • Color-coded: 🔵 info, 🟢 success, 🟡 warning, 🔴 error
    • Stack multiple toasts
    • UTF-8 emoji support
    • Smooth slide-in/fade-out animation

    Modal Notifications (blocking)¶

    • Center screen overlay
    • Require user acknowledgment (OK / Cancel)
    • For critical errors or confirmations

    Backend API¶

    • WebSocket message type: {"type": "notify", "level": "info|success|warning|error", "title": "...", "body": "...", "modal": false, "timeout": 5000}
    • Python helper: server.notify("Connection lost", level="error", modal=False)

    Design¶

    • Match existing UI style (dark glass panels, Share Tech Mono font, cyan accents)
    • Beautiful, not intrusive
    • Mobile-friendly (responsive)

    Technical Notes¶

    • All rendering in index.html (single-file frontend)
    • No framework — vanilla JS + Tailwind CSS
    • Toast container as fixed-position HTML div, not canvas

    ✅ #114: chore(web): enforce Radio protocol boundaries in web/ (follow-up to #119)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Enforce that web/ package only uses Radio protocol and capability protocols — never IcomRadio directly.

    Problem¶

    from icom_lan.radio import IcomRadio
await radio._send_civ_raw(...)  # bypasses protocol boundary

    Solution¶

    Level 1: Import ban via ruff (CI)¶

    • Added flake8-tidy-imports banned API rule for icom_lan.radio.IcomRadio.
    • Error message: Use radio_protocol.Radio in web/ modules (and capability protocols) instead of IcomRadio.

    Level 2: mypy strict on web/¶

    • Added mypy --strict src/icom_lan/web CI gate.
    • Updated web typing to protocol-first (Radio + capability protocols).
    • Removed direct typing/import dependency on IcomRadio from web/ modules.

    Checklist¶

    • [x] Ruff ban configured for IcomRadio import in web/ boundary checks.
    • [x] Web layer moved to protocol-first typing/capability checks.
    • [x] CI runs ruff check src/icom_lan/web.
    • [x] CI runs mypy --strict src/icom_lan/web.
    • [x] Rule documented in internals docs.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [x] from icom_lan.radio import IcomRadio in any web/*.py file fails CI/lint.
    • [x] All web/ code passes mypy strict with protocol-based types.
    • [x] Rule documented in architecture/contributing docs.

    ✅ #115: bug: CI-V watchdog enters reconnect loop (freq=0, all clients RECONNECTING)¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P0, ui

    Summary¶

    In production use, icom-lan enters a persistent CI-V recovery loop where backend radio state collapses (freq=0) and all Web clients show RECONNECTING with no waterfall/scope data.

    This is server reliability-critical (not UI-specific): reproduces on iPhone Safari and desktop Safari/Chrome.

    User impact¶

    • Web UI frequently degrades to RECONNECTING
    • No spectrum/waterfall/meter updates
    • Connect button appears ineffective because backend radio state is dead
    • Affects all connected clients simultaneously

    Evidence (from runtime logs)¶

    Observed sequence repeats:

    1. CI-V stream stalls 

      civ-data-watchdog: no CI-V data for 2.0s, requesting data start (transport rx_count=26, queue=0)

    2. OpenClose recovery fails for 5s 

      civ-data-watchdog: OpenClose failed for 5.1s, triggering soft_reconnect (1/3)

    3. CI-V transport teardown + reconnect 

      force_cleanup_civ: tearing down CI-V unconditionally
Disconnected
UDP connection lost: None
Soft reconnect to 192.168.55.40 (civ=50002)

    4. Still no CI-V data; loop repeats every ~20-25s.

    Additionally, /api/v1/state during failure returns zeroed radio values (main.freq: 0), matching UI symptoms.

    Scope¶

    • Reproduced with active web clients on LAN (iPhone + desktop)
    • Reproduced independent of mobile UI branch behavior (backend failure persists)

    Tree-of-Thought analysis (pruned)¶

    Candidate branches¶

    A. WebSocket/JS frontend instability
    B. Browser-specific network issue
    C. CI-V transport watchdog/recovery strategy flaw (backend)

    Evaluation¶

    • A: low completeness (cannot explain desktop failures simultaneously)
    • B: low robustness (cross-browser failures observed)
    • C: highest fit with logs (no CI-V data + reconnect loop + zeroed backend state)

    Selected path: C (backend CI-V recovery loop).

    Likely root-cause hypothesis¶

    _civ_data_watchdog_loop escalates to reconnect too aggressively for IC-7610 session-release behavior.

    Current constants in src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py: - _OPENCLOSE_DEADLINE = 5.0 - _RECONNECT_PAUSE = 15.0

    Known field behavior for IC-7610 LAN sessions requires longer cool-down (often 30–60s) after teardown. Current 15s pause likely causes reconnect attempts while radio session is still not fully releasable, resulting in repeated dead-on-arrival CI-V streams (rx_count barely changes).

    Secondary contributing factor¶

    After reconnect, web server re-enables scope for active handlers (scope: re-enable queued after reconnect), potentially increasing CI-V pressure during fragile recovery windows.

    Proposed fix plan¶

    1. Stabilize recovery policy
    2. Increase minimum reconnect pause to >=45s for IC-7610
    3. Add exponential backoff (e.g., 15s -> 30s -> 60s) with cap

    4. Add cooldown gate to avoid immediate repeated reconnect churn

    5. Add explicit recovery states + telemetry

    6. Log/emit state transitions: healthy -> recovering_openclose -> reconnect_wait -> reconnecting -> healthy/failed

    7. Add counters/metrics: consecutive stalls, reconnect attempts, last successful CI-V packet timestamp

    8. Scope re-enable throttling

    9. Delay scope re-enable until CI-V stream health is confirmed for N seconds

    10. Soak test

    11. 60-minute run with 2+ web clients
    12. Pass criteria: no watchdog reconnect loop, non-zero freq/state continuity

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • No recurring OpenClose failed ... triggering soft_reconnect loop under normal load
    • /api/v1/state remains non-zero for freq/mode during client usage
    • UI remains connected and waterfall/spectrum stable on iPhone + desktop for >=60 min

    Related code¶

    • src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py (_civ_data_watchdog_loop)
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py (soft_reconnect, _force_cleanup_civ)
    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py (scope re-enable on reconnect)

    ✅ #116: Backend architecture: Single source of truth for radio connection (radio_ready state) to prevent client-induced reconnect storms¶

    Labels: priority:P0

    Problem¶

    Currently, front-end clients (both desktop and mobile) can independently trigger radio_connect messages when they detect a stale state or WebSocket disconnect. When the radio's CI-V stream fails and the backend enters a recovery loop, clients bombard the backend with radio_connect and scope_enable commands.

    This behavior bypasses backend state management and compounds the load on a failing radio connection, often knocking it into a permanent stale state.

    Architectural Decision¶

    The connection to the radio must be exclusively managed by the backend.

    Clients should never issue radio_connect if the backend has auto_reconnect enabled and is handling recovery itself. The backend should act as the single source of truth for radio health, exposing a radio_ready flag.

    Proposed Implementation Plan¶

    1. Backend (src/icom_lan/radio.py & src/icom_lan/web/state.py):
    2. Introduce a radio_ready boolean in the state payload (only true when CI-V stream is actively flowing and healthy).

    3. Ignore radio_connect commands from clients if the backend is already in a recovering state or actively managing the connection.

    4. Frontend (src/icom_lan/web/static/index.html):

    5. Remove client-side auto-connect behavior (sendControl({type: 'cmd', name: 'radio_connect'})) when state.radio_ready is available and the backend is handling it.
    6. Bind UI Connect button and auto-reconnect logic solely to the WebSocket connection (controlConnected).
    7. Gate scope_enable requests: do not send them unless state.radio_ready === true.

    Related Issues¶

    • Relates to #115 (CI-V watchdog loop).
    • Required to stabilize mobile and desktop clients during backend recovery.

    ✅ #117: bug(transport): fix uint16 sequence wrap-around + retransmit buffer eviction¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1

    Problem¶

    IcomTransport currently treats packet sequence numbers as monotonically increasing integers, not uint16 counters with wrap-around.

    Two concrete defects:

    1. rx_last_seq logic breaks after 65535 -> 0/1 rollover.
    2. Code: src/icom_lan/transport.py (_record_rx_seq)
    3. Uses if seq > self.rx_last_seq + 1 and if seq > self.rx_last_seq.
    4. TX retransmit buffer eviction removes min(seq) instead of oldest-by-send-time.
    5. Code: src/icom_lan/transport.py (_track_sent)
    6. After rollover this removes newer packets instead of truly old packets.

    Why it matters¶

    Under sustained traffic (scope flood + keepalive), 16-bit sequence counters roll quickly. Once wrap happens: - gap detection degrades, - retransmit map quality drops, - long-running session reliability declines.

    This is a transport-layer correctness issue, not just observability.

    Reproduction hints¶

    Minimal check showing stale rx_last_seq after wrap:

    from icom_lan.transport import IcomTransport
tr = IcomTransport()
for s in [65534, 65535, 1, 2]:
    tr._record_rx_seq(s)
print(tr.rx_last_seq)  # stays 65535 (incorrect)

    Proposed solution¶

    1. Introduce modulo-aware sequence comparison helpers:
    2. seq_diff = (new - old) & 0xFFFF
    3. treat 0 as duplicate;
    4. treat 1..32767 as forward progress;
    5. treat >32767 as out-of-order/old.
    6. Rework _record_rx_seq using modulo math for gap detection and forward update.
    7. Rework TX buffer retention to true FIFO by send order:
    8. maintain insertion order (OrderedDict or separate queue),
    9. evict oldest inserted packet, not lowest sequence key.
    10. Add targeted tests:
    11. wrap-around happy path (65535 -> 0 -> 1),
    12. retransmit buffer eviction across rollover,
    13. no false large-gap resets at rollover.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Sequence tracking remains correct across multiple rollovers.
    • [ ] Retransmit buffer evicts oldest-by-time, independent of seq key value.
    • [ ] New tests fail on current code and pass with fix.
    • [ ] No regressions in existing transport tests.

    Related¶

    • Related reliability context: #115

    ✅ #118: bug(civ): preserve orphan ACKs (ACK backlog) to prevent strict-path timeouts¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1

    Problem¶

    ACK/NAK handling can drop valid ACKs when there is no active ACK waiter at the exact moment the frame is routed.

    Current behavior¶

    • CivRequestTracker.resolve() returns False for ACK/NAK if _ack_waiters is empty.
    • ACK is effectively discarded (no short-lived backlog).
    • In strict command paths this can surface as CI-V response timed out.

    Code references: - src/icom_lan/civ.py (resolve, ACK branch) - src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py (_execute_civ_raw, strict wait path)

    Why it matters¶

    This creates ACK timing fragility and contributes to strict-mode instability under bursty/queued conditions.

    Observed failure signature (tests and local repro): - test_enable_scope_strict_sends_and_acks times out waiting for ACK, - despite ACK frames being present on the CI-V RX path.

    Reproduction context¶

    A minimal mocked flow where ACKs are present but one gets consumed before next waiter registration can trigger timeout in strict path.

    Proposed solution¶

    1. Add bounded ACK backlog (deque + TTL, small size e.g. 8-32) in request tracker.
    2. On incoming ACK/NAK with no waiter:
    3. enqueue as pending ACK event (instead of dropping).
    4. On register_ack(wait=True):
    5. first attempt to consume matching cached ACK from backlog.
    6. Keep existing ACK sink behavior for fire-and-forget commands.
    7. Add instrumentation counters:
    8. ack_backlog_hits, ack_backlog_drops, ack_orphans.
    9. Strengthen tests:
    10. ACK arrives slightly before waiter,
    11. ACK bursts do not break strict sequence,
    12. stale backlog ACK expires correctly.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Strict paths no longer time out in ACK-arrival races.
    • [ ] Existing fire-and-forget ACK sink semantics remain intact.
    • [ ] tests/test_radio_coverage.py::test_enable_scope_strict_sends_and_acks stable.
    • [ ] New ACK backlog tests added.

    Related¶

    • Likely contributor to reliability incidents tracked in #115.

    ✅ #119: feat(core): introduce profile-driven radio abstraction for true multi-model reliability¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Problem¶

    icom-lan declares multi-model support, but runtime behavior is still too IC-7610-centric in several paths.

    Examples: - model/capabilities behavior can be too static. - Some routing/framing paths still assume IC-7610 command patterns. - Consumer layers need capability-driven behavior, not model assumptions.

    Why it matters¶

    Without robust profile-driven abstraction: - capability reporting can be wrong, - unsupported commands may be sent, - web/rigctld/CLI paths become brittle for new backends/models, - future backend expansion slows down.

    Positioning vs #152¶

    This issue remains the broader abstraction umbrella.

    • 152 implements the concrete shared-core + serial architecture for IC-7610 first.¶

    • 119 remains open until profile-driven behavior is generalized beyond that initial implementation and validated for multi-model operation.¶

    Execution coordination¶

    • Foundation work is tracked in #152 and sub-issues #141-#151.
    • This issue should consume those outputs, not duplicate them.

    Target Architecture¶

    Introduce first-class Radio Profile / Capability Matrix: - model id/name, - supports flags (dual_rx, scope, audio, etc.), - command routing/framing policy (e.g. cmd29 use), - feature constraints and validation rules.

    Scope¶

    • Profile-driven model and capabilities across backends.
    • Capability-driven command routing and guardrails.
    • Consumer contract discipline (web/rigctld/cli use capabilities, not model assumptions).

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Align profile model and runtime wiring with outputs from #152.
    • [x] Ensure model and capabilities are runtime/profile-derived, not hardcoded.
    • [x] Make command routing honor profile framing/support constraints.
    • [x] Ensure unsupported operations fail explicitly and predictably.
    • [x] Add tests covering at least one dual-rx and one single-rx profile path.
    • [x] Validate consumer layers operate via capability checks.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] model and capabilities are correct per active backend/model profile.
    • [x] Command29/framing paths are profile-governed.
    • [x] Web/rigctld/CLI pass without IC-7610-only assumptions.
    • [x] Multi-model paths are tested and documented.

    Related¶

    • 152 (execution epic for shared-core + IC-7610 serial)¶

    • 114 (web protocol abstraction boundary)¶

    • 101 (IC-7300 enablement downstream)¶

    • 107 (future non-Icom backend relevance)¶

    ✅ #120: bug(commands): replace IndexError with validated ValueError on malformed CI-V payloads¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P2

    Problem¶

    Some command parsers raise raw IndexError on malformed/truncated CI-V frames instead of controlled ValueError.

    Affected paths: - parse_mode_response (frame.data[0] access without length check) - parse_meter_response (delegates to _level_bcd_decode without length check)

    Code: - src/icom_lan/commands.py

    Why it matters¶

    This is protocol robustness debt: - untyped exceptions leak through API boundaries, - error handling becomes inconsistent, - malformed network input can produce confusing stack traces.

    Reproduction¶

    from icom_lan.commands import parse_mode_response, parse_meter_response
from icom_lan.types import CivFrame

parse_mode_response(CivFrame(0,0,0x04,data=b""))   # IndexError today
parse_meter_response(CivFrame(0,0,0x15,sub=0x02,data=b""))  # IndexError today

    Proposed solution¶

    1. Add strict payload length validation before indexing/decoding.
    2. Raise ValueError with actionable context:
    3. expected length,
    4. actual length,
    5. command/subcommand.
    6. Add parser test matrix for short/invalid payloads.
    7. Ensure callers can rely on ValueError contract for parse failures.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] No IndexError escapes from these parsers on malformed input.
    • [ ] ValueError messages include command context.
    • [ ] New negative tests added.
    • [ ] Existing parser tests remain green.

    ✅ #121: bug(radios): make get_civ_addr() normalization robust for common model name variants¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P2

    Problem¶

    get_civ_addr(model) does not match expected flexible model-name normalization.

    Current normalization: - input: model.upper().replace(" ", "") - registry keys remain hyphenated (IC-7610, etc.)

    As a result, typical forms like "IC 7610" and "IC7610" fail with KeyError.

    Code: - src/icom_lan/radios.py

    Why it matters¶

    This creates avoidable UX friction and contradicts the intent of tolerant model lookup.

    Reproduction¶

    get_civ_addr("IC-7610")  # ok
get_civ_addr("ic-7610")  # ok
get_civ_addr("IC 7610")  # KeyError
get_civ_addr("IC7610")   # KeyError

    Proposed solution¶

    1. Canonicalize both input and registry keys via a shared normalizer:
    2. uppercase,
    3. remove separators (-, space, optionally _).
    4. Build a normalized lookup map once at import time.
    5. Keep existing aliases backward-compatible.
    6. Add tests for common user-entered variants.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] IC-7610, IC 7610, IC7610 all resolve correctly.
    • [ ] Similar normalization works across all known models.
    • [ ] Error message on unknown models remains informative.

    ✅ #122: enhancement(api): make set_mode(str) case-insensitive with clear validation errors¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2

    Problem¶

    IcomRadio.set_mode() accepts str | Mode, but string mode lookup is case-sensitive (Mode[mode]).

    This makes common user input fail: - "usb", "Usb" -> KeyError

    Code: - src/icom_lan/radio.py

    Why it matters¶

    API is otherwise high-level and ergonomic; strict case requirement is unnecessary and error-prone.

    Proposed solution¶

    1. Normalize string input before enum lookup:
    2. mode_key = mode.strip().upper()
    3. Convert lookup errors into consistent ValueError/CommandError with supported values list.
    4. Add tests for mixed-case and whitespace-padded inputs.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] set_mode("USB"), set_mode("usb"), set_mode(" Usb ") behave equivalently.
    • [ ] Invalid mode names return clear user-facing error.
    • [ ] Existing enum-based usage is unchanged.

    ✅ #123: docs: eliminate docs/runtime drift and add reliability-focused docs sync gate¶

    Labels: documentation, area:docs, priority:P1

    Problem¶

    Project documentation has drift from current code/runtime state.

    Examples observed: 1. docs/PROJECT.md still reports older release/test counters. 2. docs/guide/configuration.md documents ICOM_STRICT_FRONTEND, but codebase does not currently consume this flag. 3. Stability-critical behavior and known caveats are not centralized in a single operational reliability section.

    Why it matters¶

    When docs lag behind runtime reality: - users and contributors make wrong assumptions, - operational debugging is slower, - roadmap/prioritization discussions get noisy.

    Proposed solution¶

    1. Perform a docs integrity pass for reliability-related docs:
    2. docs/PROJECT.md
    3. docs/guide/configuration.md
    4. README reliability/known-issues sections
    5. Either implement documented flags or remove/deprecate documentation for non-existent flags.
    6. Add a lightweight “docs sync checklist” to release workflow:
    7. version,
    8. test counts,
    9. active env flags,
    10. known reliability issues.
    11. Add CI docs check (optional): grep/assertions for critical config keys to avoid stale env var docs.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] PROJECT.md reflects current release/status data.
    • [ ] Config docs match actual supported env vars.
    • [ ] Reliability section links active high-priority reliability issues.
    • [ ] New release checklist includes docs synchronization gate.

    Related¶

    • Reliability focus: #115, #116, #117, #118, #119

    ✅ #124: bug(control): send token-remove on teardown and handle status error-driven cooldown¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1

    Problem¶

    Control-session teardown does not explicitly release token/session on disconnect/reconnect cleanup.

    In current code: - _send_token() supports magic=0x01 (remove), but this path appears unused. - disconnect() / reconnect cleanup currently closes transports without explicit token remove call.

    Code refs: - src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py (_send_token, magic semantics) - src/icom_lan/radio.py (disconnect, _reconnect_loop cleanup paths)

    Why it matters¶

    wfview explicitly sends token removal on teardown (sendToken(0x01)), and also treats status error signals (e.g. 0xffffffff) as session-level rejection requiring cooldown.

    Reference: - references/wfview/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp:101-103 (token removal) - references/wfview/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp:668-686 (sendToken impl) - references/wfview/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp:315-319 (status connection-failed handling)

    Without explicit session release, reconnect attempts can race stale server-side session state and amplify reconnect loops.

    Proposed solution¶

    1. On intentional full disconnect, send token remove (_send_token(0x01)) before control transport close.
    2. On reconnect fallback paths that fully tear down control session, attempt token remove when feasible.
    3. Parse control status error code (STATUS packet error field) and surface explicit failure reasons/metrics.
    4. Add cooldown policy branch when radio reports session rejection (avoid immediate churn).

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] disconnect() attempts token remove before closing control transport.
    • [ ] reconnect cleanup path attempts token remove when control channel is still valid.
    • [ ] status error codes are logged/observable and influence reconnect cooldown path.
    • [ ] regression tests cover token-remove invocation path and status-error handling.

    Related¶

    • Reliability umbrella: #115

    ✅ #125: test(integration): reliability regression suite for transport/ACK/session lifecycle¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P0

    Problem¶

    Critical reliability paths are still under-covered by real-radio integration tests. We fixed core bugs around sequence wrap-around and ACK backlog, but we do not yet have stable regression tests that prove behavior under long-running and contention scenarios.

    Scope (from reliability backlog items 1-5)¶

    1. Sequence wrap-around on transport/control paths under live traffic.
    2. ACK tracker mixed wait/non-wait stress with civ_stats verification.
    3. Long-duration session longevity (token renewal + CI-V/audio activity).
    4. Multi-client session contention to validate civ_port=0/session rejection recovery.
    5. radio_ready state-machine transitions around connect/recover/disconnect.

    Why this matters¶

    Without these tests, regressions can reappear silently in exactly the paths that previously caused session degradation, cascading reconnects, and operator-visible outages.

    Proposed implementation¶

    • Add marker-based integration tests with explicit env gates for destructive/long scenarios.
    • Reuse existing logging helpers for measurable assertions.
    • Use bounded-time stress loops suitable for CI-local runs; keep true longevity via explicit long-run env gate.
    • Validate radio.civ_stats() and radio.radio_ready transitions explicitly.
    • Include two-client contention scenario in one test to validate recovery and isolation.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • New tests are added and documented.
    • Tests pass on IC-7610 lab setup with expected gated skips when env flags are absent.
    • Failures produce actionable diagnostics (stats + timing + state transitions).

    Related¶

    • 117 (sequence wrap-around fix)¶

    • 118 (ACK backlog fix)¶

    • 115 (watchdog reconnect loop)¶

    • 116 (radio_ready architecture)¶

    ✅ #126: test(integration): add roundtrip coverage for control APIs (data/rf/af/squelch/nb/nr/ip+/state)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2

    Problem¶

    Integration coverage is focused on core frequency/mode/power flows. Several public control APIs are not protected by real-radio roundtrip tests, which leaves behavior drift undetected.

    Scope (from backlog items 6-9)¶

    1. DATA mode roundtrip.
    2. RF gain / AF level / squelch roundtrip and range sanity.
    3. NB/NR/IP+/DIGI-SEL toggle and conflict handling checks.
    4. snapshot_state()/restore_state() transactional restore verification.

    Proposed implementation¶

    • Add integration tests that:
    • capture baseline,
    • apply deterministic changes,
    • verify readback,
    • restore baseline in finally blocks.
    • Keep tests safety-first (no TX required).
    • Add env gate for optional aggressive frontend checks if needed.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • New integration tests cover all methods above.
    • Tests demonstrate successful restore to baseline values.
    • Failures include enough context for immediate repro.

    Related¶

    • 123 (docs/runtime drift)¶

    • 119 (profile-driven model abstraction)¶

    ✅ #127: test(integration): cover PCM audio path and scope capture APIs on real radio¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P2

    Problem¶

    Current integration tests validate Opus RX/TX but do not validate PCM audio paths or scope capture APIs against real hardware.

    Scope (from backlog items 10-11)¶

    1. PCM audio RX/TX integration coverage.
    2. Scope enable/capture/disable integration coverage.

    Proposed implementation¶

    • Add PCM integration tests using codec-backed path:
    • start_audio_rx_pcm / stop_audio_rx_pcm
    • start_audio_tx_pcm / push_audio_tx_pcm / stop_audio_tx_pcm (gated)
    • Add scope integration tests:
    • enable_scope(policy=verify)
    • capture_scope_frames(count>=1)
    • disable_scope
    • Keep TX and long scope capture behind explicit env gates.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • PCM RX validated by receiving decoded frames.
    • PCM TX/full path validated with gate.
    • Scope capture returns at least one frame with valid bins.

    Related¶

    • 66 (historical scope/CI-V contention)¶

    • 70 (audio stream architecture)¶

    ✅ #128: test(integration): codify negative auth/connect coverage and migrate legacy script checks¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2

    Problem¶

    We still rely on ad-hoc diagnostic scripts for some integration checks. Negative auth/connect behavior is not codified as marker-based pytest integration tests.

    Scope (from backlog items 12-13)¶

    1. Negative auth/connect scenarios (bad credentials, host unreachable/session denied).
    2. Convert legacy integration scripts to pytest tests (or retire with clear replacement).

    Proposed implementation¶

    • Add pytest integration tests for negative paths with explicit env gates:
    • invalid password/username
    • unreachable host/port timeout behavior
    • expected exception classes/messages
    • Migrate tests/integration/test_connect.py and test_get_freq.py script logic into pytest modules (or deprecate scripts with references).

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • Negative-path integration tests are runnable via -m integration and gated.
    • Legacy scripts are no longer the only validation path.
    • Documentation updated with new env vars and test targets.

    ✅ #129: epic(test): complete integration reliability backlog (items 1-13)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P0

    Objective¶

    Track and deliver full integration-test expansion backlog (items 1-13) focused on reliability and regression prevention.

    Implementation plan¶

    • [x] #125 Transport/ACK/session lifecycle reliability tests (items 1-5)
    • [x] #126 Control API roundtrip coverage (items 6-9)
    • [x] #127 PCM + Scope integration coverage (items 10-11)
    • [x] #128 Negative auth/connect + legacy script migration (items 12-13)

    Full checklist (traceability)¶

    • [x] 1. Sequence wrap-around integration coverage
    • [x] 2. ACK mixed wait/non-wait stress + civ_stats assertions
    • [x] 3. Longevity soak coverage with token renewal
    • [x] 4. Multi-client contention scenario
    • [x] 5. radio_ready state transition checks
    • [x] 6. get/set data mode roundtrip
    • [x] 7. RF gain / AF level / squelch roundtrip
    • [x] 8. NB / NR / IP+ / DIGI-SEL behavior and conflicts
    • [x] 9. snapshot_state / restore_state roundtrip
    • [x] 10. PCM RX/TX integration tests
    • [x] 11. Scope enable/capture/disable integration tests
    • [x] 12. Negative auth/connect tests
    • [x] 13. Migrate legacy integration scripts to pytest

    Validation summary (2026-03-05)¶

    • Reliability matrix: 5 passed
    • Full backlog package (tests/integration/test_{reliability,controls,media,negative,connect,get_freq}): 14 passed, 2 skipped
    • expected skips: optional audio codec backend unavailable, PCM TX gate disabled
    • Full regression suite: 1797 passed, 95 skipped

    Existing related work¶

    • 117 sequence wrap fix (closed)¶

    • 118 ACK backlog fix (closed)¶

    • 115 watchdog reconnect loop (open)¶

    • 116 radio_ready architecture (open)¶

    • 119 profile-driven radio abstraction (open)¶

    ✅ #130: feat(civ): IC-7610 DSP level controls parity (APF/NR/PBT/NB/filter/AF-mute)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Implement IC-7610 command parity for level-style DSP controls currently missing from icom-lan.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: L

    Why¶

    icom-lan already supports base controls (freq/mode/power/AF/RF/NB/NR/IP+/scope core), but IC-7610 has a broader CI-V command surface that wfview supports.

    This issue covers missing level commands in 0x14, 0x16, and 0x1A groups that are required for full operator control parity.

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • IC-7610 command matrix: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • CI-V command handling: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp
    • Commander interface: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/icomcommander.h

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • CI-V builders/parsers: src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • High-level API: src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • CI-V RX/state projection: src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py
    • Web polling/query model: src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py

    In scope commands¶

    From IC-7610.rig: - APF Type Level - NR Level - PBT Inner - PBT Outer - NB Level - DIGI-SEL Shift - AGC Time Constant - Filter Shape - SSB TX Bandwidth - AF Mute Status

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Add command builders in commands.py with strict range validation and typed docs.
    • Add IcomRadio async get/set methods (Pythonic naming, typed returns).
    • Parse unsolicited/response frames in _civ_rx.py and update RadioState + StateCache where applicable.
    • Keep command encoding generic where possible to reduce one-off helpers.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Every in-scope command has tested builder(s) in commands.py.
    • [ ] High-level methods are exposed on IcomRadio with docstrings and type hints.
    • [ ] RX parser updates local state for all readable commands.
    • [ ] Unit tests cover valid/invalid ranges and ACK/NAK behavior.
    • [ ] Integration tests (marked) verify at least one read/write roundtrip per command family.

    Out of scope¶

    • Memory channel commands.
    • Repeater/tone commands.
    • RIT/XIT/TX freq status commands.

    ✅ #131: feat(civ): IC-7610 operator toggles/status parity (AGC/VOX/ANF/compressor/break-in)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Implement IC-7610 parity for operator toggles/status controls (AGC/ANF/VOX/compressor/break-in/etc.) that are present in wfview but not yet exposed in icom-lan.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: L

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • IC-7610 command matrix: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • Runtime dispatch: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py
    • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py

    In scope commands¶

    • AGC Status
    • Audio Peak Filter
    • Auto Notch
    • Compressor Status
    • Monitor Status
    • Vox Status
    • Break-In Status
    • Manual Notch
    • Twin Peak Filter
    • Dial Lock Status
    • Main/Sub Tracking

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Prefer boolean/enum APIs over raw ints where command semantics are discrete.
    • Add state updates from unsolicited frames so Web/rigctld observe physical front-panel changes.
    • Keep receiver-aware behavior (cmd29) explicit for dual-receiver capable commands.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Builders and parser support for all in-scope commands.
    • [ ] Public async API on IcomRadio with clear naming and validation.
    • [ ] Web poller can query/fan-out state for supported read commands.
    • [ ] Unit tests include parsing of cmd29 vs non-cmd29 variants.
    • [ ] Integration tests verify one toggle cycle per command group.

    ✅ #132: feat(civ): IC-7610 VFO/dual-watch/scanning parity¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Close IC-7610 gaps for VFO/dual-watch/band/scanning command family.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: M

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • Command definitions: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • VFO handling paths: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • VFO/split commands: src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • High-level flows: src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • Fire-and-forget orchestration: src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py
    • Radio state updates: src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py

    In scope commands¶

    • Band Edge Freq
    • Tuning Step
    • Scanning
    • VFO Dual Watch Off
    • VFO Dual Watch On
    • Quick Dual Watch
    • Quick Split
    • VFO Main Select / VFO Sub Select / VFO Main/Sub Band (full API symmetry)

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Remove implicit workaround-only semantics where possible; expose explicit operations.
    • Ensure active receiver tracking and dual-watch state are consistent between cache and RadioState.
    • Keep no-response write path non-blocking where safe; use strict mode only where necessary.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] New API methods for in-scope functions with docs and type hints.
    • [ ] RadioState events correctly reflect dual-watch and active receiver changes.
    • [ ] Web command queue supports newly exposed operations.
    • [ ] Tests cover both MAIN and SUB receiver semantics.

    ✅ #133: feat(civ): IC-7610 memory and band-stacking parity¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Implement IC-7610 memory subsystem parity (memory mode/write/clear/contents/band stacking).

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: XL

    Why¶

    This is the largest functional hole vs wfview and requires new payload models/parsers, not just single-byte toggles.

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • Rig command matrix: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • Memory parsing/format context: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp
    • Shared type definitions: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/wfviewtypes.h

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • CI-V frame/builders: src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • Public API: src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • Types/models: src/icom_lan/types.py
    • Integration test scaffolding: tests/integration/

    In scope commands¶

    • Memory Mode
    • Memory Write
    • Memory to VFO
    • Memory Clear
    • Memory Contents
    • Band Stacking Reg

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Introduce typed dataclasses for memory channel payloads.
    • Keep API ergonomic for library users: one-line read/write operations.
    • Add strict parsing with explicit errors on malformed payloads.
    • Consider phased rollout: read-only first, then write/clear.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Typed models for memory entities are added.
    • [ ] Read memory contents works and is covered by tests.
    • [ ] Write/clear/to-vfo behavior works with validation and safety checks.
    • [ ] Band stacking register read/write is implemented.
    • [ ] Integration tests demonstrate no session instability under repeated memory ops.

    ✅ #134: feat(civ): IC-7610 repeater/tone parity (tone+TSQL)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Add IC-7610 repeater/tone control parity (tone enable + tone frequencies).

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • IC-7610 command map: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • Tone encode/decode helpers and command handling: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • src/icom_lan/types.py

    In scope commands¶

    • Repeater Tone
    • Repeater TSQL
    • Tone Frequency
    • TSQL Frequency

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Use typed tone representation (e.g. integer in tenths Hz, or explicit enum/dataclass).
    • Validate values against IC-7610 supported tone tables.
    • Keep API explicit between enable flags and tone frequency settings.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Full get/set support for all in-scope commands.
    • [ ] Clear validation errors for unsupported tone values.
    • [ ] Unit tests for encoding/decoding and range checks.
    • [ ] Integration tests for at least one tone and one TSQL scenario.

    ✅ #135: feat(civ): IC-7610 system/config parity (antenna, CI-V options, mod routing, time)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Implement IC-7610 system/config parity commands (antenna, CI-V options, date/time/UTC, mod routing/levels, REF adjust).

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: L

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • IC-7610 command map: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • Commander implementation: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py

    In scope commands¶

    • Antenna
    • RX Antenna
    • REF Adjust
    • ACC1 Mod Level
    • USB Mod Level
    • LAN Mod Level
    • Data Off Mod Input
    • DATA1 Mod Input
    • DATA2 Mod Input
    • DATA3 Mod Input
    • CIV Transceive
    • CI-V Output (ANT)
    • System Date
    • System Time
    • UTC Offset
    • Dash Ratio

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Distinguish radio config writes vs operational controls in API naming.
    • Add explicit warnings/docs for settings that can affect station behavior broadly.
    • Keep time-related values timezone-aware in docs/tests.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Builders + high-level API methods for all in-scope commands.
    • [ ] RX parsing for readable status responses.
    • [ ] Unit tests for payload formats (especially date/time and routing enums).
    • [ ] Documentation section for "persistent radio settings" safety.

    ✅ #136: feat(civ): IC-7610 transceiver/RIT/TX status parity¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Implement missing IC-7610 status/control commands for transceiver and TX/RIT domain.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • Command matrix: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • Parser/dispatcher behavior: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py
    • src/icom_lan/radio_state.py

    In scope commands¶

    • Transceiver ID
    • Tuner/ATU Status
    • XFC Status
    • Transceiver Status
    • TX Freq
    • RIT Frequency
    • RIT Status
    • RIT TX Status
    • S Meter Sql Status
    • Various Squelch
    • Overflow Status

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Extend RadioState with explicit fields for RIT/TX status where useful.
    • Ensure parser handles both solicited and unsolicited updates.
    • Keep return types Pythonic and stable for downstream users.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] API methods exist for querying/updating in-scope features where writable.
    • [ ] State model captures new status fields.
    • [ ] Web/radio poller can expose critical status updates.
    • [ ] Unit + integration coverage for command/response parsing.

    ✅ #137: feat(scope): IC-7610 advanced scope controls parity (center/during-TX/fixed-edge)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Implement IC-7610 advanced scope controls parity beyond current scope on/off/data/mode/span/ref/speed/vbw/rbw core.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • IC-7610 scope commands: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • Scope command handling: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • Builders: src/icom_lan/commands.py
    • API surface: src/icom_lan/radio.py
    • Web control integration: src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py, src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py

    In scope commands¶

    • Scope Center Type
    • Scope During TX
    • Scope Fixed Edge Freq

    Proposed implementation notes¶

    • Add explicit API methods instead of overloading enable_scope(...) excessively.
    • Validate value domains with clear errors.
    • Ensure behavior is compatible with existing scope capture stream path.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] All in-scope commands have builders and IcomRadio methods.
    • [ ] Web command path can set/read these options.
    • [ ] Scope stream stability tests pass with these settings toggled.

    ✅ #138: feat(api): expose IC-7610 parity commands across API/CLI/Web consistently¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Expose newly implemented IC-7610 commands through public API, CLI, WebSocket, and rigctld-facing state in a consistent way.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: L

    Why¶

    Command builders alone are not enough; parity should be user-visible and one-line usable from the library perspective.

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • Runtime command orchestration: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp
    • Rig capability orientation: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • Public API: src/icom_lan/radio.py, src/icom_lan/__init__.py
    • CLI: src/icom_lan/cli.py
    • Web: src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py, src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py, src/icom_lan/web/static/index.html
    • rigctld cache model: src/icom_lan/rigctld/state_cache.py

    In scope¶

    • Add/standardize public IcomRadio methods for all newly implemented commands.
    • Add CLI entries for high-value controls (non-destructive defaults).
    • Expose WebSocket commands/events for UI-driven controls.
    • Ensure state cache + broadcast events include new telemetry.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] Public API remains Pythonic and typed.
    • [ ] CLI and Web surfaces cover priority features without breaking existing UX.
    • [ ] Backward compatibility is preserved (or explicitly deprecated with notes).
    • [ ] Docs link each user-facing command to API/CLI/Web usage.

    ✅ #139: test/docs: enforce IC-7610 command parity matrix and regression gate¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:docs, type:enhancement, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Create a command parity test+docs gate so IC-7610 function coverage vs wfview is measurable and regression-proof.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: M

    Why¶

    Without an explicit parity matrix, future changes can silently regress command support.

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • Source of truth command list: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • Protocol structs and packet context: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/packettypes.h

    Current icom-lan extension points¶

    • Tests: tests/test_commands.py, tests/test_radio.py, tests/integration/
    • Docs: docs/PROJECT.md, docs/api/commands.md, docs/guide/commands.md

    In scope¶

    • Add a maintained parity matrix (implemented/partial/missing) in docs.
    • Add tests asserting builder presence for all targeted commands.
    • Add parser coverage tests for newly supported responses.
    • Add integration marks for high-risk command families.

    Acceptance criteria¶

    • [ ] CI fails if parity matrix and code diverge for targeted commands.
    • [ ] Docs include clear status and limitations per command family.
    • [ ] Integration suite has explicit parity smoke profile for IC-7610.

    ✅ #140: epic(civ): IC-7610 full command parity vs wfview (134-command matrix)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Epic goal¶

    Reach practical IC-7610 functional parity with wfview command coverage for icom-lan core library API.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: XL

    Baseline (as of 2026-03-05)¶

    Source of truth: wfview IC-7610 profile has 134 commands: - https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig

    Current icom-lan status from code audit: - Fully implemented: 38 / 134 - Partial support: 16 / 134 - Missing: 80 / 134

    Why this epic¶

    icom-lan already has strong transport/audio/scope core, but CI-V command parity is fragmented. This epic tracks all missing command families to make library behavior predictable and complete for agent-driven and human-driven automation.

    Core agent context¶

    When implementing each sub-issue, prefer this layering: 1. commands.py — low-level frame builders/parsers and validation 2. radio.py — typed, Pythonic async API 3. _civ_rx.py — unsolicited + response state projection into cache/state 4. web/radio_poller.py — optional fire-and-forget query/command integration 5. tests/ + docs/ — parity gate and user-facing docs

    Local implementation files (this repo): - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/commands.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/radio.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/types.py

    Protocol/behavior references in wfview: - Packet formats: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/packettypes.h - CI-V command handling: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp - LAN transport/auth patterns: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomudpbase.cpp - LAN session/auth handler: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp - CI-V over UDP path: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomudpcivdata.cpp

    Coverage mapping by sub-issue¶

    • 130: missing DSP/level commands (APF/NR/PBT/NB/filter/AF-mute)¶

    • 131: missing operator toggles/status (AGC/ANF/VOX/compressor/break-in)¶

    • 132: missing VFO/dual-watch/scanning controls¶

    • 133: missing memory + band-stacking command family¶

    • 134: missing repeater/tone family¶

    • 135: missing system/config family (antenna, CI-V options, mod routing, date/time/UTC)¶

    • 136: missing transceiver/RIT/TX status family¶

    • 137: missing advanced scope controls¶

    • 138: expose implemented parity features through API/CLI/Web consistently¶

    • 139: add CI parity matrix test+docs gate¶

    Sub-issues¶

    • [x] #130
    • [x] #131
    • [x] #132
    • [x] #133
    • [x] #134
    • [x] #135
    • [x] #136
    • [x] #137
    • [x] #138
    • [x] #139

    Coordination with existing roadmap¶

    Related work that should not be duplicated: - #119 (profile-driven radio abstraction) - #129 (integration reliability backlog)

    Use this epic for feature parity scope; keep platform-architecture and reliability infra in their dedicated epics.

    Definition of done¶

    • [ ] All sub-issues merged.
    • [ ] docs/PROJECT.md parity status updated with concrete counts.
    • [ ] Command parity matrix in docs is current and CI-protected.
    • [ ] No regressions in existing transport/audio/scope/rigctld tests.

    ✅ #141: refactor(core): extract shared Icom7610CoreRadio from current IcomRadio (no behavior changes)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:tech-debt, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Extract a shared executable IC-7610 logic core (Icom7610CoreRadio) from the current IcomRadio implementation without behavior changes.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: L

    Why¶

    To maximize code reuse for USB backend, we need one shared core for command/state/civ logic, and thin transport/session/audio adapters.

    Agent Context¶

    Current logic to reuse lives in: - src/icom_lan/radio.py - src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py - src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py - src/icom_lan/commander.py - src/icom_lan/civ.py - src/icom_lan/radio_state.py

    Planned target layout (suggested): - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/core.py - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/lan.py (thin wrapper)

    Scope¶

    • Move backend-agnostic IC-7610 logic into a shared core class.
    • Keep current LAN behavior identical.
    • Keep IcomRadio import and behavior working exactly as today.

    Out of scope¶

    • Serial transport implementation.
    • USB audio implementation.
    • CLI/Web/rigctld backend switching.

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Introduce Icom7610CoreRadio class and move shared fields/methods from IcomRadio.
    • [x] Keep CI-V request tracker, commander, state cache, scope assembly, callbacks in core.
    • [x] Keep LAN-specific handshake/session pieces behind LAN wrapper (Icom7610LanRadio or equivalent).
    • [x] Preserve public API signatures (no breaking changes for existing users).
    • [x] Preserve IcomRadio as backward-compatible alias/path.
    • [x] Ensure all existing LAN tests still pass with no expectation updates.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] Existing LAN unit/integration tests pass unchanged.
    • [x] IcomRadio user code continues to work without migration.
    • [x] No duplication introduced between core and LAN wrapper.
    • [x] New architecture supports plugging non-LAN drivers into same core.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Large refactor risk: use small commits and mechanical extraction first.
    • Do not change behavior in this issue; this is structural only.

    ✅ #142: feat(core): add backend driver contracts + create_radio factory/config (LAN default, serial-ready)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Add backend driver contracts (CivLink, SessionDriver, AudioDriver) and a factory/config layer (create_radio) to assemble IC-7610 LAN/Serial backends around shared core.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: M

    Why¶

    This is the pivot point that prevents if/else explosion and allows maximum reuse of shared IC-7610 core.

    Agent Context¶

    Related open issues: - #119 (profile-driven abstraction) - #101 (legacy serial idea; this issue supersedes its backend assembly approach)

    Current entry points to migrate gradually: - src/icom_lan/cli.py - src/icom_lan/web/server.py - src/icom_lan/rigctld/* - src/icom_lan/sync.py

    Suggested files: - src/icom_lan/backends/config.py - src/icom_lan/backends/factory.py - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/drivers/contracts.py

    Scope¶

    • Define internal backend driver interfaces.
    • Define typed backend config models (lan/serial).
    • Implement create_radio(config) and default LAN behavior.
    • Keep backward compatibility (IcomRadio alias to LAN backend path).

    Out of scope¶

    • Full serial transport logic.
    • USB audio engine internals.
    • Full consumer migration (handled in dedicated issues).

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Add typed config dataclasses (backend discriminator + backend-specific fields).
    • [x] Add internal driver contracts for CI-V link, session strategy, and audio path.
    • [x] Implement factory creation for LAN backend (serial stub allowed initially).
    • [x] Keep old constructors/import paths operational (IcomRadio compatibility).
    • [x] Add smoke tests for factory + config validation.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] create_radio(...) can build LAN backend end-to-end.
    • [x] Invalid backend/config combinations fail with actionable errors.
    • [x] IcomRadio remains source-compatible for existing users.
    • [x] No consumer layer is forced to import concrete backend classes directly.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Keep contracts minimal and practical; avoid speculative generalization.
    • This issue should not require changing command behavior.

    ✅ #143: feat(serial): implement SerialCivLink for IC-7610 USB CI-V (framing, health, reconnect guardrails)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Implement SerialCivLink for IC-7610 USB CI-V transport using optional serial dependencies.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Why¶

    This provides the physical CI-V link for USB backend while reusing existing command/parsing/state machinery in shared core.

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • Serial comm layer: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/commhandler.cpp
    • CI-V command parser/dispatcher: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp

    Agent Context¶

    Current code patterns to preserve: - CI-V frame semantics in src/icom_lan/commands.py - Request routing in src/icom_lan/civ.py - Commander pacing/serialization in src/icom_lan/commander.py

    Suggested files: - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/drivers/serial_civ_link.py

    Scope¶

    • Async serial open/read/write for CI-V frames.
    • Stream framing for FE FE ... FD packets.
    • Timeout/reconnect guardrails and health signals.
    • Configurable baud, default 115200 (IC-7610 scope-capable profile).

    Out of scope¶

    • Audio device pipeline.
    • Consumer layer integration.

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Add optional extras dependencies (pyserial, pyserial-asyncio) for serial backend.
    • [x] Implement frame assembler from serial byte stream.
    • [x] Handle partial frames, malformed frame recovery, and collision bytes (0xFC patterns).
    • [x] Implement writer serialization and backpressure-safe queueing.
    • [x] Expose health/connected status for core radio_ready decisions.
    • [x] Add unit tests with mocked serial stream (partial/chunked/error cases).

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] Core can send/receive CI-V frames via serial transport in tests.
    • [x] Framing is robust against packet splitting and concatenation.
    • [x] Recoverable serial I/O errors do not crash the process.
    • [x] Dependency absence yields actionable install hint.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • High RX frame rates (scope enabled) require efficient parser path.
    • Avoid blocking serial read loops; strict asyncio-only behavior.

    ✅ #144: feat(serial): add Icom7610SerialRadio via shared core + serial session driver¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Implement Icom7610SerialRadio by wiring shared Icom7610CoreRadio to serial session/link drivers.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Why¶

    After shared-core extraction and serial link, this issue delivers an actual usable IC-7610 USB backend.

    Agent Context¶

    Depends on: - #141 shared core - #142 contracts/factory - #143 serial CI-V link

    Suggested files: - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/serial.py - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/drivers/serial_session.py

    Scope¶

    • Build serial backend class implementing Radio protocol and optional capabilities.
    • Serial session lifecycle (connect/disconnect/start pumps/cleanup).
    • Hook commander, request tracker, state cache, callbacks via shared core.

    Out of scope¶

    • USB audio implementation internals.
    • CLI/Web/rigctld full migration.

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Add serial session driver (no LAN auth/token handshake).
    • [x] Wire CI-V RX pump/watchdog to serial backend lifecycle.
    • [x] Ensure radio_ready semantics are meaningful for serial path.
    • [x] Implement capability reporting for IC-7610 serial profile (scope, audio, dual_rx, etc.).
    • [x] Add unit tests for connect/disconnect/error transitions.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] Icom7610SerialRadio can execute core CI-V commands in test environment.
    • [x] No code duplication of command/state logic from LAN backend.
    • [x] Serial backend exposes same high-level API surface expected by consumers.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Keep serial session logic thin; avoid moving core behavior into backend class.

    ✅ #145: feat(audio): add UsbAudioDriver for serial backend (auto-detect + override + codec adapter)¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:audio, type:feature, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Implement UsbAudioDriver for serial backend with auto-detect + override device selection and dual-mode codec adapter.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: L

    Why¶

    Serial backend needs first-class audio support while preserving existing consumer contracts (AudioCapable, web audio channel, bridge compatibility).

    Agent Context¶

    Current audio stack to reuse: - src/icom_lan/audio.py - src/icom_lan/_audio_transcoder.py - src/icom_lan/audio_bus.py - src/icom_lan/audio_bridge.py - src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py (audio broadcaster expectations)

    Suggested files: - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/drivers/usb_audio.py - optional helper: src/icom_lan/backends/audio/device_probe.py

    Scope¶

    • USB audio RX/TX using exported system devices.
    • Auto-detect IC-7610-like devices with deterministic fallback.
    • Explicit overrides (rx_device, tx_device).
    • Internal PCM pipeline with Opus adapter as needed.

    Out of scope¶

    • Cross-platform polish beyond macOS first milestone.
    • WebRTC redesign.

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [ ] Implement device discovery/probing API (list, select default, validate selection).
    • [ ] Implement RX and TX streaming loops with lifecycle management.
    • [ ] Integrate with existing AudioBus semantics.
    • [ ] Provide codec adaptation to preserve current external contracts (PCM/Opus expectations).
    • [ ] Add actionable errors for missing deps or device mismatch.
    • [ ] Add unit tests for selection logic and stream lifecycle.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Serial backend supports start/stop RX/TX audio APIs.
    • [ ] Existing web audio channel and bridge workflows continue to work with serial backend.
    • [ ] Auto-detect works, and explicit overrides take precedence.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Device naming varies by OS/driver; keep matching logic tolerant but deterministic.

    ✅ #146: feat(scope): support scope/waterfall on IC-7610 serial backend with baud/pacing guardrails¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Enable scope/waterfall over IC-7610 serial backend with explicit throughput guardrails.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Why¶

    User goal explicitly includes scope in USB MVP. Serial throughput is more constrained than LAN, so implementation must include pacing and safety guards.

    Reference (wfview)¶

    • Scope command handling: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp
    • IC-7610 command profile: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig

    Agent Context¶

    Existing scope path to reuse: - Scope command builders in src/icom_lan/commands.py (0x27 family) - Frame assembly in src/icom_lan/scope.py - CI-V scope routing in src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py - Web scope broadcast in src/icom_lan/web/server.py + handlers.py

    Scope¶

    • Support scope stream for serial backend.
    • Add serial-specific pacing profile and guardrails (baud thresholds, warnings/errors).
    • Preserve existing scope API (enable_scope, capture_scope_frame(s), stream callbacks).

    Out of scope¶

    • SDR external panadapter source.

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Enable scope commands and RX parsing path for serial backend.
    • [x] Add serial pacing profile separate from LAN defaults.
    • [x] Add guardrail for low baud rates with clear error/override path.
    • [x] Add tests for enable/disable/capture on serial fixtures.
    • [x] Verify no regressions in LAN scope behavior.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] Scope frame capture works on serial backend in integration profile.
    • [x] Serial scope doesn’t destabilize core command path under expected settings.
    • [x] Guardrail behavior is deterministic and documented.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Scope flood can starve command responses; preserve drain-all RX pattern and command serialization.

    ✅ #147: feat(cli): unified --backend selection + serial/audio-device flags via factory¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:cli, type:feature, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Add unified CLI backend selection (--backend lan|serial) and serial/audio-device flags.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Why¶

    Implementation agents and end users need one consistent CLI entrypoint to run LAN or serial backend without separate command trees.

    Primary files: - src/icom_lan/cli.py - src/icom_lan/__main__.py - docs: docs/guide/cli.md, docs/guide/configuration.md

    • Global backend selector with LAN default.
    • Serial args: --serial-port, --serial-baud, --serial-ptt-mode.
    • Audio args: --rx-device, --tx-device, --list-audio-devices.
    • Route instance creation through create_radio(...).
    • Keep old LAN usage unchanged.

    Out of scope¶

    • New standalone CLI namespace (icom-lan serial ...).

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [ ] Build backend config from CLI args and call factory.
    • [ ] Make discover command backend-aware (lan only).
    • [ ] Add JSON/plain output for audio device listing.
    • [ ] Add CLI tests for backend selection and argument validation.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] icom-lan works unchanged when backend not specified.
    • [ ] icom-lan --backend serial ... runs all supported commands via serial backend.
    • [ ] Helpful errors for missing serial port/deps.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Preserve backward compatibility and deprecation behavior for existing flags.

    ✅ #148: feat(web): backend-neutral factory wiring + capability-gated behavior for LAN/serial radios¶

    Summary¶

    Migrate Web server wiring to backend-neutral factory path and strict capability gating for LAN/serial backends.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: M

    Web paths still include LAN-centric assumptions. Serial backend must work without hidden transport-specific coupling.

    Agent Context¶

    Main files: - src/icom_lan/web/server.py - src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py - src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py - src/icom_lan/web/static/index.html

    Related issues: - #114 protocol boundaries in web - #119 profile/capabilities abstraction

    • Remove LAN-specific assumptions in hello/capabilities/runtime behavior.
    • Gate scope/audio features strictly by runtime capabilities.

    Out of scope¶

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Ensure radio_ready semantics work for serial backend.
    • [x] Ensure scope/audio channels are enabled/disabled by backend capabilities.
    • [x] Add tests for web handlers with serial-capable mock backend.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] Web UI can run against LAN and serial backend via same flow.
    • [x] No runtime errors when capability is absent.
    • [x] Existing LAN behavior remains unchanged.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Keep protocol-first boundaries; avoid importing concrete backend types into web/.

    ✅ #149: feat(rigctld): support serial backend via factory with backend-neutral handler/poller paths¶

    Integrate serial backend into rigctld startup path and ensure backend-neutral command handling via Radio contract.

    • [ ] Validate handler methods avoid concrete LAN assumptions.

    • [ ] Existing LAN rigctld tests remain green.
    • [ ] No regressions in audit logging and circuit breaker behavior.

    ✅ #150: test(core): add LAN+serial backend contract matrix and regression gate for shared IC-7610 core¶

    Add comprehensive test matrix and regression gate for shared-core LAN + serial backend architecture.

    • Shared Radio contract tests reused across backend fixtures.
    • Unit tests for serial link/audio drivers.
    • Regression gates that ensure LAN behavior remains stable.

    Out of scope¶

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [x] Add serial framing/stability unit tests.
    • [x] Add USB audio driver unit tests (selection/lifecycle/error handling).
    • [x] Add web/rigctld smoke tests using serial mock backend.
    • [x] Keep existing LAN tests unchanged where possible.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [x] CI fails on contract divergence between LAN and serial backends.
    • [x] LAN regression coverage remains intact.
    • [x] Serial-specific failure modes are covered by deterministic tests.

    Risks / Notes¶

    • Prefer deterministic mock-based tests first; keep hardware integration optional/marked.

    ✅ #151: docs(backends): shared-core architecture, IC-7610 USB setup, and migration/compatibility guide¶

    Labels: documentation, area:docs, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Document shared-core backend architecture, USB setup for IC-7610, and migration/backward-compatibility story.

    Priority: P1
    Complexity: S

    Why¶

    Implementation agents and users need clear operational guidance and migration guarantees.

    Agent Context¶

    Docs to update: - docs/internals/architecture.md - docs/radio-protocol.md - docs/guide/cli.md - docs/guide/configuration.md - docs/guide/radios.md - README.md - docs/PROJECT.md

    Scope¶

    • Add backend architecture diagram (shared core + thin adapters).
    • Add serial backend setup guide (macOS first).
    • Add backend capability matrix (LAN vs Serial for IC-7610).
    • Add compatibility note: IcomRadio alias behavior and migration path.

    Out of scope¶

    • Marketing copy and non-technical announcements.

    Implementation Checklist¶

    • [ ] Update architecture docs with new layers and data flow.
    • [ ] Add CLI/config docs for --backend and serial/audio flags.
    • [ ] Add troubleshooting for serial port and USB audio device selection.
    • [ ] Add migration section for existing users.
