    • [ ] Backward compatibility guarantees are explicit and accurate.
    • [ ] Docs align with actual runtime behavior and tests.

    ✅ #152: epic(backends): IC-7610 shared core + USB serial/audio backend (LAN-compatible architecture)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P0

    Epic Goal¶

    Implement IC-7610 USB backend (CI-V over serial + USB audio devices) with maximum code reuse via a shared IC-7610 executable core and thin LAN/Serial adapters.

    Priority: P0
    Complexity: XL

    Problem Statement¶

    Current multi-backend abstraction is mostly contractual (Protocol), while implementation is LAN-centric. We need a reusable IC-7610 core that allows serial backend delivery without duplicating command/state logic.

    Guiding Constraints¶

    • Keep radio_protocol.Radio + optional capability protocols as consumer contract.
    • No behavior regressions for current LAN users.
    • IcomRadio remains backward-compatible alias (LAN path) during migration.
    • macOS-first for serial+USB audio rollout.
    • Optional deps via extras only.

    Agent Starter Context (Read First)¶

    Core code areas: - src/icom_lan/radio.py - src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py - src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py - src/icom_lan/audio.py - src/icom_lan/audio_bus.py - src/icom_lan/web/* - src/icom_lan/rigctld/*

    Existing strategic/related issues: - #119 profile-driven abstraction - #101 legacy serial issue (older assumptions; superseded by this epic architecture) - #114 web protocol boundaries

    External references (protocol behavior only, no code copy): - wfview serial comm handler: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/commhandler.cpp - wfview icom command logic: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp - wfview IC-7610 command profile: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig - wfview packet structs: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/packettypes.h

    Execution Plan (Doable Chunks)¶

    Phase A — Safe extraction baseline¶

    • [x] #141
    • [x] #142

    Phase B — Serial control path¶

    • [x] #143
    • [x] #144

    Phase C — Serial audio + scope¶

    • [x] #145
    • [x] #146

    Phase D — Consumer integration¶

    • [x] #147
    • [x] #148
    • [x] #149

    Phase E — Quality + docs gates¶

    • [x] #150
    • [x] #151

    Global Definition of Done¶

    • [ ] Shared IC-7610 core is used by both LAN and Serial backends.
    • [ ] IcomRadio remains backward-compatible for existing LAN users.
    • [ ] Unified CLI supports --backend lan|serial with serial/audio options.
    • [ ] Web UI and rigctld run through backend-neutral paths.
    • [ ] Serial backend supports IC-7610 core controls + audio + scope (with guardrails).
    • [ ] LAN regression suite remains green.
    • [ ] Docs reflect actual runtime behavior and migration path.

    Coordination Notes for Implementing Agents¶

    • Work strictly TDD-first per repo guidance.
    • Prefer small PRs: one sub-issue = one logical unit.
    • Avoid speculative abstraction beyond IC-7610 shared-core boundary.
    • For serial scope throughput, preserve current CI-V drain-all + command-serialization principles.

    ✅ #153: TX Freq Monitor (0x1C 0x03) not working on IC-7610¶

    Labels: bug

    Problem¶

    TX Frequency Monitor command (0x1C 0x03) does not work on IC-7610 hardware.

    Test results¶

    • SET command: Accepted without error (no NAK)
    • GET command: Always returns False
    • Expected: Should toggle on/off like other features (per wfview matrix)

    Tested on¶

    • Radio: IC-7610
    • Firmware: 1.42 (latest)
    • Connection: LAN (wfview protocol)

    Commands¶

    GET:  FE FE 98 E0 1C 03 FD
SET ON:  FE FE 98 E0 1C 03 01 FD
SET OFF: FE FE 98 E0 1C 03 00 FD

    What this command does¶

    Based on the 0x1C command family and naming: - TX Freq Monitor: Toggles TX frequency display instead of RX on main display - Use case: During split operation (RX: 14.200 MHz, TX: 14.195 MHz) - Expected behavior: Switch which frequency is shown on main display

    CI-V Reference¶

    IC-7610.rig (wfview):¶

    Commands\109\Type=TX Freq
Commands\109\String=\x1c\x03
Commands\109\Min=0
Commands\109\Max=1        ← Should work as 0/1 toggle
Commands\109\SetCommand=true
Commands\109\GetCommand=true

    IC-7300.rig (comparison):¶

    Commands\159\Max=0        ← Only 0 (read-only or OFF-only)

    Note: IC-7610 matrix shows Max=1, but real hardware doesn't respond to SET.

    0x1C Command Family¶

    0x1C 0x00 — Transceiver Status
0x1C 0x01 — Tuner/ATU Status (✅ works)
0x1C 0x02 — XFC Status
0x1C 0x03 — TX Freq Monitor (❌ doesn't work)
0x1C 0x04 — CI-V Output (ANT)

    Hypotheses¶

    1. Not implemented — Icom didn't implement this feature on IC-7610
    2. Always enabled — IC-7610 auto-shows TX freq during split (no toggle needed)
    3. Deprecated — Feature existed in older models, removed in IC-7610
    4. Menu-only — Controlled via radio menu, not CI-V
    5. wfview matrix error — Copied from IC-7300, never tested on real IC-7610

    Impact¶

    • Low priority (minor display feature)
    • Does not block other development
    • Mock tests pass (integration tests work correctly)
    • Other 3/4 RIT/tuner commands work fine (75% success rate)

    Conclusion¶

    Not a bug in our code — this is a wfview matrix vs real hardware mismatch.

    The command is correctly implemented per IC-7610.rig specification, but the real radio (firmware 1.42) doesn't support SET operation.

    Next steps¶

    • [ ] Ask wfview maintainers if they tested this on real IC-7610
    • [ ] Check if older/newer firmware versions behave differently
    • [ ] Consider marking as read-only or unsupported in our docs
    • [ ] Document workaround (if display toggle needed, use radio menu)

    ✅ #154: feat(civ): expose dual-watch public API (partial commands from #140)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

    Goal¶

    Complete the 4 partial commands from Epic #140 command matrix by adding public API, CLI, and Web integration.

    Context¶

    Currently dual-watch state is tracked in RadioState via CI-V receive handlers, but there's no maintained public API for control.

    Partial commands (0x07 family): - 0x07 0xC0 — Dual Watch Off - 0x07 0xC1 — Dual Watch On - 0x07 0xC2 — Dual Watch Query/Status - 0x07 0xD2 — Main/Sub Band (internal, RadioState only)

    Scope¶

    Radio API (commands.py + radio.py)¶

    • [ ] get_dual_watch() -> bool — query current state (0x07 0xC2)
    • [ ] set_dual_watch(on: bool) — enable/disable (0x07 0xC0/0xC1)
    • [ ] Unit tests for command builders/parsers
    • [ ] Integration tests (roundtrip on real IC-7610)

    CLI (cli.py)¶

    • [ ] icom-lan dualwatch [on|off] — get if no arg, set otherwise
    • [ ] Error handling + user-friendly output

    Web API (handlers.py + radio_poller.py)¶

    • [ ] WebSocket handlers: get_dual_watch, set_dual_watch
    • [ ] Radio Poller operation: SetDualWatch(on: bool)
    • [ ] Update Web UI (optional, future work)

    Implementation notes¶

    • Existing CI-V handlers in _civ_rx.py already update RadioState
    • Command builders likely exist in commands.py (check build_vfo_command)
    • Pattern: similar to get/set_split_mode (0x0F family)

    Acceptance¶

    • [ ] 4 partial commands → implemented in parity matrix
    • [ ] get/set_dual_watch work on real IC-7610
    • [ ] CLI subcommand functional
    • [ ] WebSocket handlers registered
    • [ ] Tests pass (3006+ → 3020+)

    Estimated effort¶

    S (small) — ~30-40 minutes with agent

    Related¶

    • Epic #140 (IC-7610 command parity)
    • Issue #132 (VFO/dual-watch/scanning — SET commands done, GET missing)

    ✅ #155: feat: expose antenna tuner (ATU) control via CLI and Web API¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:cli, ui

    Summary¶

    Expose the antenna tuner (ATU) control through CLI and Web API.

    The CI-V layer already has full tuner support — builders (get_tuner_status, set_tuner_status), async radio methods, unsolicited frame handling, RadioState.tuner_status field, and state polling. What's missing is the user-facing integration: a CLI subcommand and REST/WebSocket API endpoints.

    Motivation¶

    The IC-7610's built-in ATU is commonly used before every band change. Being able to query tuner status and initiate a tune cycle from the CLI or web UI (without touching the radio front panel) is essential for remote operation workflows — especially combined with icom-lan's existing band/freq/mode commands.

    Current State¶

    Layer Tuner support Status
    CI-V builders (commands.py) get_tuner_status(), set_tuner_status(0\|1\|2) ✅ Done
    Radio methods (radio.py) get_tuner_status() → int, set_tuner_status(value) ✅ Done
    Unsolicited RX (_civ_rx.py) tuner_changed notify + rs.tuner_status update ✅ Done
    RadioState (radio_state.py) tuner_status: int (0=off, 1=on, 2=tuning) ✅ Done
    State API (/api/v1/state) Exposed via RadioState.to_dict() ✅ Done
    CLI subcommand — ❌ Missing
    Web handlers (WS command) — ❌ Missing
    REST API endpoint — ❌ Missing

    Design¶

    CLI¶

    icom-lan tuner              # GET → "Tuner: OFF" / "Tuner: ON" / "Tuner: TUNING"
icom-lan tuner on           # SET → enable ATU
icom-lan tuner off          # SET → disable ATU  
icom-lan tuner tune         # SET → initiate tune cycle (value=2)
icom-lan tuner --json       # JSON output: {"tuner_status": 0}

    Pattern follows existing dualwatch, att, preamp CLI subcommands.

    Web API¶

    WebSocket command (via existing _COMMANDS dispatch in handlers.py):

    {"type": "command", "id": "1", "name": "get_tuner_status", "params": {}}
→ {"type": "response", "id": "1", "ok": true, "result": {"status": 0}}

{"type": "command", "id": "2", "name": "set_tuner_status", "params": {"value": 2}}
→ {"type": "response", "id": "2", "ok": true, "result": {"value": 2}}

    REST endpoint (optional, lower priority):

    GET  /api/v1/tuner          → {"status": 0, "label": "OFF"}
POST /api/v1/tuner          → {"action": "tune"} / {"action": "on"} / {"action": "off"}

    Data Flow¶

    CLI / REST / WebSocket
        │
        ▼
   handlers.py (_COMMANDS dispatch)
        │
        ▼
   radio.get_tuner_status()  /  radio.set_tuner_status(value)
        │
        ▼
   commands.py (CI-V frame builders)
        │
        ▼
   IC-7610 (0x1C 0x01)

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Add CLI subcommand tuner with get/set/tune/--json (in cli.py)
    • [ ] Add "get_tuner_status" and "set_tuner_status" to _COMMANDS frozenset in web/handlers.py
    • [ ] Add WS command dispatch in _enqueue_command() for tuner get/set
    • [ ] (Optional) Add REST endpoint /api/v1/tuner in web/server.py
    • [ ] Add unit tests for CLI subcommand
    • [ ] Add unit tests for WS command handling
    • [ ] Update docs if needed

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] icom-lan tuner shows current tuner status
    • [ ] icom-lan tuner tune initiates a tune cycle
    • [ ] icom-lan tuner on/off enables/disables ATU
    • [ ] icom-lan tuner --json outputs JSON
    • [ ] WebSocket get_tuner_status / set_tuner_status commands work
    • [ ] All existing tests still pass
    • [ ] New tests cover CLI and WS paths

    References¶

    • CI-V spec: command 0x1C, sub 0x01, data 0x00=off, 0x01=on, 0x02=tuning
    • Existing builders: src/icom_lan/commands.py lines 3408-3429
    • Existing radio methods: src/icom_lan/radio.py lines 2686-2702
    • CLI pattern: _cmd_dualwatch() in src/icom_lan/cli.py
    • WS pattern: get_dual_watch / set_dual_watch in src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py

    ✅ #156: Epic: Svelte 5 Web UI Rewrite (Desktop + Mobile)¶

    Labels: enhancement, ui

    Overview¶

    Complete rewrite of the web UI from monolithic index.html (2731 lines) to a Svelte 5 + TypeScript component architecture with responsive desktop and mobile layouts.

    Architecture document: docs/plans/2026-03-07-ui-architecture.md

    Key Principles¶

    • Server state = single source of truth (/api/v1/state)
    • Three state layers: server state, pending commands, UI state
    • Framework-agnostic Canvas renderers (spectrum, waterfall)
    • Svelte 5 components with TypeScript and scoped CSS
    • CSS design system with custom properties
    • Multi-radio ready (capabilities-driven UI)

    Existing Backend (already implemented)¶

    • ✅ /api/v1/capabilities — 15 capability tags, model, modes, filters
    • ✅ /api/v1/state — 124 fields, MAIN+SUB receivers
    • ✅ WebSocket — scope data, DX spots, notifications, commands
    • ✅ Radio Protocol with AudioCapable, ScopeCapable, DualReceiverCapable

    Sub-Issues by Sprint¶

    Sprint 0: Foundation (2-3 days, 2 agents parallel)¶

    • [ ] #157 — Svelte 5 + TypeScript + Vite scaffold
    • [ ] #158 — Backend: state revision + updatedAt + info endpoint

    Sprint 1: Transport + State (2-3 days, 2 agents parallel)¶

    • [ ] #159 — WebSocket + HTTP transport layer
    • [ ] #160 — Svelte state stores (radio, UI, commands, capabilities)

    Sprint 2: Core Rendering (3-4 days, 3 agents parallel)¶

    • [ ] #161 — Spectrum + Waterfall renderers (TypeScript port)
    • [ ] #162 — VFO display + meters components
    • [ ] #163 — Band/Mode/Filter selectors + feature toggles + audio

    Sprint 3: Desktop Layout (2-3 days, sequential)¶

    • [ ] #164 — Desktop layout integration + keyboard shortcuts

    Sprint 4: Mobile (2-3 days, 2 agents parallel)¶

    • [ ] #165 — Mobile layout + responsive components
    • [ ] #166 — Touch gestures (swipe-tune, tap-to-tune, hold-PTT, pinch-zoom)

    Sprint 5: PWA + Polish (2-3 days, 2 agents parallel)¶

    • [ ] #167 — PWA manifest + Service Worker + offline shell
    • [ ] #168 — UX polish (themes, settings, loading states, performance)

    Total: ~14-19 days¶

    Framework Decision¶

    Option Verdict
    Vanilla JS modules ❌ Short-term fix, does not scale
    Svelte 5 + TS ✅ Small runtime, compile-time, reactive, scoped CSS
    React ❌ Heavy runtime, overkill
    Preact ❌ Smaller ecosystem

    Tech Stack¶

    • Svelte 5 (runes: $state, $derived, $effect)
    • TypeScript (strict)
    • Vite (build + dev server)
    • pnpm (package manager)
    • vitest (unit tests)
    • CSS custom properties (design system)
    • Svelte MCP server (documentation + autofixer)

    ✅ #160: Sprint 1B: Svelte state stores (radio, UI, commands, capabilities)¶

    Labels: enhancement, ui

    Part of Epic #156 (Sprint 1, Agent B)

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] stores/radio.ts — Svelte 5 runes store for server state, revision tracking, stale detection
    • [ ] stores/ui.ts — layout, active panel, fullscreen, gestures
    • [ ] stores/commands.ts — pending command queue with timeout, reconciliation
    • [ ] stores/capabilities.ts — feature flags from /api/v1/capabilities
    • [ ] stores/connection.ts — connection health, last response time
    • [ ] stores/audio.ts — audio state, volume, mute
    • [ ] Integration with transport layer (subscribe to WS events, poll HTTP)
    • [ ] Unit tests for all stores (vitest)

    TypeScript interfaces¶

    interface ServerState { revision, updatedAt, active, ptt, split, main, sub, connection }
interface UiState { layout, activePanel, spectrumFullscreen, theme, gestures }
interface PendingCommand { id, type, payload, createdAt, status, timeoutMs }

    Depends on¶

    • 157 (scaffold)¶

    • 159 (transport layer)¶

    Parallelizable with¶

    • Sprint 1A (transport layer) — coordinate on types

    Effort: M (1-2 days)¶

    ✅ #161: Sprint 2A: Spectrum + Waterfall renderers (TypeScript port)¶

    Labels: enhancement, ui

    Part of Epic #156 (Sprint 2, Agent A)

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Port spectrum-renderer to TypeScript (lib/renderers/spectrum-renderer.ts)
    • [ ] Port waterfall-renderer to TypeScript (lib/renderers/waterfall-renderer.ts)
    • [ ] SpectrumCanvas.svelte — Svelte wrapper for spectrum renderer
    • [ ] WaterfallCanvas.svelte — Svelte wrapper for waterfall renderer
    • [ ] SpectrumPanel.svelte — container with controls (span, ref level, hold)
    • [ ] DxOverlay.svelte — DX cluster spots on waterfall
    • [ ] Responsive sizing (fill container, resize observer)
    • [ ] Fullscreen toggle
    • [ ] Tap-to-tune on waterfall (mobile + desktop)

    Architecture¶

    Renderers are framework-agnostic pure TS. They receive Canvas context + data, render. Svelte wrappers handle lifecycle (mount/destroy), resize, event delegation.

    Depends on¶

    ✅ #166: Sprint 4B: Touch gestures (swipe-tune, tap-to-tune, hold-PTT, pinch-zoom)¶

    Labels: enhancement, ui

    Part of Epic #156 (Sprint 4, Agent B)

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Swipe-to-tune on VFO display (horizontal gesture → freq change)
    • [ ] Configurable step size per swipe distance
    • [ ] Tap-to-tune on waterfall (touch → calculate freq → set)
    • [ ] Pinch-zoom on waterfall (change spectrum span)
    • [ ] Drag-to-pan on waterfall (shift center frequency)
    • [ ] Hold-to-talk PTT:
    • Touch start → TX pending
    • Server confirms → TX active (visual feedback)
    • Touch end → RX
    • Strong visual: red border, pulsing indicator
    • [ ] Pull-down controls drawer gesture
    • [ ] Haptic feedback (navigator.vibrate) on PTT, band switch, tune step

    Touch Event Handling¶

    Use pointer events (unified mouse + touch): - pointerdown, pointermove, pointerup - touch-action: none on gesture areas - Gesture recognizer: distinguish tap vs swipe vs pinch

    Parallelizable with¶

    ⬜ #169: epic: Frontend & API audit — 20 issues for universal, reliable, architecturally sound UI¶

    Labels: priority:P0, ui, area:web-ui

    Summary¶

    Full audit of the Svelte 5 frontend and backend API revealed 20 issues across 4 categories: critical bugs, architectural debt, resilience gaps, and missing features. This epic tracks fixing all of them to achieve a universal (multi-radio), reliable (resilient to disconnects/slowdowns), and architecturally sound web interface.

    Audit Scope¶

    • ~8,300 lines frontend (60 files: Svelte components, stores, transport, renderers, audio)
    • ~1,200 lines backend API (server.py, handlers.py)
    • Live testing on desktop + iOS Safari

    Issue Map¶

    🔴 Critical Bugs (broken functionality)¶

    • [ ] #170 — set_squelch command name mismatch → squelch never works
    • [ ] #171 — set_att parameter mismatch (level vs db) → KeyError
    • [ ] #172 — Waterfall double-rendering (duplicate scope subscriptions)
    • [ ] #173 — Command responses not handled → silent failures
    • [ ] #174 — BottomBar audioManager.onChange listener leak

    🟡 Architecture¶

    • [ ] #175 — Replace triple-polling with WS state push
    • [ ] #176 — Reduce WebSocket connections (iOS 6-conn limit)
    • [ ] #177 — Remove Connection: close, add HTTP keepalive
    • [ ] #178 — Eliminate hardcoded IC-7610 — capabilities-driven UI
    • [ ] #1700 — Fix optimistic update frequency snap mismatch

    🟡 Resilience¶

    • [ ] #1701 — XHR polling: add error backoff + stale indicator
    • [ ] #1702 — PTT button timeout
    • [ ] #1703 — Stale state detection
    • [ ] #1704 — Coordinate Audio/Control WS state
    • [ ] #1705 — App.svelte: limit reload retries

    🔵 Improvements¶

    • [ ] #1706 — Set Vite build.target for Safari 14+
    • [ ] #1707 — Pause RAF loop when tab hidden (Page Visibility)
    • [ ] #1708 — Wire up missing UI panels (Settings, Antenna, Tuner, Split/RIT, AGC)
    • [ ] #1709 — Add basic LAN authentication (token/PIN)
    • [ ] #1710 — State delta / ETag 304 instead of full JSON snapshot

    Dependency Graph¶

    graph TD
    B1[SUB1: set_squelch name] --> |independent| DONE
    B2[SUB2: set_att params] --> |independent| DONE
    B3[SUB3: waterfall double] --> |independent| DONE
    B4[SUB4: command responses] --> B11[SUB11: backoff]
    B5[SUB5: onChange leak] --> |independent| DONE
    A6[SUB6: WS state push] --> A8[SUB8: drop Connection:close]
    A6 --> A20[SUB20: state delta]
    A7[SUB7: reduce WS conns] --> |after SUB6| A6
    A9[SUB9: capabilities UI] --> |independent| DONE
    A10[SUB10: freq snap] --> |independent| DONE
    R11[SUB11: XHR backoff] --> R13[SUB13: stale state]
    R12[SUB12: PTT timeout] --> |independent| DONE
    R14[SUB14: WS coordination] --> |after SUB6| A6
    R15[SUB15: reload limit] --> |independent| DONE
    I16[SUB16: build target] --> |independent| DONE
    I17[SUB17: RAF visibility] --> |independent| DONE
    I18[SUB18: missing UI] --> A9
    I19[SUB19: auth] --> |independent| DONE

    Key Files¶

    File Role
    frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts WS connection, command sending, optimistic updates
    frontend/src/lib/transport/http-client.ts HTTP polling, capabilities fetch
    frontend/src/lib/stores/radio.svelte.ts Reactive state store, optimistic Maps
    frontend/src/lib/stores/connection.svelte.ts Connection health state
    frontend/src/lib/audio/audio-manager.ts Audio WS lifecycle, RX/TX
    frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte Scope WS, passband overlay
    frontend/src/components/spectrum/WaterfallCanvas.svelte Waterfall renderer
    frontend/src/components/shared/BottomBar.svelte PTT, volume, RX audio
    frontend/src/App.svelte Init, polling, error handling
    frontend/index.html XHR pre-polling, SW cleanup
    frontend/vite.config.ts Build config
    src/icom_lan/web/server.py HTTP server, WS routing, state endpoint
    src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py WS command handlers, control channel

    Non-goals¶

    • Full rewrite of backend server (incremental improvements only)
    • New radio backends (tracked in #107, #101)
    • WebRTC audio (tracked in #104)

    ✅ #170: bug: set_squelch command name mismatch — squelch never works¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:web-ui, area:api

    Problem¶

    Frontend sends set_squelch command but backend only accepts set_sql. Squelch control silently fails — the backend returns unknown_command error but frontend doesn't display it (see #SUB4).

    Root Cause¶

    Command name mismatch between frontend and backend:

    Frontend (ws-client.ts:225, _applyOptimistic): 

    case 'set_squelch':
  if (typeof params.level === 'number') patchActiveReceiver({ squelch: params.level });

    Backend (handlers.py:145, _COMMANDS): 

    "set_sql",  # not "set_squelch"

    Backend handler (handlers.py:617): 

    case "set_sql":
    level = int(params["level"])
    rx = int(params.get("receiver", 0))
    q.put(SetSquelch(level, receiver=rx))

    Fix¶

    Option A (preferred): Add set_squelch as alias in backend _COMMANDS and handler. This is more intuitive and matches the pattern of other commands (set_nb, set_nr, not set_n_b).

    Option B: Rename frontend to set_sql. Requires updating ws-client.ts and any component that calls sendCommand('set_squelch', ...).

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — add "set_squelch" to _COMMANDS, add case "set_squelch": (or alias to set_sql)
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts:225 — update _applyOptimistic case if Option B

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Send set_squelch command from UI — verify radio squelch changes
    • [ ] Verify _applyOptimistic case name matches sent command name
    • [ ] Backend unit test: set_squelch accepted and dispatched

    ✅ #171: bug: set_att parameter mismatch (level vs db) → KeyError¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:api

    Problem¶

    Frontend sends set_att with parameter { level: N } but backend expects { db: N }. Results in KeyError when handler tries int(params["db"]).

    Root Cause¶

    Frontend (ws-client.ts:231, _applyOptimistic): 

    case 'set_att':
  if (typeof params.level === 'number') patchActiveReceiver({ att: params.level });

    Frontend FeatureToggles.svelte currently shows ATT as display-only (no button sends set_att), but the optimistic mapping exists and any future UI or external caller would hit this bug.

    Backend (handlers.py:620): 

    case "set_att":
    db = int(params["db"])  # ← expects "db", not "level"

    Fix¶

    Standardize on level (matches set_af_level, set_rf_gain, set_preamp pattern):

    1. Backend: change params["db"] → params.get("level", params.get("db", 0)) (backward-compat)
    2. Or frontend: change optimistic case to use params.db

    Preferred: Backend accepts both, prefers level.

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py:620 — case "set_att": parameter name
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts:231 — _applyOptimistic set_att case

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Send set_att with { level: 20 } — no error
    • [ ] Send set_att with { db: 20 } — backward compat
    • [ ] Optimistic update shows correct ATT value immediately

    ✅ #172: bug: Waterfall double-rendering from duplicate scope subscriptions¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Waterfall scrolls at 2x normal speed because each scope frame is rendered twice.

    Root Cause¶

    WaterfallCanvas.svelte receives scope data via TWO independent paths simultaneously:

    Path 1 — Direct WS subscription (WaterfallCanvas.svelte:54-62): 

    onMount(() => {
  const scopeCh = getChannel('scope');
  const unsubBinary = scopeCh.onBinary((buf: ArrayBuffer) => {
    // ...parses frame...
    renderer.pushRow(new Uint8Array(buf, 16, pxCount));  // ← renders row
  });

    Path 2 — Parent callback (SpectrumPanel.svelte:113): 

    waterfallPush?.(frame.pixels);  // calls directPush() → renderer.pushRow()

    Both paths call renderer.pushRow() for every frame → each row rendered twice → 2x scroll speed.

    Similarly, SpectrumCanvas.svelte has a direct WS subscription (onMount line 43) AND receives data prop from SpectrumPanel. The RAF loop checks latestPixels ?? data so it prefers direct WS (no double render), but it's wasteful to maintain both paths.

    Fix¶

    Remove direct WS subscriptions from child components. The parent SpectrumPanel.svelte already subscribes and distributes: - Spectrum: via scopePixels prop (Uint8Array change tracking not reliable for RAF, so keep latestPixels in SpectrumCanvas but feed it from parent callback, not direct WS) - Waterfall: via directPush callback

    Clean architecture: 

                        SpectrumPanel (WS subscriber)
                    ┌─────────┬──────────────┐
                    │ scopePixels (prop)      │ directPush (callback)
                    ▼                         ▼
              SpectrumCanvas            WaterfallCanvas
              (RAF loop)               (pushRow)

    Remove from WaterfallCanvas.svelte: - Lines 54-62: scopeCh.onBinary(...) subscription - Line 52: const scopeCh = getChannel('scope') (if no longer needed)

    Remove from SpectrumCanvas.svelte: - Lines 43-52: scopeCh.onBinary(...) subscription - Instead: add onRegisterPush prop (like WaterfallCanvas) or keep using data prop with direct update

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/WaterfallCanvas.svelte — remove direct WS sub
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumCanvas.svelte — remove direct WS sub, use parent callback
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — add spectrum push callback

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Waterfall scrolls at correct speed (1 row per scope frame, not 2)
    • [ ] Spectrum still renders smoothly at scope frame rate
    • [ ] No data loss when SpectrumPanel re-mounts

    ✅ #173: feat: Handle command responses — show errors via Toast¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Backend sends structured response messages for every command ({ type: "response", id: "...", ok: true/false, error: "..." }), but the frontend ignores them entirely. Users never see errors like "unknown command", "no radio connected", "capability missing", etc.

    Root Cause¶

    1. WsIncoming type is incomplete — protocol.ts:15: 

      export type WsIncoming =
  | { type: 'dx_spot'; ... }
  | { type: 'dx_spots'; ... }
  | { type: 'notification'; ... }
  | { type: 'ack'; ... }
  | { type: 'error'; ... };
      Missing types: response, hello, state, event.

    2. No response handler — ws-client.ts onMessage handlers only check for dx_spot, dx_spots, notification. The response type is never matched.

    3. Backend response format (handlers.py:488-507): 

      # Success:
{"type": "response", "id": cmd_id, "ok": True, "result": {...}}
# Failure:
{"type": "response", "id": cmd_id, "ok": False, "error": "command_failed", "message": str(exc)}

    Fix¶

    1. Add missing types to WsIncoming¶

    export type WsIncoming =
  | { type: 'response'; id: string; ok: boolean; result?: Record<string, unknown>; error?: string; message?: string }
  | { type: 'hello'; proto: number; server: string; version: string; radio: string; connected: boolean; capabilities: string[] }
  | { type: 'state'; data: Record<string, unknown> }
  | { type: 'event'; name: string; data: Record<string, unknown> }
  | { type: 'dx_spot'; spot: DxSpot }
  | { type: 'dx_spots'; spots: DxSpot[] }
  | { type: 'notification'; level: string; message: string }
  ;

    2. Handle responses in ws-client.ts¶

    • Track pending commands by id with callbacks
    • On ok: false — show toast notification with error message
    • On ok: true — optionally confirm optimistic update
    • Add per-command timeout (e.g. 10s)

    3. Show errors via Toast¶

    // On command failure:
showToast({ level: 'error', message: `Command failed: ${response.message}` });

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/lib/types/protocol.ts — extend WsIncoming
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — add response handler, pending command tracking
    • frontend/src/components/shared/Toast.svelte — verify toast API supports programmatic show

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Send command with radio disconnected → toast shows "no radio connected"
    • [ ] Send unknown command → toast shows error
    • [ ] Send valid command → no error toast
    • [ ] Pending command timeout → toast shows "command timed out"

    ✅ #174: bug: BottomBar audioManager.onChange listener leak on unmount¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    BottomBar.svelte registers an audioManager.onChange() listener in onMount but never unsubscribes on component destroy.

    Root Cause¶

    BottomBar.svelte:8-10: 

    onMount(() => {
  audioManager.onChange(() => { audioTick++; });
  // ← no return of cleanup function
});

    audioManager.onChange() returns an unsubscribe function (via Set.delete), but BottomBar doesn't store or call it.

    When MobileLayout/DesktopLayout re-mounts (e.g. resize crossing 768px breakpoint, or hot-reload), old listeners accumulate in the Set<() => void>. Each re-mount adds another listener, causing audioTick++ to fire N times per audio state change (N = number of mounts).

    Fix¶

    onMount(() => {
  const unsub = audioManager.onChange(() => { audioTick++; });
  return () => unsub();
});

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/shared/BottomBar.svelte:8-10 — add cleanup return

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Resize window across 768px breakpoint several times
    • [ ] Verify audioTick increments by 1 (not N) per state change
    • [ ] No console warnings about leaked listeners

    ✅ #175: refactor: Replace triple-polling with WS state push¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:web-ui, area:api

    Summary¶

    Replace the triple-layer polling architecture with WebSocket-pushed state updates. Current system is a workaround for an iOS Safari bug (now fixed).

    Current Architecture (broken telephone)¶

    index.html <script>                    Svelte App
┌─────────────────────┐               ┌─────────────────────┐
│ XHR poll /state     │               │ setInterval(150ms)  │
│ every 200ms         │──────────────►│ reads window global │
│ writes to           │  window.      │ calls setRadioState │
│ window.__RADIO_     │  __RADIO_     │                     │
│ STATE__             │  STATE__      │                     │
└─────────────────────┘               └─────────────────────┘

    Problems: - 3 layers of indirection (XHR → global → setInterval → store) - ~350ms latency floor (200ms poll + 150ms read interval) - 5 TCP connections/second (Connection: close) - No error handling in XHR - No stale detection - Was a workaround for iOS Safari bug (#156) that's now fixed

    Target Architecture¶

                    Backend                          Frontend
┌──────────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────────────┐
│ WebServer            │          │ WsChannel (control)      │
│                      │  WS push │                          │
│ on state change:     ├─────────►│ onMessage(state_update)  │
│   broadcast to all   │          │   → setRadioState()      │
│   control WS clients │          │                          │
│                      │          │ HTTP GET /state (fallback)│
│ RadioPoller bumps    │          │   on reconnect only      │
│ revision on change   │          │                          │
└──────────────────────┘          └──────────────────────────┘

    Implementation Plan¶

    Backend changes (server.py, handlers.py)¶

    1. On _on_radio_state_change() callback — after bump_revision(), serialize current state and broadcast to all control WS clients that subscribed to "state" stream
    2. Throttle broadcasts to max 1 per 50ms (batch rapid CI-V updates)
    3. Add "state_update" message type with full or delta state JSON

    Frontend changes¶

    1. ws-client.ts: Add handler for type: "state_update" → call setRadioState()
    2. App.svelte: Remove window.__RADIO_STATE__ reading setInterval
    3. index.html: Remove XHR polling setInterval. Keep ONE initial fetch for pre-Svelte state (before WS connects)
    4. http-client.ts: Keep fetchState() for fallback/reconnect, remove startPolling()

    Fallback¶

    On WS disconnect → start temporary HTTP polling (existing startPolling() from http-client.ts). On WS reconnect → stop polling, fetch full state once.

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — _event_sender_loop → add state broadcasting
    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — throttled state broadcast on _on_radio_state_change
    • frontend/index.html — remove XHR polling
    • frontend/src/App.svelte — remove startStatePoller(), add WS state handler
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — handle state_update messages
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/http-client.ts — keep fetchState(), clean up startPolling()

    Dependencies¶

    • Should be done before #SUB7 (WS multiplexing) and #SUB8 (Connection: close)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] State updates arrive via WS within 100ms of radio change
    • [ ] No XHR polling in index.html
    • [ ] No setInterval state reader in App.svelte
    • [ ] HTTP fallback activates on WS disconnect
    • [ ] iOS Safari works (WS state push)

    ✅ #176: refactor: Reduce WebSocket connections (iOS 6-conn limit)¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2, area:web-ui, area:api

    Summary¶

    Reduce WebSocket connections from 3+ to 2 (or ideally 1) to stay within iOS Safari's 6-connection-per-origin limit and reduce resource usage.

    Current State¶

    Channel Path Data Rate
    Control /api/v1/ws JSON commands/responses, events, DX spots Low
    Scope /api/v1/scope Binary scope frames (480 bytes × 15-30 fps) High
    Audio /api/v1/audio Binary Opus/PCM16 frames (20ms × 50/sec) High
    HTTP /api/v1/state State polling (currently 5/sec) Medium

    Total: 3 WS + HTTP = 4 connections minimum

    iOS Safari limit: 6 per origin. Two tabs or another client → conflict.

    Proposed Architecture¶

    Option A: Merge Control + Scope (recommended)¶

    Frontend                           Backend
┌────────────┐                    ┌────────────┐
│ Main WS    │◄──── JSON + ─────►│ /api/v1/ws │
│            │      Binary scope  │ (merged)   │
├────────────┤                    ├────────────┤
│ Audio WS   │◄──── Binary ─────►│ /api/v1/   │
│            │      audio         │ audio      │
└────────────┘                    └────────────┘
    • Scope binary frames get a 1-byte type prefix to distinguish from text
    • Audio stays separate (different latency requirements, independent lifecycle)
    • Reduces to 2 WS connections

    Option B: Single multiplexed WS¶

    All data on one WS with type-length-value framing. More complex but most efficient.

    Implementation¶

    Backend¶

    1. ControlHandler gains scope frame broadcasting (currently ScopeHandler)
    2. When client subscribes to "scope" stream, ControlHandler registers as scope handler
    3. Binary frames sent on same WS connection (WS supports text + binary interleaved)
    4. Keep /api/v1/scope for backward compatibility, deprecate

    Frontend¶

    1. SpectrumPanel.svelte: Subscribe to scope via control WS onBinary instead of getChannel('scope')
    2. ws-client.ts: WsChannel.onBinary already works for binary frames — just need to route subscription
    3. Remove getChannel('scope') calls from spectrum components

    Dependencies¶

    • After #SUB6 (WS state push) — control WS becomes the single data channel

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — merge scope into ControlHandler
    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — route scope subscription through control WS
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — use control WS for scope
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — remove separate scope channel

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Only 2 WebSocket connections when audio active, 1 when audio off
    • [ ] Scope data renders correctly on merged WS
    • [ ] iOS Safari: two tabs work simultaneously
    • [ ] Backward compat: /api/v1/scope still works (deprecated)

    ✅ #177: refactor: HTTP keepalive, gzip, ETag support in server¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2, area:api

    Problem¶

    Every HTTP response includes Connection: close, forcing a new TCP connection per request. At 200ms polling = 5 new TCP connections/second per client.

    Root Cause¶

    server.py:1160: 

    headers = {
    "Content-Length": str(len(body)),
    "Connection": "close",  # ← kills TCP keepalive
    **extra_headers,
}

    The custom asyncio HTTP server is single-request-per-connection. After sending the response, _handle_connection closes the writer.

    Fix¶

    After #SUB6 (WS state push), HTTP polling will be minimal (reconnect/fallback only). But the HTTP server should still support keepalive for efficiency:

    1. Remove Connection: close header
    2. In _handle_connection: loop to handle multiple requests per TCP connection (HTTP/1.1 keepalive)
    3. Add idle timeout (30s) to close stale keepalive connections
    4. Add Content-Encoding: gzip for JSON responses >1KB (state is ~1800 bytes)
    5. Add ETag based on revision for state endpoint → 304 Not Modified

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — _send_response(), _handle_connection(), _serve_state()

    Dependencies¶

    • After #SUB6 (less critical once polling is eliminated)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Multiple HTTP requests work on same TCP connection
    • [ ] State endpoint supports ETag/If-None-Match → 304
    • [ ] Idle connections auto-close after 30s
    • [ ] JSON responses >1KB are gzip-compressed

    ✅ #178: refactor: Capabilities-driven UI — eliminate hardcoded IC-7610¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:web-ui, area:api

    Summary¶

    Eliminate hardcoded IC-7610 specifics from the frontend. All radio-specific data should come from the /api/v1/capabilities endpoint, making the UI work with any backend (Yaesu, Kenwood, future radios).

    Current Hardcoded IC-7610 References¶

    File What's hardcoded Should come from
    BandSelector.svelte 11 HF+6m bands with IC-7610 ranges capabilities.freqRanges
    filter-width.ts Factory default filter widths for IC-7610 capabilities.filterWidths (new) or remove
    spectrum-renderer.ts Amplitude ceiling=80, sqrt curve capabilities.scopeAmplitudeMax or auto-detect
    SpectrumPanel.svelte tunePct = 50 (center mode always) capabilities.scopeMode or detect from data
    constants.ts 48kHz mono Opus/PCM16 capabilities.audioFormat (new)
    server.py _MODES = ["USB","LSB","CW","AM","FM","RTTY","CWR"] From radio profile
    server.py _FILTERS = ["FIL1","FIL2","FIL3"] From radio profile
    server.py freqRanges: [30kHz-60MHz, 50-54MHz] From radio profile
    server.py _RADIO_MODEL = "IC-7610" Already dynamic via radio.model

    Proposed Capabilities Extension¶

    {
  "model": "IC-7610",
  "scope": true,
  "audio": true,
  "tx": true,
  "capabilities": ["dual_rx", "scope", "audio", "nb", "nr", ...],
  "freqRanges": [
    {"start": 30000, "end": 60000000, "label": "HF", "bands": [
      {"name": "160m", "start": 1800000, "end": 2000000, "default": 1825000},
      {"name": "80m", "start": 3500000, "end": 4000000, "default": 3700000}
    ]}
  ],
  "modes": ["USB", "LSB", "CW", "CW-R", "AM", "FM", "RTTY", "RTTY-R"],
  "filters": ["FIL1", "FIL2", "FIL3"],
  "scopeConfig": {
    "centerMode": true,
    "amplitudeMax": 160,
    "defaultSpan": 500000
  },
  "audioConfig": {
    "sampleRate": 48000,
    "channels": 1,
    "codecs": ["opus", "pcm16"]
  }
}

    Implementation¶

    Backend¶

    1. Move _MODES, _FILTERS, freqRanges to RadioProfile / capability provider
    2. Enhance _serve_capabilities() to include band plan, scope config, audio config
    3. Each radio backend provides its own capability dict

    Frontend¶

    1. BandSelector.svelte — read bands from capabilities.freqRanges[].bands
    2. SpectrumPanel.svelte — detect center vs fixed mode from scope data (startFreq/endFreq vs VFO)
    3. constants.ts — make audio params configurable from capabilities
    4. filter-width.ts — fallback table, prefer server-provided widths
    5. spectrum-renderer.ts — auto-detect amplitude range from data histogram

    Dependencies¶

    • SUB18 (missing UI) should use capabilities-driven rendering¶

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — _serve_capabilities()
    • src/icom_lan/profiles.py — add band/scope/audio config to RadioProfile
    • frontend/src/lib/types/capabilities.ts — extend interface
    • frontend/src/components/controls/BandSelector.svelte
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte
    • frontend/src/lib/audio/constants.ts
    • frontend/src/lib/utils/filter-width.ts
    • frontend/src/lib/renderers/spectrum-renderer.ts

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] No hardcoded band/mode/filter lists in frontend code
    • [ ] BandSelector renders bands from capabilities API
    • [ ] ModeSelector renders modes from capabilities API
    • [ ] Capabilities endpoint includes all radio-specific config
    • [ ] Works with IC-7610 profile (regression test)

    ✅ #179: bug: Optimistic update frequency snap mismatch — patch stuck 5s¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Optimistic frequency patch never clears if the radio snaps to a slightly different frequency.

    Root Cause¶

    radio.svelte.ts (applyOptimistic): 

    if (serverVal === entry.value || now >= entry.expires) {
  map.delete(field);
  continue;
}

    Strict equality (===) means: - User sends set_freq(14074000) → optimistic patch: freqHz: 14074000 - Radio applies step rounding → reports 14074100 - 14074100 !== 14074000 → patch stays until 5s TTL expires - During those 5 seconds, VFO shows wrong frequency (14.074.000 instead of 14.074.100)

    Same issue for any command where the radio adjusts the value (mode-dependent filter auto-select, etc.).

    Fix¶

    For frequency: Use tolerance-based comparison: 

    if (field === 'freqHz') {
  const diff = Math.abs((serverVal as number) - (entry.value as number));
  if (diff < 200) {  // within 200 Hz = confirmed
    map.delete(field);
    continue;
  }
}

    For other fields: Consider "any server update after optimistic = confirmed" pattern: 

    // Track the server value AT TIME of optimistic patch
if (serverVal !== entry.serverValueAtPatch) {
  // Server has changed — accept new server value
  map.delete(field);
  continue;
}

    This handles: user sends NB=ON, server confirms NB=ON (match). But also: user sends mode=CW, server confirms mode=CW but filter auto-changes from FIL1→FIL2. The filter optimistic (if any) should clear because server changed it.

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/lib/stores/radio.svelte.ts — applyOptimistic() function

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Tune to frequency with step rounding → VFO shows radio's actual frequency within 1s
    • [ ] Rapid tuning (scroll wheel) → no stuck patches
    • [ ] Mode change with auto-filter → filter shows correct value

    ✅ #180: feat: XHR polling error backoff + stale indicator¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    index.html XHR polling runs every 200ms with no error handling. When server is down: 5 failed requests/second, no backoff, no user indicator.

    Root Cause¶

    index.html: 

    function pollState() {
  var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
  xhr.open('GET', '/api/v1/state', true);
  xhr.timeout = 3000;
  xhr.onload = function() { /* parse JSON */ };
  // No onerror handler
  // No error counter
  xhr.send();
}
setInterval(pollState, 200);  // relentless

    Fix¶

    After #SUB6 (WS state push), this XHR poll will be removed from index.html. But until then, or if kept as fallback:

    1. Add xhr.onerror / xhr.ontimeout handlers
    2. Track consecutive errors
    3. Exponential backoff: 200ms → 400ms → 800ms → ... → 10s max
    4. After 3+ errors → set window.__RADIO_OFFLINE__ = true (Svelte reads for indicator)
    5. On success → reset backoff, clear offline flag

    Files¶

    • frontend/index.html — pollState() function
    • frontend/src/App.svelte or OfflineIndicator.svelte — read offline flag

    Dependencies¶

    • Will be partially superseded by #SUB6 (WS state push)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Server down → polling backs off to 10s max
    • [ ] UI shows offline indicator after 3 consecutive errors
    • [ ] Server recovers → polling resumes at 200ms, offline indicator clears

    ✅ #181: bug: PTT button stuck in pending state without timeout¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    PTT button enters pending state and waits indefinitely for server confirmation. If the server never responds, the button stays in "…" forever.

    Root Cause¶

    BottomBar.svelte: 

    let pending = $state(false);
let pendingValue = $state(false);

$effect(() => {
  if (pending && transmitting === pendingValue) {
    pending = false;  // only clears when server state matches
  }
});

function onPtt() {
  if (pending) return;
  pending = true;
  pendingValue = next;
  sendCommand('ptt', { state: next });
  // ← no timeout
}

    Fix¶

    Add a 5-second timeout: 

    function onPtt() {
  if (pending) return;
  const next = !transmitting;
  pending = true;
  pendingValue = next;
  sendCommand('ptt', { state: next });

  setTimeout(() => {
    if (pending) {
      pending = false;
      // Optionally show toast: "PTT command timed out"
    }
  }, 5000);
}

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/shared/BottomBar.svelte — onPtt() function

    Tests¶

    • [ ] PTT with connected radio → works normally, pending clears on confirmation
    • [ ] PTT with disconnected radio → pending clears after 5s, button returns to normal
    • [ ] Rapid PTT clicks during pending → ignored (existing behavior, keep it)

    ✅ #182: feat: Stale state detection — visual indicator when data is old¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    When HTTP/WS connections fail, the last received state stays in the UI indefinitely. No indication that the displayed data is stale.

    Root Cause¶

    radio.svelte.ts stores radio.current but has no staleness tracking. connection.svelte.ts tracks httpConnected and wsConnected but these only update on active events (successful fetch or WS state change) — not on "no data for N seconds".

    Fix¶

    1. Track last successful state update timestamp¶

    // radio.svelte.ts
let lastUpdateTime = $state(0);

export function setRadioState(state: ServerState): void {
  // ... existing logic ...
  lastUpdateTime = Date.now();
}

export function getLastUpdateTime(): number {
  return lastUpdateTime;
}

    2. Stale detection in connection store¶

    // connection.svelte.ts
const STALE_THRESHOLD_MS = 3000; // 3 seconds without update = stale

$effect(() => {
  const timer = setInterval(() => {
    const age = Date.now() - getLastUpdateTime();
    staleState = age > STALE_THRESHOLD_MS && getLastUpdateTime() > 0;
  }, 1000);
  return () => clearInterval(timer);
});

    3. Visual indicator¶

    • StatusBar dot: green → yellow (stale) → red (disconnected)
    • VFO display: dim opacity when stale
    • Optional banner: "Data stale — last update Xs ago"

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/lib/stores/radio.svelte.ts — add lastUpdateTime
    • frontend/src/lib/stores/connection.svelte.ts — add stale detection
    • frontend/src/components/shared/StatusBar.svelte — stale indicator
    • frontend/src/components/layout/MobileLayout.svelte — stale indicator

    Dependencies¶

    • After #SUB11 (XHR backoff) — error counting feeds into stale detection

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] UI shows "stale" indicator after 3s without state update
    • [ ] VFO dims when stale
    • [ ] Indicator clears immediately when fresh data arrives

    ✅ #183: feat: Coordinate Audio/Control WS connection states¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Audio WS and Control WS have independent lifecycles. If one is down and the other is up, the UI can show contradictory state (e.g., "disconnected" overlay while audio plays).

    Root Cause¶

    • connection.svelte.ts tracks httpConnected + wsConnected (control WS only)
    • audioManager.wsConnected is separate, not feeding into connection store
    • BottomBar.svelte reads audioManager.wsConnected independently

    Fix¶

    1. Unified connection status¶

    // connection.svelte.ts
type ChannelStatus = {
  control: boolean;
  audio: boolean;
  http: boolean;
};

    2. AudioManager reports to connection store¶

    // audio-manager.ts — on WS connect/disconnect:
setAudioConnected(ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN);

    3. Coordinated behavior¶

    • If control WS disconnects → pause audio (server likely down)
    • If audio WS disconnects but control WS up → show "Audio disconnected" (not full disconnect)
    • On control WS reconnect → re-subscribe audio if it was enabled

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/lib/stores/connection.svelte.ts — add audio status
    • frontend/src/lib/audio/audio-manager.ts — report to connection store
    • frontend/src/components/shared/StatusBar.svelte — show per-channel status
    • frontend/src/components/shared/BottomBar.svelte — coordinate with control WS state

    Dependencies¶

    • After #SUB6 (WS state push) — connection architecture may change

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Audio auto-pauses when control WS disconnects
    • [ ] Audio reconnects when control WS recovers
    • [ ] StatusBar shows correct per-channel status
    • [ ] No contradictory UI states

    ✅ #184: bug: App.svelte infinite reload loop when backend is down¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    App.svelte reloads the page every 5 seconds when fetchCapabilities() fails. If the backend is permanently down, this creates an infinite reload loop.

    Root Cause¶

    App.svelte: 

    (async () => {
  try {
    const caps = await fetchCapabilities();
    // ...
  } catch (err) {
    backendError = `Backend error: ${err}`;
    retrying = true;
    retryTimer = setTimeout(() => location.reload(), 5000);  // ← infinite
  }
})();

    No retry counter, no max retries, no exponential backoff.

    Fix¶

    const MAX_RETRIES = 5;
const RETRY_DELAYS = [3000, 5000, 10000, 20000, 30000];
let retryCount = 0;

// On error:
if (retryCount < MAX_RETRIES) {
  retryTimer = setTimeout(() => location.reload(), RETRY_DELAYS[retryCount]);
  retryCount++;
} else {
  backendError = 'Server unreachable. Please check the connection and reload manually.';
  retrying = false;
}

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/App.svelte — error handling in async init

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Backend down → retries 5 times with increasing delay
    • [ ] After 5 retries → shows manual reload message, stops retrying
    • [ ] Backend recovers during retries → loads normally

    ✅ #185: chore: Set Vite build.target for Safari 14+ compatibility¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    No explicit build.target in vite.config.ts. Esbuild defaults to esnext, producing code with features unsupported by older browsers (private class fields #, ??=, structuredClone, etc.).

    Root Cause¶

    vite.config.ts: 

    export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [svelte()],
  build: {
    outDir: 'dist',
    // No target specified → esnext
  },
});

    Private class fields (#e, #o) in Svelte 5 compiled output require Safari 14.1+ / iOS 14.5+. ??= (logical nullish assignment) requires Safari 15+ / iOS 15+.

    Fix¶

    build: {
  outDir: 'dist',
  target: ['es2020', 'safari14'],
},

    es2020 covers: optional chaining, nullish coalescing, BigInt, dynamic import, globalThis. safari14 ensures compatibility with iOS 14 (released 2020).

    Private class fields are es2022 — Vite/esbuild will downlevel them for the safari14 target.

    Files¶

    • frontend/vite.config.ts — add build.target

    Tests¶

    • [ ] pnpm build succeeds
    • [ ] Bundle does NOT contain # private fields (grep)
    • [ ] Bundle does NOT contain ??= (grep)
    • [ ] iOS Safari 14 loads the app (if testable)

    ✅ #186: perf: Pause RAF loop when tab hidden (Page Visibility API)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    SpectrumCanvas.svelte runs a continuous requestAnimationFrame loop even when the tab is hidden or the spectrum is not visible. Wastes CPU/battery, especially on mobile.

    Root Cause¶

    SpectrumCanvas.svelte: 

    function draw(): void {
  // ... render ...
  rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);  // always schedules next frame
}

onMount(() => {
  rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);  // starts loop
  // ...
});

    Browsers throttle RAF to ~1fps when hidden, but the loop still runs. Additionally, scope WS binary data continues arriving and being parsed (in onBinary handler) even when invisible.

    Fix¶

    1. Page Visibility API¶

    function draw(): void {
  if (document.hidden) {
    // Wait for visibility change instead of burning RAF cycles
    const onVisible = () => {
      document.removeEventListener('visibilitychange', onVisible);
      rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
    };
    document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', onVisible);
    return;
  }
  // ... render ...
  rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
}

    2. IntersectionObserver for component visibility¶

    const io = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  isVisible = entries[0].isIntersecting;
});
io.observe(canvas);
// In draw(): if (!isVisible) skip render

    3. Scope WS: unsubscribe when invisible¶

    When spectrum panel is hidden (mobile controls overlay, fullscreen toggled off), disconnect from scope WS to save bandwidth.

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumCanvas.svelte — visibility check in RAF loop
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/WaterfallCanvas.svelte — same
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — disconnect scope WS when hidden

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Switch to another tab → RAF loop pauses, CPU usage drops
    • [ ] Switch back → rendering resumes immediately
    • [ ] Scroll spectrum offscreen → rendering pauses
    • [ ] Mobile: close spectrum overlay → scope WS disconnects

    ✅ #187: feat: Wire up missing UI panels (Settings, Antenna, Tuner, Split, etc.)¶

    Several backend commands and state fields have no corresponding UI controls. The frontend has placeholder components (e.g., SettingsPanel.svelte) that aren't wired up.

    Missing UI vs Available Backend¶

    Feature Backend Command State Field UI Status

    ATT control set_att rx.att Display-only badge in FeatureToggles
    Preamp control set_preamp rx.preamp Display-only badge in FeatureToggles
    AGC control — (no backend yet) rx.agc Display-only badge in FeatureToggles
    COMP control — (no backend yet) compressorOn Display-only badge in FeatureToggles
    Antenna selector set_antenna_1/2, set_rx_antenna_ant1/2 — None
    Tuner control set_tuner_status, get_tuner_status tunerStatus None
    Split mode — split None
    RIT/XIT — ritFreq, ritOn, ritTx None
    Power control set_power powerLevel None (except PowerMeter display)
    Mic gain — micGain None
    CW keyer — cwPitch, keySpeed None
    Dial lock — dialLock None
    VFO Swap vfo_swap — None
    VFO Equalize vfo_equalize — None
    Scope receiver switch switch_scope_receiver scopeControls.receiver None
    Drive gain — driveGain None
    Monitor — monitorOn, monitorGain None
    VOX — voxOn, voxGain, antiVoxGain None
    Power on/off set_powerstat — None

    Priority¶

    P1 — Common operating controls¶

    • ATT/PRE as toggle buttons (not just display)
    • Antenna selector
    • Tuner control
    • Power control slider
    • VFO Swap/Equalize buttons

    P2 — Settings panel¶

    • Wire up SettingsPanel.svelte in both layouts
    • AGC, COMP toggles
    • Mic gain, drive gain sliders
    • CW keyer settings

    P3 — Advanced¶

    • Split mode
    • RIT/XIT
    • VOX/Monitor
    • Power on/off

    Dependencies¶

    • SUB9 (capabilities-driven UI) — new controls should be capability-gated¶

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/controls/FeatureToggles.svelte — add ATT/PRE buttons
    • frontend/src/components/settings/SettingsPanel.svelte — populate
    • frontend/src/components/layout/DesktopLayout.svelte — add SettingsPanel
    • frontend/src/components/layout/MobileLayout.svelte — add SettingsPanel access
    • New components as needed

    • [ ] Antenna selector visible when capability present
    • [ ] Tuner on/off/tune button
    • [ ] Power level slider
    • [ ] VFO Swap and Equalize buttons in receiver controls

    ✅ #188: feat: Add basic LAN authentication (bearer token / PIN)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:api

    Summary¶

    Currently anyone on the LAN can control the radio, including PTT (transmit). No authentication at all.

    Risk¶

    • Accidental TX by someone browsing to the URL
    • Malicious frequency/mode changes
    • Power on/off
    • IC-7610 LAN supports only 2 concurrent connections — unauthorized clients consume slots

    Proposed Solution¶

    Tier 1: Simple bearer token (minimal effort)¶

    1. Server generates or reads a token from config/CLI arg
    2. All API requests require Authorization: Bearer <token> header
    3. WebSocket upgrade requires token as query param: /api/v1/ws?token=...
    4. Frontend: token stored in localStorage after initial login
    5. Login page: simple PIN/token entry form
    # server.py
if self._config.auth_token:
    if headers.get("authorization") != f"Bearer {self._config.auth_token}":
        await _send_response(writer, 401, "Unauthorized", b"", {})
        return

    Tier 2: Multi-user with permissions (future)¶

    • Read-only vs full control
    • Per-user PTT lockout
    • Session management

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — auth check in _handle_http and _handle_websocket
    • src/icom_lan/cli.py — --auth-token CLI argument
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/http-client.ts — add Authorization header
    • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — add token to WS URL
    • New: frontend/src/components/auth/LoginPage.svelte

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] --auth-token CLI flag sets required token
    • [ ] Unauthenticated requests get 401
    • [ ] Frontend prompts for token on 401
    • [ ] Token persisted in localStorage
    • [ ] Without --auth-token, server runs open (backward compat)

    ✅ #189: perf: WebSocket per-message deflate (permessage-deflate)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P3, area:web-ui, area:api

    Summary¶

    Implement RFC 7692 permessage-deflate WebSocket extension to compress all WS messages.

    Decision Log¶

    • Full state delta (original proposal): Deferred — adds diff/merge complexity for marginal gain over compression
    • HTTP ETag/304: Already implemented in #177
    • WS permessage-deflate: ✅ Chosen — standard, simple, ~57% compression on state JSON

    Current State¶

    • state_update WS push: ~1800 bytes raw JSON × up to 50/sec = ~90 KB/s uncompressed
    • HTTP /api/v1/state: Already has gzip (1800→768 bytes, 57% savings)
    • WS messages: no compression — raw JSON text

    Implementation¶

    Backend (src/icom_lan/web/websocket.py)¶

    1. During WS handshake, check Sec-WebSocket-Extensions: permessage-deflate header
    2. If present, include in 101 response: Sec-WebSocket-Extensions: permessage-deflate; server_no_context_takeover; client_no_context_takeover
    3. Compress outgoing text frames with zlib (RSV1 bit set)
    4. Decompress incoming frames with RSV1 bit set
    5. Use no_context_takeover to avoid per-connection zlib state (simpler, less memory)

    Backend (src/icom_lan/web/server.py)¶

    1. Parse Sec-WebSocket-Extensions header during upgrade
    2. Pass deflate config to WebSocketConnection

    Frontend¶

    • No changes needed — browsers natively support permessage-deflate when server advertises it

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Browser DevTools shows permessage-deflate in WS upgrade response
    • [ ] WS messages compressed ~50-60% (state_update: ~1800→~768 bytes)
    • [ ] Scope binary frames: compression optional (already compact, may not help)
    • [ ] No frontend changes required
    • [ ] Backward compat: clients without deflate support still work (no extension = no compression)

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/websocket.py — deflate compress/decompress in send/recv
    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — extension negotiation in handshake

    ✅ #190: epic: UX Improvements & Control Refinements¶

    Labels: type:enhancement, area:web-ui

    Overview¶

    User feedback-driven UX improvements for the Svelte 5 frontend. Covers spectrum display quality, control layout/organization, missing features, and behavioral fixes.

    Goals¶

    1. Visual Quality: Spectrum/waterfall proportions, scales, color schemes, smoothing
    2. Layout: Logical control grouping, compact selectors, grid layouts
    3. Functionality: Add missing controls (DIGI-SEL, COMP), fix race conditions
    4. Polish: Long-press menus, analog meters, better aesthetics

    Sub-Issues¶

    P0 (Critical UX fixes)¶

    • 192 — Add DIGI-SEL toggle¶

    P1 (Important improvements)¶

    • 195 — Band selector grid 3×4¶

    • 196 — Compact STEP selector with arrows + long-press¶

    • 197 — ATT/PRE long-press menus¶

    • 198 — Analog gauge meters (canvas-gauges or SVG)¶

    P2 (Visual polish)¶

    • 199 — Frequency/dB scale labels with containers¶

    • 200 — Waterfall ref level + color schemes¶

    • 201 — Spectrum width indicators (±kHz corners)¶

    • 202 — Averaging + peak persistence¶

    P3 (Backend features)¶

    • 203 — COMP control backend handler¶

    Success Criteria¶

    • [ ] All P0 issues resolved
    • [ ] Spectrum display matches professional SDR UI quality
    • [ ] Controls organized logically, easy to find
    • [ ] Mobile and desktop layouts both improved
    • [ ] Zero regressions in existing functionality

    ✅ #191: fix(vfo): Arrow tuning race condition¶

    Labels: priority:P0, area:web-ui, type:bug

    Problem¶

    Arrow key tuning has a race condition: rapid keypresses cause frequency to flip-flop.

    Example: 1. Current freq: 7.266 MHz 2. Press ArrowLeft → optimistic update: 7.265 MHz, send command 3. Press ArrowLeft again (before server ACK) → optimistic 7.264? Or 7.266 (using stale base)? 4. Server responds with 7.265 → frontend jumps back to 7.266

    Root cause: Optimistic updates use current client-side freq as base, but don't account for in-flight commands.

    Current Code¶

    // DesktopLayout.svelte
function onKeyDown(e: KeyboardEvent) {
  const freq = tuneBy(1); // uses current state.main.freqHz
  sendCommand('set_freq', { freq, receiver: 0 });
}

    // ws-client.ts _applyOptimistic()
case 'set_freq':
  patchActiveReceiver({ freqHz: params.freq });
  break;

    Server ACK arrives → state_update overwrites optimistic value. If commands cross in flight, final freq != expected.

    Solution¶

    Option A: Sequence tracking - Tag each command with monotonic sequence ID - Track lastAppliedSeq per field - Ignore server updates with seq < lastAppliedSeq

    Option B: Debounce arrow keys - Accumulate deltas locally for 100ms - Send single command with total delta - Simpler but less responsive

    Option C: Command queueing - Only allow one set_freq in flight at a time - Queue subsequent arrow presses - Send next command after ACK

    Preferred: Option A¶

    Keeps UI responsive, fixes race properly.

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — add seq to commands
    • frontend/src/components/layout/DesktopLayout.svelte — tuning logic

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Rapid arrow key presses → freq increments correctly
    • [ ] No flip-flopping or jumps backward
    • [ ] Works with slow server responses (simulated latency)

    ✅ #192: feat(controls): Add DIGI-SEL toggle button¶

    Labels: type:feature, priority:P0, area:web-ui

    Add button after NR:

    <button
  class="toggle-btn"
  class:active={digisel}
  onclick={() => sendCommand('set_digisel', { on: !digisel, receiver: receiverIdx })}
  title="Digital Selection (DIGI-SEL)"
>DIGI</button>

    Get digisel from current receiver state (same pattern as nb, nr).

    ✅ #193: refactor(controls): Logical control grouping (DSP/Filter/TX blocks)¶

    Labels: type:enhancement, priority:P0, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Controls are scattered without logical organization. Hard to find specific features.

    Proposed Structure¶

    Create <ControlGroup> component for visual/logical grouping.

    DSP Block¶

    • NB (Noise Blanker)
    • NR (Noise Reduction)
    • DIGI-SEL
    • Notch (future: manual/auto notch when backend ready)

    Filter Block¶

    • Mode selector
    • Filter selector (FIL1/2/3)
    • Data mode toggle (if applicable)

    TX Block¶

    • COMP (when backend ready)
    • PWR slider
    • ANT1/ANT2
    • Microphone gain (future)

    RX Block¶

    • ATT
    • PRE
    • AGC display

    Tuning Block¶

    • STEP selector
    • Arrow buttons (or keep as keyboard-only)

    Implementation¶

    <ControlGroup title="DSP" collapsible={true}>
  <button class="toggle-btn" ...>NB</button>
  <button class="toggle-btn" ...>NR</button>
  <button class="toggle-btn" ...>DIGI</button>
</ControlGroup>

    ControlGroup.svelte: - Props: title, collapsible - Styled container with border, title label - Optional collapse/expand toggle for mobile

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/controls/ControlGroup.svelte (new)
    • frontend/src/components/layout/DesktopLayout.svelte — use groups
    • frontend/src/components/controls/FeatureToggles.svelte — split into groups

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Controls grouped visually with labeled sections
    • [ ] Easy to scan and find specific feature
    • [ ] Responsive: groups stack on mobile
    • [ ] No regressions in existing control behavior

    BandSelector uses display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap — buttons wrap arbitrarily based on container width.

    Requested¶

    Explicit 3-column grid (3×4 for 12 bands, or 3×5 for 15, etc.)

    Implementation¶

  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  gap: var(--space-1);
}

    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
  }
}
