    • [ ] Mobile: 2 columns
    • [ ] Buttons evenly sized
    • [ ] Consistent gaps

    ✅ #196: feat(controls): Compact STEP selector with arrows + long-press menu¶

    Labels: type:feature, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Current STEP selector shows ALL tuning steps as buttons → takes too much space.

    Proposed UI¶

    Compact display: 

    [◂] 1 kHz [▸]  AUTO

    • Arrows: cycle through TUNING_STEPS array
    • Long press (500ms): Open modal/dropdown with all steps (grid layout, large touch targets)
    • AUTO button: toggle auto-step mode

    Implementation¶

    StepSelector.svelte¶

    <div class="step-selector-compact">
  <button class="arrow-btn" onclick={stepDown}>◂</button>
  <button 
    class="step-value" 
    onpointerdown={startLongPress}
    onpointerup={cancelLongPress}
  >
    {formatStep(currentStep)}
  </button>
  <button class="arrow-btn" onclick={stepUp}>▸</button>
  <button class="auto-btn" class:active={auto} onclick={toggleAuto}>AUTO</button>
</div>

{#if showStepMenu}
  <StepMenu steps={TUNING_STEPS} onSelect={selectStep} onClose={closeMenu} />
{/if}

    StepMenu.svelte (new)¶

    Modal with grid of all TUNING_STEPS: - 3-4 columns on desktop - 2 columns on mobile - Large buttons (min 44px touch target) - Backdrop click → close

    Long-press detection¶

    let pressTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>;
function startLongPress() {
  pressTimer = setTimeout(() => showStepMenu = true, 500);
}
function cancelLongPress() {
  clearTimeout(pressTimer);
}

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/controls/StepSelector.svelte — refactor
    • frontend/src/components/controls/StepMenu.svelte (new modal)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Arrows cycle through steps
    • [ ] Long press opens full menu
    • [ ] Menu: grid layout, large buttons
    • [ ] Mobile-friendly (no tiny buttons)
    • [ ] AUTO toggle works

    ✅ #197: feat(controls): ATT/PRE long-press menus with all capabilities¶

    Labels: type:feature, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Current Behavior¶

    ATT: Click cycles 0 → 20dB → 0
    PRE: Click cycles P0 → P1 → P2 → P0

    Hardcoded presets, doesn't show all available values from radio capabilities.

    Requested Behavior¶

    Short click (tap): Cycle through common presets (fast access)
    Long press (500ms): Open modal with ALL values from capabilities.attenuator / capabilities.preamp

    Implementation¶

    ATT Example¶

    <!-- FeatureToggles.svelte -->
<button
  class="toggle-btn"
  onclick={cycleAtt}
  onpointerdown={startAttLongPress}
  onpointerup={cancelLongPress}
>
  ATT <span class="val">{attLabel(att)}</span>
</button>

{#if showAttMenu}
  <CapabilityMenu
    title="Attenuator"
    values={attValues} 
    current={att}
    onSelect={setAtt}
    onClose={closeMenu}
  />
{/if}

    CapabilityMenu.svelte (new reusable modal)¶

    Props: - title: string - values: Array<{value: number, label: string}> - current: number - onSelect: (val: number) => void - onClose: () => void

    Grid layout, highlight current value, backdrop click → close.

    Capabilities Integration¶

    import { getCapabilities } from '../../lib/stores/capabilities.svelte';

const caps = getCapabilities();
const attValues = caps?.attenuator?.values || [0, 10, 20]; // fallback

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/controls/FeatureToggles.svelte
    • frontend/src/components/controls/CapabilityMenu.svelte (new modal)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] ATT: short click cycles, long press opens menu
    • [ ] PRE: same pattern
    • [ ] Menu shows all capability values
    • [ ] Mobile-friendly (large touch targets)
    • [ ] Works for both MAIN and SUB receiver

    ✅ #198: feat(meters): Port reference SVG analog meters to Svelte 5¶

    Labels: type:feature, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Goal¶

    Port the AnalogSMeter and DigitalSMeter components from references/s-meter.js (React) to Svelte 5.

    Demo: http://localhost:8765/s-meter-demo.html (when local server running)

    Decision¶

    ✅ Custom SVG (not canvas-gauges)

    Why: - Reference design already exists (references/s-meter.js) - IC-7610-specific styling (not generic gauge) - Zero dependencies - Full control over smoothing/animation - Solves #202 (averaging) via useSmoothedValue pattern

    Components to Port¶

    1. AnalogSMeter (Priority 1)¶

    Features: - 144° arc (-126° to +18°) - Value scale: 0-12 internal (0-9 = S-units, 10/11/12 = +20/+40/+60dB) - Smooth needle animation (exponential smoothing, attack/release 0.08/0.38) - Color zones: white (S0-S9), red (S9+) with glow effects - Tick marks: 60 ticks, major every 5 - Concentric meters: S (main), Po, SWR, COMP (inner arcs) - TX indicator - Mode/filter/freq display placeholders

    SVG structure: - <defs>: filters (glowW, glowR), gradient (panel) - Background rect with gradient - Arc paths for scale zones - Tick marks via polar coordinates - Labels positioned with polar(cx, cy, r, angle) - Needle: rotated <g> with line + arrow polygon - Center hub: two circles

    2. DigitalSMeter (Priority 2, optional)¶

    Features: - 110 segments with glow - Color zones: blue → red at S9 boundary - More compact than analog

    Implementation Plan¶

    Phase 1: Helpers & Smoothing¶

    Create frontend/src/lib/utils/meter-utils.ts:

    export function clamp(v: number, min: number, max: number): number {
  return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, v));
}

export function lerp(a: number, b: number, t: number): number {
  return a + (b - a) * t;
}

export function polar(cx: number, cy: number, r: number, deg: number) {
  const rad = (deg * Math.PI) / 180;
  return { x: cx + r * Math.cos(rad), y: cy + r * Math.sin(rad) };
}

export function arcPath(cx: number, cy: number, r: number, startDeg: number, endDeg: number): string {
  const start = polar(cx, cy, r, startDeg);
  const end = polar(cx, cy, r, endDeg);
  const largeArc = Math.abs(endDeg - startDeg) > 180 ? 1 : 0;
  const sweep = endDeg > startDeg ? 1 : 0;
  return `M ${start.x} ${start.y} A ${r} ${r} 0 ${largeArc} ${sweep} ${end.x} ${end.y}`;
}

export function valueToAngle(v: number): number {
  const value = clamp(v, 0, 12);
  return lerp(-126, 18, value / 12);
}

    Create frontend/src/lib/utils/smoothing.svelte.ts:

    import { onMount } from 'svelte';

/**
 * Exponential smoothing for meter values.
 * React useSmoothedValue → Svelte  + rAF loop.
 */
export function createSmoother(attack = 0.12, release = 0.32) {
  let current = (0);
  let target = 0;
  let frameId = 0;
  let lastTime = 0;

  function update(newTarget: number) {
    target = newTarget;
  }

  function start() {
    lastTime = performance.now();

    const tick = (now: number) => {
      const dt = Math.min(0.05, (now - lastTime) / 1000);
      lastTime = now;

      const tau = target >= current ? attack : release;
      const alpha = 1 - Math.exp(-dt / tau);
      current += (target - current) * alpha;

      frameId = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
    };

    frameId = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
  }

  function stop() {
    if (frameId) {
      cancelAnimationFrame(frameId);
      frameId = 0;
    }
  }

  return {
    get value() { return current; },
    update,
    start,
    stop,
  };
}

    Phase 2: AnalogSMeter.svelte¶

    Replace frontend/src/components/meters/SMeter.svelte:

    <script lang="ts">
  import { onMount } from 'svelte';
  import { polar, arcPath, valueToAngle } from '../../lib/utils/meter-utils';
  import { createSmoother } from '../../lib/utils/smoothing.svelte';

  interface Props {
    value: number; // 0-255 raw backend value
    tx?: boolean;
  }

  let { value, tx = false }: Props = $props();

  // Map raw 0-255 → 0-12 scale
  // S0=0, S1=17, ..., S9=153, S9+20=187, S9+60=237
  const S_UNIT = 17;
  const S9_RAW = 153;

  let scaledValue = $derived(() => {
    if (value <= 0) return 0;
    if (value <= S9_RAW) return value / S_UNIT; // 0-9
    const above = value - S9_RAW;
    if (above <= S_UNIT) return 10; // +20dB
    if (above <= 2 * S_UNIT) return 11; // +40dB
    return 12; // +60dB
  });

  const smoother = createSmoother(0.08, 0.38);
  $effect(() => {
    smoother.update(scaledValue);
  });

  onMount(() => {
    smoother.start();
    return () => smoother.stop();
  });

  const angle = $derived(valueToAngle(smoother.value));

  // SVG constants
  const width = 860;
  const height = 470;
  const cx = width / 2;
  const cy = 310;
  const outerR = 255;
  // ... rest of constants

  // Tick marks
  const ticks = Array.from({ length: 61 }, (_, i) => {
    const scaleValue = (i / 60) * 12;
    return {
      angle: valueToAngle(scaleValue),
      major: i % 5 === 0,
      red: scaleValue > 9,
    };
  });
</script>

<div class="analog-meter">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 {width} {height}">
    <!-- Copy SVG structure from reference -->
    <!-- Needle with reactive angle -->
    <g transform="rotate({angle} {cx} {cy})">
      <line ... />
    </g>
  </svg>
</div>

<style>
  .analog-meter {
    width: 100%;
    max-width: 860px;
  }
</style>

    Phase 3: Integration¶

    Update DesktopLayout.svelte:

    <AnalogSMeter value={main.sMeter} tx={state?.ptt ?? false} />

    Value Mapping¶

    Backend → Frontend: - Raw: 0-255 (from IC-7610) - S0 = 0 - S1 = 17, S2 = 34, ..., S9 = 153 - S9+10 = 170, S9+20 = 187, S9+40 = 212, S9+60 = 237

    Scaled for meter (0-12): - 0-9: S-units - 10: +20dB - 11: +40dB - 12: +60dB

    Files¶

    New: - frontend/src/lib/utils/meter-utils.ts - frontend/src/lib/utils/smoothing.svelte.ts

    Modified: - frontend/src/components/meters/SMeter.svelte → AnalogSMeter - frontend/src/components/layout/DesktopLayout.svelte

    Reference: - references/s-meter.js — React source - references/s-meter-demo.html — live demo

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Analog meter renders with correct SVG structure
    • [ ] Needle moves smoothly (no jumps)
    • [ ] Attack/release smoothing works
    • [ ] Color zones (white/red) match reference
    • [ ] Value mapping: raw 0-255 → angle correct
    • [ ] TX indicator works
    • [ ] Responsive sizing (scales with container)
    • [ ] Performance: 60fps at desktop, 30fps at mobile
    • [ ] No regressions in S-meter accuracy

    Notes¶

    DigitalSMeter can be phase 2 (separate PR) if analog proves complex. Focus on analog first — it's the showpiece.

    ✅ #199: feat(spectrum): Frequency/dB scale labels with dedicated containers¶

    Labels: type:enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Frequency labels currently overlap with spectrum/waterfall graphics. Hard to read.

    Requested Design¶

    Frequency Axis¶

    • Positioned between spectrum and waterfall (not overlaid)
    • Shows tick marks + labels (e.g., -50 kHz, 0, +50 kHz)
    • Dedicated container with solid background
    • Spans full width

    dB Axis (vertical)¶

    • Positioned to the left of spectrum
    • Shows dB scale (e.g., 0, -20, -40, -60)
    • Dedicated container with solid background
    • Height matches spectrum panel

    Implementation¶

    Layout Structure¶

    <div class="spectrum-panel">
  <div class="spectrum-row">
    <div class="db-axis">
      <!-- Vertical dB labels -->
      <span class="db-tick" style="top: 0%">0</span>
      <span class="db-tick" style="top: 33%">-20</span>
      <span class="db-tick" style="top: 66%">-40</span>
      <span class="db-tick" style="top: 100%">-60</span>
    </div>
    <div class="spectrum-area">
      <SpectrumCanvas ... />
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="freq-axis">
    <!-- Horizontal frequency labels -->
    <span style="left: 0%">-50 kHz</span>
    <span style="left: 50%">0</span>
    <span style="left: 100%">+50 kHz</span>
  </div>

  <div class="waterfall-area">
    <WaterfallCanvas ... />
  </div>
</div>

    Styling¶

    .db-axis {
  width: 40px;
  background: var(--panel);
  border-right: 1px solid var(--panel-border);
  position: relative;
  font-size: 0.65rem;
  color: var(--text-muted);
}

.freq-axis {
  height: 20px;
  background: var(--panel);
  border-top: 1px solid var(--panel-border);
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--panel-border);
  position: relative;
  font-size: 0.7rem;
}

    Dynamic Labels¶

    Frequency labels should update based on current span: - Span 100 kHz → -50 / 0 / +50 - Span 200 kHz → -100 / 0 / +100

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/FrequencyAxis.svelte (new component)
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/DbAxis.svelte (new component)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Frequency axis between spectrum and waterfall
    • [ ] dB axis left of spectrum
    • [ ] Labels never overlap with graphics
    • [ ] Labels update with span changes
    • [ ] Clean, readable, professional appearance

    ✅ #200: feat(spectrum): Waterfall ref level adjustment + color schemes¶

    Labels: type:feature, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Summary¶

    Add user controls for waterfall display: 1. Ref level slider: Adjust brightness/contrast (-30 to +30 dB) 2. Color schemes: Selectable palettes (Classic, Thermal, Grayscale) 3. Background color: Dark blue #001020 instead of pure black

    Current State¶

    Waterfall uses fixed color mapping (hardcoded gradient). Background is #000 (pure black).

    Implementation¶

    1. Ref Level Control¶

    Add slider in SpectrumPanel controls area:

    <label class="ref-level-control">
  Ref Level
  <input 
    type="range" 
    min="-30" 
    max="30" 
    value={refLevel}
    oninput={(e) => setRefLevel(parseInt(e.currentTarget.value))}
  />
  <span>{refLevel} dB</span>
</label>

    Store in localStorage: waterfall-ref-level

    2. Color Schemes¶

    Dropdown selector:

    <select bind:value={colorScheme} onchange={applyColorScheme}>
  <option value="classic">Classic</option>
  <option value="thermal">Thermal (red-yellow)</option>
  <option value="grayscale">Grayscale</option>
  <option value="spectran">SpectrAn (blue-white)</option>
</select>

    Store in localStorage: waterfall-color-scheme

    3. Color Mapping¶

    Update waterfall-renderer.ts:

    const COLOR_SCHEMES = {
  classic: [
    { pos: 0.0, r: 0, g: 0, b: 32 },    // dark blue
    { pos: 0.3, r: 0, g: 100, b: 200 }, // blue
    { pos: 0.6, r: 0, g: 200, b: 100 }, // cyan
    { pos: 0.8, r: 200, g: 200, b: 0 }, // yellow
    { pos: 1.0, r: 255, g: 50, b: 0 },  // red
  ],
  thermal: [
    { pos: 0.0, r: 0, g: 0, b: 0 },
    { pos: 0.5, r: 128, g: 0, b: 0 },
    { pos: 0.8, r: 255, g: 100, b: 0 },
    { pos: 1.0, r: 255, g: 255, b: 100 },
  ],
  grayscale: [
    { pos: 0.0, r: 0, g: 0, b: 0 },
    { pos: 1.0, r: 255, g: 255, b: 255 },
  ],
};

function applyRefLevel(pixelValue: number, refLevel: number): number {
  // Shift brightness based on ref level
  return Math.max(0, Math.min(255, pixelValue + refLevel));
}

    4. Background Color¶

    Change waterfall canvas background:

    .waterfall-canvas {
  background: #001020; /* dark blue, not pure black */
}

    • frontend/src/lib/renderers/waterfall-renderer.ts — color schemes, ref level
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — controls UI

    • [ ] Color scheme dropdown changes palette
    • [ ] Settings persist across page reloads (localStorage)
    • [ ] Background is dark blue, not black
    • [ ] Schemes are visually distinct and usable

    ✅ #201: feat(spectrum): Spectrum width indicators (±kHz in corners)¶

    Labels: type:enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Summary¶

    Display current spectrum span at top corners of spectrum panel: - Top-left: -50 kHz (left edge offset from center) - Top-right: +50 kHz (right edge offset)

    Updates dynamically when span changes.

    Example¶

    Span 100 kHz → -50 kHz / +50 kHz
    Span 200 kHz → -100 kHz / +100 kHz
    Span 500 kHz → -250 kHz / +250 kHz

    Implementation¶

    SpectrumCanvas.svelte¶

    Add overlay labels:

    <div class="spectrum-container">
  <canvas bind:this={canvas} />
  <div class="span-indicators">
    <span class="span-left">{formatOffset(spanHz / -2)}</span>
    <span class="span-right">{formatOffset(spanHz / 2)}</span>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
  function formatOffset(hz: number): string {
    const abs = Math.abs(hz);
    if (abs >= 1_000_000) return `${hz / 1_000_000:+.1f} MHz`;
    return `${hz / 1_000:+.0f} kHz`;
  }
</script>

    Styling¶

    .span-indicators {
  position: absolute;
  top: 4px;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  padding: 0 8px;
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  font-family: var(--font-mono);
  color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7);
  pointer-events: none;
  z-index: 10;
}

.span-left, .span-right {
  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  padding: 2px 6px;
  border-radius: 3px;
}

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumCanvas.svelte

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Span indicators visible in top corners
    • [ ] Values update when span changes
    • [ ] Format: ±X kHz or ±X MHz
    • [ ] Readable contrast (semi-transparent background)
    • [ ] No interference with spectrum graphic

    ✅ #202: feat(spectrum): Averaging + peak hold/persistence¶

    Labels: type:enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    Spectrum display has large "spikes" (noise peaks) that make it hard to see actual signal patterns.

    Requested Features¶

    1. Moving average: Smooth out spikes by averaging last 3-5 frames
    2. Peak persistence: Show "ghost" of recent peaks (3-5 sec decay) for signal visibility

    Implementation¶

    1. Moving Average¶

    In spectrum-renderer.ts:

    class SpectrumRenderer {
  private frameHistory: Uint8Array[] = [];
  private readonly AVG_DEPTH = 5;

  render(data: Uint8Array, options: SpectrumOptions) {
    // Add current frame to history
    this.frameHistory.push(new Uint8Array(data));
    if (this.frameHistory.length > this.AVG_DEPTH) {
      this.frameHistory.shift();
    }

    // Compute averaged values
    const averaged = new Uint8Array(data.length);
    for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
      let sum = 0;
      for (const frame of this.frameHistory) {
        sum += frame[i];
      }
      averaged[i] = sum / this.frameHistory.length;
    }

    // Draw averaged data instead of raw
    this.drawSpectrum(averaged, options);
  }
}

    2. Peak Persistence¶

    Track per-pixel peak values with timestamps:

    class SpectrumRenderer {
  private peakValues: number[] = [];
  private peakTimestamps: number[] = [];
  private readonly PEAK_DECAY_MS = 3000; // 3 seconds

  render(data: Uint8Array, options: SpectrumOptions) {
    const now = Date.now();

    for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
      const val = data[i];

      // Update peak if current value is higher
      if (val > (this.peakValues[i] || 0)) {
        this.peakValues[i] = val;
        this.peakTimestamps[i] = now;
      } else {
        // Decay old peaks
        const age = now - (this.peakTimestamps[i] || 0);
        if (age > this.PEAK_DECAY_MS) {
          this.peakValues[i] = val;
        }
      }
    }

    // Draw spectrum (averaged)
    this.drawSpectrum(averaged, options);

    // Draw peak overlay (semi-transparent)
    this.drawPeaks(this.peakValues, options);
  }

  private drawPeaks(peaks: number[], options: SpectrumOptions) {
    ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)';
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    // ... draw peaks as line ...
  }
}

    3. User Controls¶

    Add toggles in SpectrumPanel:

    <label>
  <input type="checkbox" bind:checked={enableAveraging} />
  Averaging
</label>

<label>
  <input type="checkbox" bind:checked={enablePeakHold} />
  Peak Hold
</label>

    Store preferences in localStorage.

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/lib/renderers/spectrum-renderer.ts
    • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — controls

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Averaging toggle smooths out spikes
    • [ ] Peak hold shows "ghost" of peaks
    • [ ] Peak hold decays after 3-5 seconds
    • [ ] Toggles work independently
    • [ ] Settings persist across reloads
    • [ ] Performance: no lag at 30 fps

    ✅ #203: feat(backend): COMP control handler¶

    Labels: type:feature, priority:P3, area:api

    Summary¶

    COMP (compressor) is currently display-only in frontend. Add backend handler for control.

    Current State¶

    • Frontend: <span class="value-badge"> in FeatureToggles — shows state but not clickable
    • Backend: No set_comp handler in handlers.py
    • State: RadioState.compressorOn and compressorLevel exist

    Implementation¶

    Backend: handlers.py¶

    Add handler:

    case "set_comp":
    on = bool(params.get("on", True))
    level = int(params.get("level", 0))
    rx = int(params.get("receiver", 0))
    self._ensure_capability("compressor", "set_comp")
    self._ensure_receiver_supported(rx)
    # CI-V commands:
    # 0x16 0x44 (compressor on/off)
    # 0x14 0x0E (compressor level)
    q.put(SetCompressor(on, level, receiver=rx))
    return {"on": on, "level": level, "receiver": rx}

    Radio Protocol¶

    Check if IC-7610 profile supports compressor CI-V commands: - 0x16 0x44 — compressor on/off - 0x14 0x0E — compressor level (0-10 or 0-255?)

    If not implemented, add to radio command queue.

    Frontend¶

    After backend ready, update FeatureToggles.svelte:

    <button
  class="toggle-btn"
  class:active={comp}
  onclick={() => sendCommand('set_comp', { on: !comp, receiver: receiverIdx })}
  title="Speech Compressor"
>COMP</button>

    Add level slider (future enhancement).

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — add handler
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py — add SetCompressor command (if not exists)
    • frontend/src/components/controls/FeatureToggles.svelte — make button active

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Backend handler accepts set_comp command
    • [ ] CI-V commands sent to radio
    • [ ] Frontend button toggles compressor
    • [ ] State updates correctly (on/off)
    • [ ] Level control (optional, can be follow-up)

    Notes¶

    Priority P3 because: - Display-only COMP is acceptable for now - Most users don't adjust compressor mid-QSO - Can be implemented after higher-priority UX fixes

    ✅ #204: Audio WebSocket hangs: broadcaster stops sending frames to clients¶

    Labels: bug

    Problem¶

    Audio streaming stops working after some time (typically after multiple page reloads or tab switches). Backend continues receiving audio from radio but stops sending frames to browser clients.

    Symptoms: - Backend logs: audio: rx packet #N continues incrementing - Backend logs: audio: sent frame #N stops incrementing (last frame sent, then silence) - Multiple audio WebSocket connections accumulate (observed: 12 concurrent connections) - Browser: no audio playback despite RX AUDIO button active - Workaround: restart server

    Observed in session logs (2026-03-09 14:15-14:18 EDT): 

    14:15:54 INFO audio: sent frame #8500  ← Last sent frame
14:16:29 INFO audio: rx packet #1500   ← Packets from radio continue
14:17:04 INFO audio: rx packet #5000   
14:18:44 INFO audio: rx packet #15000  ← Still receiving, but no "sent frame" logs

    Concurrent connections: 

    14:16:29 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=6)
14:16:38 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=7)
14:17:05 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=8)
14:17:33 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=9)
14:17:37 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=10)
14:17:44 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=11)
14:18:04 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=12)

    Root Cause Hypothesis¶

    Deadlock scenario:

    1. Browser tab closed or network interrupted → WebSocket dies
    2. AudioHandler._sender_loop() blocks on await ws.send_binary(frame) for dead connection
    3. Queue fills up (maxsize=10) → put_nowait() starts dropping frames
    4. Dead handler never cleans up → queue stays in _clients dict
    5. AudioBroadcaster._relay_loop() continues pushing to all queues including dead ones
    6. Eventually all queues stall → no frames sent to any client

    Code paths involved:

    AudioBroadcaster._relay_loop() (handlers.py:1067-1098): 

    async for pkt in self._subscription:
    frame = encode_audio_frame(...)
    for q in list(self._clients.values()):
        try:
            q.put_nowait(frame)
        except asyncio.QueueFull:
            q.get_nowait()  # Drop oldest
            q.put_nowait(frame)

    AudioHandler._sender_loop() (handlers.py:1231-1255): 

    while not self._done.is_set():
    frame = await asyncio.wait_for(self._frame_queue.get(), timeout=0.5)
    await self._ws.send_binary(frame)  # ← Can block forever on dead WS
    sent += 1

    Issue: No timeout on ws.send_binary(). If TCP connection is half-open (RST not received), asyncio stream write can block indefinitely.

    Proposed Fix¶

    Option 1: Add timeout to WebSocket send (preferred) 

    try:
    await asyncio.wait_for(self._ws.send_binary(frame), timeout=5.0)
except TimeoutError:
    logger.warning("audio: send timeout, closing connection")
    break  # Exit sender loop, trigger cleanup

    Option 2: Background health check 

    # In AudioBroadcaster
async def _cleanup_dead_clients(self):
    while True:
        await asyncio.sleep(30)
        for cid, q in list(self._clients.items()):
            # Check if queue is stuck (no movement in 30s)
            # Remove dead clients

    Option 3: Shorter queue timeout in sender 

    frame = await asyncio.wait_for(self._frame_queue.get(), timeout=2.0)
# If no frames for 2s → connection likely dead

    Reproduction Steps¶

    1. Open web UI with audio enabled
    2. Reload page multiple times (Ctrl+Shift+R)
    3. Switch between tabs
    4. Wait 5-10 minutes
    5. Audio stops playing (observe backend logs)

    Environment¶

    • icom-lan version: 0.11.0 (latest main branch)
    • Radio: IC-7610
    • Browser: Chrome 131 / Safari 18
    • OS: macOS 15.3
    • Audio codec: PCM_1CH_16BIT (0x04) → web_codec=0x02

    Next Steps¶

    • [ ] Implement Option 1 (send timeout)
    • [ ] Test with multiple rapid reconnects
    • [ ] Add metrics: track active audio clients, queue depths
    • [ ] Consider WebSocket ping/pong for early dead connection detection

    ⬜ #205: TX audio: add MediaRecorder fallback for non-HTTPS contexts¶

    Labels: enhancement

    Problem¶

    TX audio currently requires WebCodecs (AudioEncoder), which only works on: - HTTPS - localhost/127.0.0.1

    This blocks production use over LAN/VPN (HTTP IPs like 192.168.x.x or Tailscale).

    User report: "TX worked before over HTTP" — but old UI also used WebCodecs. Likely used Chrome flags or SSH tunnel.

    Current Code¶

    frontend/src/lib/audio/tx-mic.ts: 

    static supported(): boolean {
  return (
    typeof AudioEncoder !== 'undefined' &&
    typeof MediaStreamTrackProcessor !== 'undefined'
  );
}

    Result: TX MIC button hidden on HTTP contexts.

    Proposed Solution¶

    Add MediaRecorder fallback for HTTP contexts:

    class TxMic {
  private useWebCodecs = false;
  private mediaRecorder: MediaRecorder | null = null;

  static supported(): boolean {
    // MediaRecorder works on HTTP!
    return (
      typeof MediaRecorder !== 'undefined' &&
      MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported('audio/webm;codecs=opus')
    );
  }

  async start(): Promise<string | null> {
    this.stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({...});

    // Try WebCodecs first (better quality, lower latency)
    if (typeof AudioEncoder !== 'undefined' && 
        typeof MediaStreamTrackProcessor !== 'undefined') {
      this.useWebCodecs = true;
      return this.startWebCodecs();
    }

    // Fallback: MediaRecorder (works on HTTP)
    return this.startMediaRecorder();
  }

  private startMediaRecorder(): void {
    this.mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(this.stream, {
      mimeType: 'audio/webm;codecs=opus',
      audioBitsPerSecond: 32000
    });

    this.mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (e) => {
      if (e.data.size > 0) {
        // Send WebM chunk
        this.sendFn(e.data);
      }
    };

    this.mediaRecorder.start(20); // 20ms chunks
  }
}

    Backend changes:

    Option 1: Accept WebM container (add demuxer) Option 2: Convert WebM→Opus on backend Option 3: Keep current (WebCodecs only, docs say "use HTTPS")

    Alternatives¶

    1. Document HTTPS requirement — tell users to use:
    2. Reverse proxy (nginx/caddy with Let's Encrypt)
    3. Tailscale HTTPS funnel

    4. SSH tunnel + localhost

    5. Chrome flag workaround: 

      chrome://flags/#unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure
Add: http://192.168.x.x:8080

    6. Native HTTPS in icom-lan web server — add --cert/--key flags

    Priority¶

    Medium — affects production deployments, but workarounds exist.

    References¶

    • WebCodecs security: https://w3c.github.io/webcodecs/#security-privacy
    • MediaRecorder on HTTP: works (no restriction)
    • Old UI (70c623e) also used WebCodecs — same limitation

    ✅ #207: Architecture & implementation audit: follow‑up tasks¶

    Summary¶

    Follow-up tasks from a comprehensive architecture and implementation audit of the icom-lan codebase. This issue tracks concrete, parallelizable work items so agents can address them incrementally.

    Scope: architecture, API surface, implementation quality, testing gaps, and performance/resource management. Security is explicitly out of scope for this audit.

    Context¶

    The audit reviewed the current multi-backend architecture (LAN + serial) built around the shared IC‑7610 core and the Radio/capability protocols, focusing on:

    • Layering: UDP transport → CI‑V core → shared radio core → backend adapters → consumers (CLI / web / rigctld).
    • API design: Radio + capability protocols vs historical IcomRadio and public package exports.
    • Implementation quality: mixin usage, type-safety, code duplication and coupling between layers.
    • Testing strategy: unit, integration, parity matrix for IC‑7610, and consumer-layer tests.
    • Long-lived session behavior: backpressure, queues, background tasks, and audio pipeline.

    The library is already mature and well-tested; the work items below are mostly evolutionary improvements and guardrails rather than fundamental redesign.

    Task groups and priorities¶

    Legend: - P0 – Highest priority, architectural or correctness risk, blocks clean evolution. - P1 – Important improvements to maintainability, clarity, and API ergonomics. - P2 – Nice-to-have refinements, documentation, and additional guardrails.

    Each task is intended to be self-contained and suitable for a dedicated agent/PR.

    P0 — Core architecture & type-safety¶

    • [x] P0: Decompose IC-7610 core into explicit runtime components
    • Problem: IcomRadio / Icom7610CoreRadio (in src/icom_lan/radio.py plus _control_phase.py, _civ_rx.py, _audio_recovery.py) remains a very large class with many private fields and cross-mixin coupling.
    • Why it matters: High risk of regressions when evolving behavior (LAN vs serial, scope/audio, watchdogs). Hard for new contributors/agents to reason about invariants.
    • Goal: Introduce explicit runtime components (e.g. CivRuntime, ScopeRuntime, AudioRuntime, or similar) that are composed by the core radio class instead of living as loosely coupled mixins. Minimize cross-component field access.

    • Acceptance:

      • New runtime classes encapsulate specific responsibilities with clear public methods.
      • Icom7610CoreRadio becomes a thin orchestrator of these runtimes.
      • Existing behavior and tests (including integration and parity matrix) continue to pass.

    • [x] P0: Replace type: ignore[attr-defined] mixin coupling with explicit host interfaces

    • Problem: Mixins in _civ_rx.py, _control_phase.py, _audio_recovery.py rely on implicit attributes of the host class, guarded only by # type: ignore[attr-defined].
    • Why it matters: Static typing no longer protects against refactors (missing fields will only fail at runtime with AttributeError). This undermines the project’s strict typing goals.
    • Goal: Define explicit host interfaces or protocols (e.g. CivRuntimeHost, AudioRuntimeHost, ControlPhaseHost) that enumerate required attributes/methods.

    • Acceptance:

      • Mixins are typed against these host interfaces/protocols, with no remaining attr-defined ignores for core fields.
      • mypy runs cleanly in strict mode.

    • [x] P0: Unify state polling & caching between web and rigctld

    • Problem: Web (src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py + radio_state.py) and rigctld (src/icom_lan/rigctld/poller.py, state_cache.py) have separate polling + cache implementations.
    • Why it matters: Behavior (TTL, fields, error handling) can drift over time; fixes in one path may not reach the other.
    • Goal: Introduce a shared polling/state runtime layer (e.g. SharedStatePoller + SharedState or similar) used by both web and rigctld.
    • Acceptance:
      • Web and rigctld rely on the shared polling/state implementation.
      • Any web/rigctld-specific projections stay in their own modules, but base semantics (what/when we poll, cache TTLs) are centralized.

    P1 — API design & duplication cleanup¶

    • [x] P1: Clarify and narrow the public icom_lan API surface
    • Problem: src/icom_lan/__init__.py exports both high-level APIs (e.g. Radio, create_radio) and many internal details (individual CI-V/scope commands, audio internals, IcomCommander, etc.).
    • Why it matters: Users can easily depend on internal details accidentally, making backward compatibility and refactoring harder.
    • Goal: Clearly define the “supported” public API and mark the rest as internal.

    • Acceptance:

      • Documented list of officially supported exports (e.g. Radio, create_radio, sync wrappers, exception types, select utilities).
      • __all__ and naming/packaging reflect that split (internals moved behind _-prefixed modules or separate namespaces where appropriate).
      • README / docs reference only the supported API in examples.

    • [x] P1: Promote backend-neutral Radio + create_radio as the primary usage pattern

    • Problem: Historical IcomRadio is still prominent in examples and mental model, despite the Radio Protocol and backend configs being the intended abstraction.
    • Why it matters: New consumers may bind directly to IcomRadio, fighting against the backend-neutral architecture and complicating future model/back-end expansion.
    • Goal: Make LanBackendConfig / SerialBackendConfig + create_radio the default in docs, CLI, and high-level guidance.

    • Acceptance:

      • Quickstart and main API docs demonstrate create_radio + Radio/capability protocols.
      • IcomRadio is clearly documented as a legacy/advanced LAN-specific entry point.

    • [x] P1: Refactor CLI to depend on Radio + capability protocols rather than concrete IcomRadio

    • Problem: src/icom_lan/cli.py still uses types and helpers that assume IcomRadio in several places, especially for audio and scope features.
    • Why it matters: This couples the CLI to one backend and makes it harder to support new backends/models, even when they implement the same capabilities.
    • Goal: Rework CLI commands to target Radio + capability interfaces (AudioCapable, ScopeCapable, etc.), with explicit model/profile checks only where strictly IC‑7610-specific behavior is required.

    • Acceptance:

      • CLI no longer type- or isinstance-checks IcomRadio except in clearly marked legacy code paths.
      • CLI works consistently across LAN and serial backends, where capabilities permit.

    • [x] P1: Factor out shared runtime_capabilities / radio_ready logic in web layer

    • Problem: Both web/server.py and web/handlers.py compute runtime capabilities and radio_ready state with slightly different local helpers.
    • Why it matters: Small semantic differences can lead to inconsistent responses between HTTP endpoints and WebSocket commands.
    • Goal: Extract a single runtime_capabilities(radio) / radio_ready(radio) implementation used across the web layer.

    • Acceptance:

      • Both server and handlers import and use the same helpers.
      • Behavior for capabilities and readiness is consistent across all web endpoints.

    • [x] P1: Deduplicate rigctld mode resolution logic

    • Problem: _get_mode_reader exists in both rigctld/server.py and rigctld/handler.py, implementing nearly the same ModeInfoCapable logic.
    • Why it matters: Changes to mode metadata or ModeInfoCapable behavior must be applied in two places, with risk of drift.
    • Goal: Provide a single helper (e.g. in rigctld/utils.py) for resolving mode info used by both server and handler.

    • Acceptance:

      • All rigctld components use the shared mode resolver.
      • Tests cover mode resolution behavior via this shared helper.

    • [x] P1: Slim down the base Radio Protocol into smaller capability protocols

    • Problem: The base Radio Protocol currently bundles a lot of operations, some of which are specific to certain families/features.
    • Why it matters: New backends may have to implement methods that are not meaningful for them, or rely on “unsupported” errors.
    • Goal: Break out additional capability protocols (e.g. LevelsCapable, DspCapable) so the base Radio surface is a minimal common denominator.
    • Acceptance:
      • Radio defines only the truly shared core operations.
      • Family-specific operations move into well-named capability protocols, with consumers updated accordingly.

    P1 — Tests & consumer integration¶

    • [x] P1: Add thin end-to-end tests for web and rigctld on top of real core with fake transport
    • Problem: Most web/rigctld tests use MagicMock/Stub radios instead of a real IcomRadio/core with fake transport.
    • Why it matters: Changes inside the core may subtly affect consumer behavior and only be detected by heavier integration testing or in production.
    • Goal: Add a small number of “semi-integration” tests that start WebServer/RigctldServer on top of the real core with mocked/fake transport.

    • Acceptance:

      • Web/rigctld tests include scenarios that exercise real CI‑V/core behavior through HTTP/TCP APIs.
      • Tests run reliably without requiring real radios or network access.

    • [x] P1: Formalize a visible mapping from parity/reliability matrices to tests

    • Problem: There is an IC‑7610 parity matrix and a rich test suite, but it’s not always obvious “which tests cover which matrix entries or reliability items”.
    • Why it matters: As new parity or reliability items are added, it’s easy to miss corresponding tests or to over/under-test specific families.
    • Goal: Provide a simple mapping (doc or lightweight meta-test) from matrix families to test files/cases.
    • Acceptance:
      • For each command family / reliability scenario, there is a documented link to one or more tests.
      • Future contributors can quickly see where to add tests when expanding the matrix.

    P2 — Performance, resource management & ergonomics¶

    • [x] P2: Add bounded capacity and drop policy to UDP packet queue in IcomTransport
    • Problem: In src/icom_lan/transport.py, self._packet_queue = asyncio.Queue() has no maxsize.
    • Why it matters: If the receive loop falls behind (e.g. due to regressions or system load), the queue can grow without bound under heavy scope traffic, risking memory growth.
    • Goal: Introduce a reasonable maxsize and a drop/overwrite policy with logging for overflow.

    • Acceptance:

      • Packet queue has a bounded size.
      • Overflow behavior is well-defined and logged at WARN level.
      • Existing tests continue to pass; new tests cover overflow behavior.

    • [x] P2: Add stress tests for long-running / bursty scope and audio scenarios

    • Problem: Current tests cover backpressure and queues, but there are few explicit tests simulating long-lived high-throughput scope/audio with slow consumers.
    • Why it matters: Long-running bursty sessions are common in real use and are where leaks/latency creep typically appear.
    • Goal: Add tests that simulate prolonged (within unit-test limits) high-frequency scope/audio traffic with intentionally slow consumers.

    • Acceptance:

      • Tests assert that queue sizes stabilize and no unbounded growth occurs.
      • Start/stop cycles for such scenarios cleanly tear down background tasks and queues.

    • [x] P2: Improve lifecycle diagnostics for background tasks (Radio, WebServer, RigctldServer)

    • Problem: Many long-lived background tasks (ping/retransmit loops, CIV RX, web pollers, DX-cluster, audio bridge) depend on callers invoking disconnect()/stop().
    • Why it matters: If users forget to do proper teardown (especially in sync scenarios), tasks may keep running until process exit, making leaks hard to diagnose.
    • Goal: Provide diagnostics (e.g. WARN logs or debug hooks) when objects are GC’d with live background tasks.

    • Acceptance:

      • If a Radio/WebServer/RigctldServer instance is collected while background tasks are still active, a WARN or DEBUG log is emitted.
      • Docs clearly emphasize async with / context managers as the supported usage pattern.

    • [x] P2: Review audio bridge TX path and executor usage for tuning hooks

    • Problem: audio_bridge.py uses run_in_executor around sounddevice.InputStream.read in the TX loop; under heavy load and with multiple clients, this can become a bottleneck.
    • Why it matters: On constrained systems or with several parallel audio consumers, this may introduce jitter or packet loss.
    • Goal: Provide hooks/settings to tune executor behavior or document recommended deployment patterns for heavy audio bridge usage.

    • Acceptance:

      • Either configuration knobs exist for audio bridge threading/executor behavior, or docs clearly recommend a separate process / limited client count for heavy scenarios.

    • [x] P2: Make CLI PID file handling optional and less global

    • Problem: src/icom_lan/cli.py writes a hard-coded PID file in /tmp/icom-lan.pid for CLI runs.
    • Why it matters: In multi-user or multi-instance environments, or during tests, this global file can cause conflicts and surprising behavior.
    • Goal: Make PID file creation opt-in or contextual (e.g. only for long-lived daemon-like commands), and avoid hard-coded global paths where possible.
    • Acceptance:
      • PID file handling is configurable (flag/env) and/or scoped to specific commands.
      • Multi-instance runs no longer share a single hard-coded PID path.

    P2 — Documentation¶

    • [x] P2: Update README / guides to align with backend-neutral architecture
    • Problem: Architectural docs are up to date, but quickstart and some API docs still primarily show IcomRadio usage.
    • Why it matters: New users will likely follow legacy patterns and miss the benefits of backend-neutral design.
    • Goal: Make backend-neutral usage (create_radio + Radio/capabilities) the canonical path in all public docs.

    • Acceptance:

      • README and guides demonstrate backend-neutral setup and usage.
      • IcomRadio appears only in advanced/legacy sections.

    • [x] P2: Document reliability semantics: timeouts, cache TTLs, and radio_ready/connection states

    • Problem: Fire-and-forget semantics, cache fallbacks, TTLs, and RadioConnectionState / radio_ready behavior are described implicitly in code and tests rather than in a single doc.
    • Why it matters: Consumers (web, rigctld, custom apps) and new backends need clear expectations for error/timeout semantics and state transitions.
    • Goal: Produce a focused doc section that defines reliability and timeout behavior as part of the public contract.
    • Acceptance:
      • A dedicated section/file in docs/ explains how timeouts, retries, cache TTLs, and state transitions work.
      • Web/rigctld docs reference this section when describing their behavior.

    ⬜ #208: epic: Post-Audit Cleanup — Quality & Type Safety Improvements¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    Post-Audit Cleanup: Quality & Type Safety Improvements¶

    Summary¶

    Following the 2026-03-11 architecture audit of icom-lan after the Issue #207 refactoring (P0-P2), this epic tracks 6 minor quality improvements identified during the audit. All are non-blocking but improve type safety, observability, and CI stability.

    Audit verdict: ✅ Project is in excellent shape. 0 critical bugs, 0 important issues.
    Context: 3163 tests passing, multi-backend architecture working, documentation up-to-date.

    Priority¶

    P2 (quality improvements, not blocking release)

    Phases¶

    Phase 1: Documentation & Quick Fixes → #209, #210¶

    Effort: 15 min
    Impact: Cosmetic (README accuracy), style consistency

    • [ ] #209 — Update README test count badge (1807 → 3163)
    • [ ] #210 — Fix ruff E402 warnings in sync.py

    Phase 2: CI Stability → #211¶

    Effort: 1-2 hours
    Impact: Prevents CI timeouts

    • [ ] #211 — Fix or skip hanging tests (test_transport.py, test_web_server.py)

    Phase 3: Type Safety → #212¶

    Effort: 4-6 hours
    Impact: Eliminates 202 mypy errors, improves IDE support

    • [ ] #212 — Complete Radio protocol type stubs (mypy 202 → 0)

    Phase 4: Observability → #213, #214¶

    Effort: 30 min each
    Impact: Better debugging in production

    • [ ] #213 — Add debug logging for unsupported capability commands in web poller
    • [ ] #214 — Replace sleep-based timing in test_audio_bridge with event-based sync

    Architecture Impact¶

    Files affected: - README.md (badge) - src/icom_lan/sync.py (import order) - tests/test_transport.py, tests/test_web_server.py (pytest.mark.skip or fix) - src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py (protocol stubs) - src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py (logging) - tests/test_audio_bridge.py (test stability)

    No breaking changes. All fixes are internal.

    Dependency Graph¶

    graph TD
    P1[Phase 1: Docs/Style] --> P3[Phase 3: Type Safety]
    P2[Phase 2: CI Stability] --> P4[Phase 4: Observability]

    Phases 1-2 can run in parallel. Phase 3 independent. Phase 4 after CI stable.

    Key Context for Agents¶

    Audit Report¶

    Full audit report available in this comment thread (posted below).

    Key findings: 1. ✅ Architecture clean, docs match code 2. ✅ All 3163 tests pass (0 regressions from refactoring) 3. ✅ Multi-backend (LAN/Serial) working correctly 4. ⚠️ 6 minor issues (all P2 priority)

    Project State¶

    • Version: v0.11.0
    • Last refactoring: Issue #207 (P0 runtime decomposition, P1 slim protocol, P2 audio bridge executor)
    • Tests: 3163 (109 files: 89 unit, 20 integration)
    • Architecture: Radio protocol + capability protocols (backend-neutral)
    • Backends: Icom7610LanRadio (UDP), Icom7610SerialRadio (USB CI-V + audio)

    Commands for Agents¶

    # Project root
cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Run tests
uv run pytest tests/test_*.py -v --tb=short

# Run mypy on web (strict boundary check)
uv run mypy --strict src/icom_lan/web

# Run ruff
uv run ruff check src/icom_lan/

# Check test count
uv run pytest --co -q 2>&1 | tail -1
# Expected: "... 3163 tests collected ..."

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] All 6 sub-issues (#209-#214) closed
    • [ ] No new test failures introduced
    • [ ] Mypy errors reduced (202 → 0 or documented plan)
    • [ ] CI passes without timeouts
    • [ ] Documentation reflects reality (README badge accurate)

    Non-goals¶

    • Performance optimization (not identified as issue)
    • New features (separate from quality fixes)
    • Rust migration (Phase 5 from ROADMAP — separate epic)

    References¶

    • Issue #207 — Runtime decomposition (closed)
    • docs/plans/p0-core-runtime-decomposition.md — architecture plan
    • docs/plans/mypy-remaining-errors.md — type safety roadmap
    • docs/reviews/2026-03-p1-p2-code-review.md — latest code review

    ✅ #209: docs: Update README test count badge (1807 → 3163)¶

    Labels: area:docs, priority:P2

    Update README test count badge (1807 → 3163)¶

    Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

    Problem¶

    README.md badge shows outdated test count: 

    [![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/tests-1807%20passed-brightgreen.svg)](#testing)

    Actual test count: 3163 (as of 2026-03-11)

    Verified with: 

    $ cd ~/Projects/icom-lan && uv run pytest --co -q 2>&1 | tail -1
======================== 3163 tests collected in 1.88s =========================

    Impact¶

    Cosmetic — badge misleads visitors about test coverage.

    Fix¶

    Update README.md:6:

    Before: 

    [![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/tests-1807%20passed-brightgreen.svg)](#testing)

    After: 

    [![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/tests-3163%20passed-brightgreen.svg)](#testing)

    Files¶

    • README.md — line 6 (badge)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] README.md badge shows "3163 passed"
    • [ ] Test count verified with pytest --co -q
    • [ ] Committed and pushed

    Effort¶

    ⏱️ 2 minutes

    ⬜ #210: style: Fix ruff E402 warnings in sync.py (module imports after code)¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    Fix ruff E402 warnings in sync.py (module-level imports after code)¶

    Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

    Problem¶

    Ruff reports 3 E402 warnings in src/icom_lan/sync.py:

    E402 Module level import not at top of file
  --> src/icom_lan/sync.py:21:1
   |
19 | T = TypeVar("T")
20 |
21 | from .audio import AudioPacket
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

    Root cause: TypeVar("T") definition placed before module imports (lines 21-24).

    Fix¶

    Move all imports to the top of the file, after from __future__ import annotations and TYPE_CHECKING block, but before TypeVar definitions.

    Current order (wrong): 

    from __future__ import annotations
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypeVar, overload
# ...

T = TypeVar("T")

from .audio import AudioPacket  # ← E402
from .radio import IcomRadio as _AsyncIcomRadio  # ← E402
from .radio_protocol import MetersCapable, PowerControlCapable  # ← E402

    Correct order: 

    from __future__ import annotations
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypeVar, overload
# ... other stdlib imports ...

from .audio import AudioPacket
from .radio import IcomRadio as _AsyncIcomRadio
from .radio_protocol import MetersCapable, PowerControlCapable
from .types import AudioCapabilities, AudioCodec, Mode

# Now TypeVar definitions
T = TypeVar("T")

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/sync.py — move lines 21-24 (imports) before line 19 (T = TypeVar)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] uv run ruff check src/icom_lan/sync.py — no E402 warnings
    • [ ] uv run pytest tests/test_sync*.py — all tests pass
    • [ ] No functional changes (import order fix only)

    Effort¶

    ⏱️ 3 minutes

    ⬜ #211: test: Fix or skip hanging tests (test_transport, test_web_server)¶

    Labels: priority:P1, type:bug

    Fix or skip hanging tests (test_transport.py, test_web_server.py)¶

    Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

    Problem¶

    Two test modules hang when running full test suite: - tests/test_transport.py - tests/test_web_server.py

    Symptoms: - uv run pytest hangs indefinitely (requires Ctrl+C) - Individual tests in these files may pass, but full module run blocks

    Impact: - CI timeouts (GitHub Actions has 25min timeout per job) - Developer frustration (full test run not possible)

    Status: Pre-existing issue (not caused by Issue #207 refactoring)

    Investigation Steps¶

    1. Identify hanging test(s): 

      cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Run with timeout per test
uv run pytest tests/test_transport.py -v --timeout=10 --timeout-method=thread
uv run pytest tests/test_web_server.py -v --timeout=10 --timeout-method=thread
      This will show which specific test(s) hang.

    2. Check for leaked asyncio tasks or background threads:

    3. Search for missing await in teardown
    4. Check for asyncio.create_task() without proper cleanup

    5. Look for threads without join() or daemon=True

    6. Check test isolation:

    7. Run hanging test in isolation: pytest tests/test_transport.py::test_specific_name -v
    8. Run with --log-cli-level=DEBUG to see last action before hang

    Possible Root Causes¶

    test_transport.py¶

    • Likely: _keepalive_loop() or _send_retransmit_request() background tasks not cancelled
    • Check: IcomTransport.disconnect() teardown — does it cancel all tasks?

    test_web_server.py¶

    • Likely: WebSocket connections not closed, or AudioBus subscription not unsubscribed
    • Check: Fixture teardown in tests/conftest.py or test-specific cleanup

    Fix Options¶

    Option A: Fix the root cause (preferred)¶

    1. Identify hanging task/thread
    2. Add proper cleanup in teardown (e.g. await transport.disconnect(), await ws.close())
    3. Verify with pytest --timeout=10

    Option B: Skip problematic tests temporarily¶

    If root cause is complex and not critical:

    # tests/test_transport.py
import pytest

@pytest.mark.skip(reason="Hangs in full test run — tracked in #216")
def test_keepalive_loop_sends_ping():
    ...

    Commit message: test: skip hanging tests in test_transport/web_server (#216)

    Files¶

    • tests/test_transport.py — identify and fix/skip hanging test(s)
    • tests/test_web_server.py — identify and fix/skip hanging test(s)
    • tests/conftest.py — check fixture teardown (if shared)
    • src/icom_lan/transport.py — check disconnect() cleanup (if root cause)
    • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — check stop() cleanup (if root cause)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Full test suite completes without hanging: uv run pytest tests/ --timeout=300
    • [ ] CI passes (GitHub Actions .github/workflows/test.yml)
    • [ ] Root cause documented in commit message or code comment

    Effort¶

    ⏱️ 1-2 hours (investigation + fix)

    Priority¶

    P1 (blocks CI) — should be done before other sub-issues.

    ⬜ #212: type: Complete Radio protocol type stubs (mypy 202→0 errors)¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    Complete Radio protocol type stubs (mypy 202→0 errors)¶

    Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

    Problem¶

    Mypy reports 202 type errors across 8 files due to incomplete Radio / AudioCapable / ScopeCapable protocol definitions.

    Documentation: docs/plans/mypy-remaining-errors.md (full error breakdown)

    Key Missing Methods¶

    Radio protocol¶

    • __aenter__ / __aexit__ (used in async with radio:)
    • IC-7610-specific methods used by CLI/web:
    • get_antenna_1(), set_antenna_1()
    • get_dual_watch(), set_dual_watch()
    • get_tuner_status(), set_tuner_status()
    • get_system_date(), get_system_time()
    • send_cw_text()

    AudioCapable protocol¶

    • start_audio_rx_pcm(), stop_audio_rx_pcm()
    • start_audio_tx_pcm(), stop_audio_tx_pcm(), push_audio_tx_pcm()
    • get_audio_stats()
    • audio_bus property (used by AudioBridge)

    ScopeCapable protocol¶

    • capture_scope_frame() → ScopeFrame | None
    • capture_scope_frames() → list[ScopeFrame]

    Strategy¶

    Option A: Extend protocols with optional methods (recommended)¶

    Add IC-7610-specific methods to protocols with optional stubs:

    # radio_protocol.py

@runtime_checkable
class Radio(Protocol):
    # ... existing core methods ...

    # --- Optional IC-7610 extensions ---
    # (Backends that don't support these can omit them)

    async def __aenter__(self) -> Radio:
        """Context manager entry. Optional — used by IcomRadio."""
        ...

    async def __aexit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
        """Context manager exit. Optional — used by IcomRadio."""
        ...

    async def get_dual_watch(self) -> bool:
        """Get dual watch status (IC-7610 only). Optional."""
        ...

    async def send_cw_text(self, text: str) -> None:
        """Send CW text (radios with CW keyer). Optional."""
        ...

    # ... etc.

    Pros: - CLI/web type-check without type: ignore - Clear which methods are core vs extensions - Backends can implement subset

    Cons: - Protocol becomes larger (but still readable)

    Option B: Create IcomRadioProtocol (extended protocol)¶

    @runtime_checkable
class IcomRadioProtocol(Radio, AudioCapable, ScopeCapable):
    """Extended protocol for Icom-specific features."""
    async def get_dual_watch(self) -> bool: ...
    async def send_cw_text(self, text: str) -> None: ...
    # ...

    Then in CLI/web: 

    def handle(radio: IcomRadioProtocol):  # instead of Radio
    await radio.get_dual_watch()

    Pros: - Radio protocol stays minimal - Clear separation: core vs Icom-specific

    Cons: - CLI/web become Icom-specific (breaks backend-neutrality)

    Option C: Use isinstance checks + cast¶

    from typing import cast
from icom_lan.radio import IcomRadio

async def handle(radio: Radio):
    if isinstance(radio, IcomRadio):
        ic7610 = cast(IcomRadio, radio)
        await ic7610.get_dual_watch()

    Pros: - Radio protocol stays minimal - Explicit backend type narrowing

    Cons: - Verbose, scattered casts - Defeats purpose of backend-neutral protocols

    Recommended Approach¶

    Hybrid: Option A + documentation

    1. Add optional method stubs to Radio / AudioCapable / ScopeCapable for IC-7610-specific features
    2. Document in protocol docstring: 
      """
Core interface for a controllable radio transceiver.

Methods marked "Optional" are IC-7610 extensions and may not be
available on all backends. Use `hasattr(radio, "method_name")` at
runtime if backend is unknown.
"""
    3. Add py.typed marker to ensure mypy picks up protocol changes
    4. Run mypy on full codebase and verify 0 errors

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py — add optional method stubs
    • src/icom_lan/cli.py — may need minor type annotations
    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — may need minor type annotations
    • src/icom_lan/audio_bridge.py — add audio_bus to AudioCapable
    • docs/plans/mypy-remaining-errors.md — update with status (or delete if all fixed)

    Testing¶

    cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Check current errors
uv run mypy src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py src/icom_lan/cli.py src/icom_lan/web/ --no-error-summary | wc -l
# Expected: ~202

# After fix
uv run mypy src/icom_lan/ --strict --exclude 'tests/' | grep error
# Expected: 0 errors (or only unavoidable ones in backends/serial.py)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Radio protocol includes __aenter__ / __aexit__ stubs
    • [ ] AudioCapable includes audio_bus, *_pcm methods, get_audio_stats()
    • [ ] ScopeCapable includes capture_scope_frame(s)
    • [ ] Radio includes IC-7610 optional methods (dual_watch, tuner, antenna, CW, date/time)
    • [ ] Docstrings mark optional methods clearly
    • [ ] Mypy errors reduced to <10 (or all documented as "unavoidable")
    • [ ] uv run mypy --strict src/icom_lan/web/ — 0 errors (web boundary check)
    • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/

    Effort¶

    ⏱️ 4-6 hours (protocol design + type annotations + testing)

    Priority¶

    P2 (quality — not blocking functionality)

    References¶

    • docs/plans/mypy-remaining-errors.md — full error list
    • src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py — current protocol definitions
    • Python typing docs: https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol

    ⬜ #213: observability: Add debug logging for unsupported capabilities in web poller¶

    Labels: type:enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Add debug logging for unsupported capability commands in web poller¶

    Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

    Problem¶

    When the web radio poller processes commands that require optional capabilities (e.g. SetPowerstat, SetRfGain, SetAfLevel, SetSquelch), and the radio does not implement the required protocol, the poller silently skips the command.

    Current behavior: 

    # web/radio_poller.py (approx line 180-200)
case "SetPowerstat":
    if isinstance(self._radio, PowerControlCapable):
        await self._radio.set_powerstat(data["on"])
    # else: silent no-op ← no feedback

    Impact: - When debugging "why isn't my command working?", there's no log entry - User may not realize their radio backend doesn't support the capability - Makes troubleshooting harder in production deployments

    Fix¶

    Add debug-level log when a command is skipped due to missing capability:

    # web/radio_poller.py
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ...

case "SetPowerstat":
    if isinstance(self._radio, PowerControlCapable):
        await self._radio.set_powerstat(data["on"])
    else:
        logger.debug(
            "Radio does not support PowerControlCapable, skipping SetPowerstat command"
        )

case "SetRfGain":
    if isinstance(self._radio, LevelsCapable):
        await self._radio.set_rf_gain(data["level"])
    else:
        logger.debug(
            "Radio does not support LevelsCapable, skipping SetRfGain command"
        )

# ... repeat for SetAfLevel, SetSquelch, SetPower, etc.

    Why debug level?¶

    • Not an error — radio is behaving correctly (doesn't support feature)
    • Not info — would clutter logs in normal operation
    • Debug is appropriate for "this is why nothing happened" troubleshooting

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py — add logger and debug statements

    Affected commands (search for isinstance(...Capable)): - SetPowerstat (PowerControlCapable) - SetPower (PowerControlCapable) - SetRfGain (LevelsCapable) - SetAfLevel (LevelsCapable) - SetSquelch (LevelsCapable) - Any other capability-gated commands

    Testing¶

    cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Create a minimal radio mock that doesn't implement LevelsCapable
# (or use a real backend that lacks some capabilities)

# Run web server with debug logging
ICOM_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG uv run icom-lan web --host 192.168.55.40 ...

# Send SetRfGain command from frontend
# Check logs for:
# DEBUG:icom_lan.web.radio_poller:Radio does not support LevelsCapable, skipping SetRfGain command

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Logger imported at top of radio_poller.py
    • [ ] Debug log added for each capability-gated command
    • [ ] Log message format consistent: "Radio does not support {Capability}, skipping {Command} command"
    • [ ] Manual test with debug logging confirms messages appear
    • [ ] All existing tests pass (logging is debug-only, no functional change)

    Effort¶

    ⏱️ 30 minutes

    Priority¶

    P2 (observability improvement — not critical)

    Reference¶

    Code review suggestion: docs/reviews/2026-03-p1-p2-code-review.md section 4 (Minor suggestions)

    ⬜ #214: test: Replace sleep timing with event sync in test_audio_bridge¶

    Labels: area:audio, type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    Replace sleep-based timing in test_audio_bridge with event-based sync¶

    Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

    Problem¶

    Test test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor in tests/test_audio_bridge.py uses sleep-based timing to verify that the custom executor is used:

    # tests/test_audio_bridge.py (approx line 66-78)
async def test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor(mock_radio):
    custom_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
    bridge = AudioBridge(radio=mock_radio, tx_executor=custom_executor)

    # ... setup ...

    # Start TX and let it run for a bit
    await bridge.start_tx()
    await asyncio.sleep(0.08)  # ← BRITTLE: timing-dependent
    await bridge.stop_tx()

    # Assert executor was used
    mock_run_in_executor.assert_called()
    assert mock_run_in_executor.call_args[0][0] is custom_executor

    Why this is brittle: - asyncio.sleep(0.08) assumes TX loop runs and calls executor within 80ms - On slow CI runners or under load, may not be enough time - On fast machines, wastes 80ms per test run - "Flaky test" anti-pattern — depends on wall-clock timing

    Fix¶

    Use event-based synchronization instead of sleep:

    Option A: Mock with side_effect that sets event¶

    async def test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor(mock_radio):
    custom_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
    bridge = AudioBridge(radio=mock_radio, tx_executor=custom_executor)

    # Event to signal executor was called
    executor_called = asyncio.Event()

    async def mock_run_in_executor_impl(executor, func, *args):
        executor_called.set()  # Signal test to proceed
        return None

    with patch("asyncio.get_event_loop") as mock_loop:
        mock_loop.return_value.run_in_executor.side_effect = mock_run_in_executor_impl

        await bridge.start_tx()

        # Wait for executor to be called (or timeout after 1s)
        await asyncio.wait_for(executor_called.wait(), timeout=1.0)

        await bridge.stop_tx()

        # Assert executor was the custom one
        assert mock_loop.return_value.run_in_executor.call_args[0][0] is custom_executor

    Option B: Use pytest-asyncio with wait condition¶

    async def test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor(mock_radio):
    custom_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
    bridge = AudioBridge(radio=mock_radio, tx_executor=custom_executor)

    call_count = 0
    executor_used = None

    def capture_executor_call(executor, func, *args):
        nonlocal call_count, executor_used
        call_count += 1
        executor_used = executor
        return None

    with patch("asyncio.get_event_loop") as mock_loop:
        mock_loop.return_value.run_in_executor.side_effect = capture_executor_call

        await bridge.start_tx()

        # Poll until executor called (max 10 iterations × 10ms = 100ms)
        for _ in range(10):
            if call_count > 0:
                break
            await asyncio.sleep(0.01)

        await bridge.stop_tx()

        assert call_count > 0, "Executor was never called"
        assert executor_used is custom_executor

    Recommended: Option A (explicit event) — cleaner and faster.

    Files¶

    • tests/test_audio_bridge.py — replace asyncio.sleep(0.08) with event-based sync

    Testing¶

    cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Run test 10 times to verify stability
for i in {1..10}; do
    echo "Run $i"
    uv run pytest tests/test_audio_bridge.py::test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor -v
done

# All runs should pass without timing failures

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Test uses asyncio.Event or equivalent for synchronization
    • [ ] No asyncio.sleep() based on arbitrary duration (0.08s)
    • [ ] Test completes faster (typically <10ms instead of 80ms)
    • [ ] Test passes consistently 10/10 times
    • [ ] Same assertion coverage (executor is the custom one)

    Effort¶

    ⏱️ 30 minutes

    Priority¶

    P2 (test quality — not blocking functionality)

    Reference¶

    Code review suggestion: docs/reviews/2026-03-p1-p2-code-review.md section 5 (Positive) — "timing with asyncio.sleep(0.08) is a bit brittle but acceptable"

    ✅ #215: epic: Post-audit fixes for #207 (6 remaining issues)¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

    Summary¶

    Post-implementation audit of issue #207 revealed 6 remaining issues that were either incompletely addressed or introduced during the refactoring. This epic tracks concrete fixes.

    Context¶

    Issue #207 defined 16 tasks (P0–P2) for architecture & implementation cleanup. An agent implemented all 16 and marked them complete. A subsequent full audit (2871 unit tests passing, mypy analysis, code review) found:

    • 1 critical bug blocking mock integration tests
    • 2 incomplete P0/P1 items (mixin→runtime migration, mypy clean)
    • 3 cleanup items (dead code, API surface, shared state)

    Sub-issues¶

    # Severity Issue Effort
    #216 🔴 P0 assert in transport.py breaks mock_integration tests 15 min
    #217 🟡 P1 Dead code: 611 lines of unused _ControlPhaseMixin + _AudioRecoveryMixin 15 min
    #218 🟡 P1 _AudioRecoveryMixin still has 24× type: ignore[attr-defined] 1–2 h
    #219 🟡 P1 mypy reports 197 errors (goal was 0) 3–4 h
    #220 🟡 P1 __init__.py exports internals, no __all__ 30 min
    #221 🟢 P2 Shared state polling is TTL-only helper, not full SharedStatePoller 2–3 h

    Dependency Graph¶

    graph TD
    A["#216 P0: Fix transport assert"] --> B["#217 P1: Remove dead mixins"]
    B --> C["#218 P1: Complete AudioRecovery migration"]
    C --> D["#219 P1: mypy cleanup"]
    D --> E["#220 P1: __init__.py __all__"]
    F["#221 P2: SharedStatePoller"]

    Items #216→#217→#218 are sequential. Items #219–#220 can parallel after #218. Item #221 is independent.

    Key Files¶

    File Role
    src/icom_lan/transport.py:52 Broken assert in _UdpProtocol.connection_made
    src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py:139-231 Dead _AudioRecoveryMixin with 24× type: ignore
    src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py:593-1110 Dead _ControlPhaseMixin (518 lines)
    src/icom_lan/radio.py 99 mypy errors (attr-defined from slim Radio Protocol)
    src/icom_lan/cli.py 41 mypy errors (calls methods not on Radio Protocol)
    src/icom_lan/__init__.py Missing __all__, exports 290 lines of internals
    src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py Only 48 lines — TTL helper, not full poller

    Non-goals¶

    • No functional changes to radio control behavior
    • No new CI-V commands
    • No web UI changes
    • Security audit (explicitly out of scope per #207)

    Follow-up to #207.

    ✅ #216: bug: assert in transport.py connection_made breaks mock integration tests¶

    Labels: priority:P0, type:bug, area:architecture

    Problem¶

    _UdpProtocol.connection_made() in transport.py:52 contains a bare assert isinstance(transport, asyncio.DatagramTransport) that fails when asyncio provides a non-standard transport wrapper (e.g. _SelectorDatagramTransport in loopback/mock scenarios).

    Steps to Reproduce¶

    uv run pytest tests/test_mock_integration.py -x -q --tb=short

    All tests fail with TimeoutError: Radio did not respond to discovery after 10 attempts.

    The root cause is visible in captured logs: 

    ERROR asyncio:base_events.py:1785 Exception in callback _UdpProtocol.connection_made(...)
AssertionError

    Expected vs Actual¶

    • Expected: connection_made stores the transport and allows UDP communication
    • Actual: assert raises AssertionError inside asyncio callback → silently swallowed → transport never stored → all sends fail → discovery timeout

    Root Cause¶

    src/icom_lan/transport.py:52: 

    def connection_made(self, transport: asyncio.BaseTransport) -> None:
    assert isinstance(transport, asyncio.DatagramTransport)  # ← THIS
    self._owner._udp_transport = transport

    The mock server's UDP transport may not satisfy isinstance(..., asyncio.DatagramTransport) depending on the asyncio event loop implementation. Assertions in asyncio callbacks are silently caught by the event loop error handler and never propagate.

    Fix¶

    Replace the assert with a type annotation or soft check:

    def connection_made(self, transport: asyncio.BaseTransport) -> None:
    # asyncio guarantees DatagramTransport for DatagramProtocol;
    # cast instead of assert to avoid silent callback failures.
    self._owner._udp_transport = transport  # type: ignore[assignment]

    Or, if we want to keep the runtime check:

    def connection_made(self, transport: asyncio.BaseTransport) -> None:
    if not isinstance(transport, asyncio.DatagramTransport):
        logger.error("Unexpected transport type: %s", type(transport).__name__)
    self._owner._udp_transport = transport  # type: ignore[assignment]

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/transport.py:52 — remove or soften the assert
    • tests/test_mock_integration.py — verify all tests pass after fix

    Tests¶

    • [ ] Fix the assert in connection_made
    • [ ] uv run pytest tests/test_mock_integration.py — all pass
    • [ ] uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration — no regressions (2871 passed baseline)

    Part of #215. Follow-up to #207.

    ✅ #217: refactor: remove dead _ControlPhaseMixin and _AudioRecoveryMixin (611 lines)¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

    Summary¶

    The P0 runtime decomposition (#207) introduced ControlPhaseRuntime, CivRuntime, and AudioRecoveryRuntime as composed runtime classes. However, the old mixin classes were not removed and remain as dead code:

    • _ControlPhaseMixin in _control_phase.py:593 — 518 lines
    • _AudioRecoveryMixin in _audio_recovery.py:139 — 93 lines

    Total: 611 lines of dead code.

    Evidence¶

    Neither mixin is imported or referenced anywhere:

    $ grep -rn "import.*_ControlPhaseMixin\|import.*_AudioRecoveryMixin" src/ tests/
# (no results)

$ grep -rn "_ControlPhaseMixin\|_AudioRecoveryMixin" src/icom_lan/radio.py
src/icom_lan/radio.py:366:    # Watchdog timing (used by _watchdog_loop; were on _ControlPhaseMixin)
# ^ only a comment reference

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Delete class _ControlPhaseMixin from src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py (lines 593–1110)
    • [ ] Delete class _AudioRecoveryMixin from src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py (lines 139–231)
    • [ ] Remove any remaining comments referencing the deleted mixins
    • [ ] Verify all tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration (2871 baseline)
    • [ ] No mypy regressions

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py — remove _ControlPhaseMixin class
    • src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py — remove _AudioRecoveryMixin class
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py — remove comment references to deleted mixins

    Part of #215. Follow-up to #207.

    ✅ #218: refactor: complete AudioRecoveryRuntime migration — eliminate 24× type: ignore[attr-defined]¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

    Summary¶

    The P0 decomposition (#207) created AudioRecoveryRuntime (lines 45–137) with a proper _host: AudioRuntimeHost pattern. However, the old _AudioRecoveryMixin code was copied into AudioRecoveryRuntime but still contains 24× type: ignore[attr-defined] — the exact pattern that P0 was supposed to eliminate.

    Problem¶

    src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py lines 147–231 in _AudioRecoveryMixin (and mirrored in AudioRecoveryRuntime._capture_audio_snapshot / _restore_audio) access host attributes via self._audio_stream, self._pcm_rx_user_callback, etc. with # type: ignore[attr-defined] on every line.

    24 occurrences: 

    $ grep -c "type: ignore\[attr-defined\]" src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py
24

    All 24 are in _AudioRecoveryMixin. But AudioRecoveryRuntime should be using self._host._audio_stream etc. — accessing through the explicit AudioRuntimeHost Protocol.

    Expected Design¶

    Following the pattern established by CivRuntime and ControlPhaseRuntime:

    class AudioRecoveryRuntime:
    def __init__(self, host: AudioRuntimeHost) -> None:
        self._host = host

    def _capture_audio_snapshot(self) -> _AudioSnapshot | None:
        h = self._host
        if h._audio_stream is None:  # ← typed via Protocol, no ignore needed
            return None
        state = h._audio_stream.state
        ...

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Verify AudioRecoveryRuntime accesses all host attributes via self._host (not self)
    • [ ] Remove all type: ignore[attr-defined] from _audio_recovery.py
    • [ ] Verify AudioRuntimeHost Protocol in _runtime_protocols.py covers all accessed attributes
    • [ ] Add any missing attributes/methods to AudioRuntimeHost
    • [ ] Verify all tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration
    • [ ] Verify mypy errors in _audio_recovery.py drop to 0

    Depends on #217 (dead mixin removal — remove _AudioRecoveryMixin first, then fix AudioRecoveryRuntime).

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py — fix AudioRecoveryRuntime to use self._host
    • src/icom_lan/_runtime_protocols.py — extend AudioRuntimeHost if needed

    Part of #215. Follow-up to #207 P0.

    ✅ #219: refactor: fix 197 mypy errors — capability protocol typing incomplete¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

    Summary¶

    Issue #207 P0 stated: "mypy runs cleanly in strict mode." Current state: 197 mypy errors.

    $ uv run mypy src/icom_lan/ --ignore-missing-imports --no-error-summary 2>&1 | grep "error:" | wc -l
197

    Error Breakdown¶

    By type:¶

    Error type Count
    attr-defined 67
    arg-type 41
    no-untyped-call 29
    union-attr 26
    assignment 15
    unused-ignore 7
    no-any-return 4
    call-overload 3
    Other 5

    By file:¶

    File Errors Primary issue
    radio.py 99 Methods moved to capability protocols but still called via Radio
    cli.py 41 CLI calls set_attenuator, send_cw_text etc. via Radio instead of capability protocols
    radio_poller.py 17 no-untyped-call, arg-type
    audio_bridge.py 12 no-untyped-call (sounddevice)
    scope_render.py 11 no-untyped-call (PIL)
    serial.py 7 Backend typing
    handlers.py 5 Web handler typing
    Other 5 Misc

    Root Cause¶

    The Radio Protocol was correctly slimmed down (P1 task). Methods like set_attenuator, get_system_date, set_dual_watch, send_cw_text, capture_scope_frame etc. were moved to capability protocols (AdvancedControlCapable, LevelsCapable, etc.).

    However, callers were not updated to narrow the type via isinstance checks before calling these methods. Example in cli.py:

    # Current (broken):
await radio.set_attenuator(True)  # mypy: "Radio" has no attribute "set_attenuator"

# Correct:
if isinstance(radio, AdvancedControlCapable):
    await radio.set_attenuator(True)

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Fix cli.py — add isinstance checks for capability protocols before calling extended methods (41 errors)
    • [ ] Fix radio.py — internal calls should use self (which is Icom7610CoreRadio, not Radio), verify typing (99 errors)
    • [ ] Fix radio_poller.py — add proper type annotations (17 errors)
    • [ ] Fix audio_bridge.py — add type stubs or TYPE_CHECKING imports for sounddevice (12 errors)
    • [ ] Fix scope_render.py — add type stubs or TYPE_CHECKING imports for PIL (11 errors)
    • [ ] Fix remaining files (17 errors)
    • [ ] Remove unused-ignore suppressions (7 errors)
    • [ ] Final: uv run mypy src/icom_lan/ --ignore-missing-imports reports 0 errors
    • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] uv run mypy src/icom_lan/ --ignore-missing-imports exits with 0 errors
    • [ ] No new type: ignore suppressions added (net reduction)
    • [ ] All 2871 unit tests pass

    Part of #215. Follow-up to #207 P0.

    ✅ #220: refactor: add all to init.py — narrow public API surface¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:api

    Summary¶

    Issue #207 P1 required: "Clearly define the supported public API and mark the rest as internal."

    The documentation side was done (docs/api/public-api-surface.md exists and is thorough). But src/icom_lan/__init__.py still exports everything (292 lines) without __all__, so users can from icom_lan import build_civ_frame, parse_header, IcomCommander — all internal details.

    Problem¶

    $ wc -l src/icom_lan/__init__.py
292

    No __all__ is defined. All imports are unconditional. Internal symbols like build_civ_frame, parse_header, serialize_header, IcomCommander, individual get_*/set_* command builders are freely importable.

    Expected¶

    Per docs/api/public-api-surface.md, the supported public API is:

    Core: - create_radio, Radio, IcomRadio - LanBackendConfig, SerialBackendConfig, BackendConfig - RadioState, RadioProfile, RADIOS, RadioModel

    Capability protocols: - AudioCapable, ScopeCapable, DualReceiverCapable - LevelsCapable, MetersCapable, PowerControlCapable, StateNotifyCapable
