    Exceptions: - IcomLanError, ConnectionError, AuthenticationError, CommandError, TimeoutError - AudioError, AudioCodecBackendError, AudioFormatError, AudioTranscodeError

    Types: - AudioRecoveryState, RadioConnectionState, Mode

    Everything else should be importable from submodules for advanced use, but not advertised in __all__.

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Define __all__ in src/icom_lan/__init__.py with only the supported public API symbols
    • [ ] Keep all current imports (don't break backward compat), but __all__ controls what from icom_lan import * exports
    • [ ] Verify docs/api/public-api-surface.md matches __all__
    • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/__init__.py — add __all__

    Part of #215. Follow-up to #207 P1.

    ✅ #221: refactor: expand shared state polling beyond TTL helper — deduplicate web/rigctld polling¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2, area:architecture

    Summary¶

    Issue #207 P0 required: "Introduce a shared polling/state runtime layer used by both web and rigctld."

    Current implementation (_shared_state_runtime.py) is only 48 lines — a DEFAULT_STATE_CACHE_TTL constant and an is_cache_fresh() helper. The actual polling logic remains duplicated:

    • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py — web-specific polling (248 lines)
    • src/icom_lan/rigctld/poller.py — rigctld-specific polling

    Both implement their own poll-interval logic, error handling, and cache write patterns.

    Current State¶

    # _shared_state_runtime.py (entire file):
DEFAULT_STATE_CACHE_TTL: Final[float] = 0.2

def is_cache_fresh(cache, field, max_age_s) -> bool:
    if max_age_s is None or max_age_s <= 0.0:
        return False
    return cache.is_fresh(field, max_age_s)

    This is better than nothing (shared TTL prevents drift), but falls short of "shared polling/state runtime layer."

    Design Options¶

    Option A: SharedStatePoller (full unification)¶

    Extract common polling logic into a shared class:

    class SharedStatePoller:
    """Polls radio state (freq, mode, etc.) on a configurable interval."""

    def __init__(self, radio: Radio, cache: StateCache, interval: float = 0.2):
        ...

    async def poll_once(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Poll all standard fields, update cache, return snapshot."""
        ...

    async def run(self) -> None:
        """Background polling loop."""
        ...

    Web and rigctld would both use SharedStatePoller and add their own projection/delivery on top.

    Option B: Shared poll helpers (lighter touch)¶

    Keep separate pollers but extract shared "what to poll and how" into _shared_state_runtime.py:

    async def poll_frequency(radio: Radio, cache: StateCache) -> int | None: ...
async def poll_mode(radio: Radio, cache: StateCache) -> tuple[Mode, int] | None: ...
async def poll_standard_fields(radio: Radio, cache: StateCache) -> dict: ...

    Recommendation¶

    Option B is more pragmatic — web and rigctld have different polling intervals, different field sets, and different error handling needs. Full unification (Option A) may over-constrain them.

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Analyze differences between web/radio_poller.py and rigctld/poller.py polling logic
    • [ ] Extract shared "poll field X with cache check" helpers into _shared_state_runtime.py
    • [ ] Update both pollers to use shared helpers
    • [ ] Verify behavior parity: web and rigctld return same values for same radio state
    • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py — expand with shared poll helpers
    • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py — refactor to use shared helpers
    • src/icom_lan/rigctld/poller.py — refactor to use shared helpers

    Part of #215. Follow-up to #207 P0.

    ✅ #222: epic: Serial port auto-discovery for Icom radios¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

    Summary¶

    Automatic discovery of Icom radios on USB serial ports. Extend icom-lan discover to scan serial devices, auto-detect baud rate, probe CI-V addresses, and identify radio models — zero manual configuration needed.

    Motivation¶

    Current state: User must manually specify serial port path and baud rate: 

    export ICOM_SERIAL_PORT=/dev/cu.usbserial-11320
export ICOM_SERIAL_BAUD=19200
icom-lan status

    Problem: Requires trial-and-error (wrong port → timeout, wrong baud → garbage).

    Goal: Plug-and-play experience matching LAN discovery: 

    icom-lan discover
# Output:
# LAN radios:
#   IC-7610 @ 192.168.55.40 (LAN)
#
# Serial radios:
#   IC-7610 (0x98) @ /dev/cu.usbserial-11320 (19200 baud)
#   IC-705 (0xA4) @ /dev/cu.usbserial-54321 (115200 baud)

    Architecture¶

    graph TD
    A[icom-lan discover] --> B[LAN Discovery]
    A --> C[Serial Discovery]
    C --> D[list_ports.comports]
    D --> E[Filter candidates]
    E --> F[Probe each port]
    F --> G{CI-V response?}
    G -->|Yes| H[Parse model + address]
    G -->|No| I[Skip]
    H --> J[Return radio info]

    Design¶

    1. Serial Port Enumeration¶

    • Use pyserial.tools.list_ports.comports()
    • Filter candidates:
    • USB serial devices (exclude Bluetooth, virtual)
    • Known Icom VID (if available in metadata)
    • Skip audio-only devices (macOS IC-7610 creates 2 ports: CI-V + audio)

    2. Baud Rate Auto-Detection¶

    • Try common rates in order: [19200, 9600, 115200, 4800]
    • Stop on first successful CI-V response
    • Default: 19200 (most common for Icom)

    3. CI-V Probing¶

    • Send Read Transceiver ID command: FE FE 00 E0 19 00 FD
    • 00 = broadcast address (any radio responds)
    • 19 00 = "who are you?"
    • Parse response: FE FE E0 <addr> 19 00 <model_id> FD
    • Extract <addr> (e.g. 0x98 for IC-7610)
    • Extract <model_id> (2 bytes BCD, e.g. 0x01 0x06 = IC-7610)

    4. Model Identification¶

    • Map CI-V address + model ID → radio name
    • Table: 
      RADIO_MAP = {
    0x98: ("IC-7610", b"\x01\x06"),
    0xA4: ("IC-705", b"\x01\x05"),
    0x94: ("IC-7300", b"\x01\x01"),
    0xA2: ("IC-9700", b"\x01\x20"),
    # ...
}

    5. Integration with icom-lan discover¶

    • Unified output: LAN + serial results in one table
    • Priority: LAN first (faster), then serial (slower)
    • Parallel: Probe serial ports concurrently (asyncio)

    Phases¶

    Phase 1: Serial port enumeration + filtering → #223¶

    • [ ] Implement enumerate_serial_ports() with candidate filtering
    • [ ] Filter logic: USB serial only, skip Bluetooth/virtual/audio
    • [ ] Unit tests with mock list_ports.comports()

    Phase 2: CI-V probe logic → #224¶

    • [ ] Implement probe_serial_civ(port, baud) — send 0x19 0x00, parse response
    • [ ] Baud rate iteration (19200 → 9600 → 115200 → 4800)
    • [ ] Timeout handling (1s per probe)
    • [ ] Unit tests with mock serial transport

    Phase 3: Model identification + mapping → #225¶

    • [ ] Add SERIAL_RADIO_MAP (CI-V addr + model ID → name)
    • [ ] Implement identify_radio(address, model_bytes) → radio name
    • [ ] Support IC-7610, IC-705, IC-7300, IC-9700 (MVP)
    • [ ] Unit tests for model mapping

    Phase 4: Integration with discover command → #226¶

    • [ ] Update CLI discover to call both LAN and serial discovery
    • [ ] Unified output format (table or JSON)
    • [ ] Add --serial-only and --lan-only flags (optional)
    • [ ] Update docs: guide/quickstart.md, guide/cli.md
    • [ ] Integration test (requires USB radio)

    Dependency Graph¶

    graph TD
    P1[Phase 1: Enumeration] --> P4[Phase 4: Integration]
    P2[Phase 2: CI-V probe] --> P4
    P3[Phase 3: Model ID] --> P4
    P1 -.parallel.-> P2
    P1 -.parallel.-> P3

    Parallel work: Phases 1-3 can be developed independently, merged in Phase 4.

    Key Files¶

    File Role
    src/icom_lan/discovery.py (new) Serial discovery logic
    src/icom_lan/cli.py Update discover command
    src/icom_lan/radios.py Add SERIAL_RADIO_MAP
    tests/test_discovery.py (new) Unit tests for enumeration/probe
    docs/guide/quickstart.md Update discovery examples

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] icom-lan discover finds radios on both LAN and serial
    • [ ] Auto-detects baud rate (no manual config)
    • [ ] Identifies IC-7610, IC-705, IC-7300 correctly
    • [ ] Graceful failure (no crash if no serial radios found)
    • [ ] < 5s discovery time (parallel probing)
    • [ ] Docs updated with serial discovery workflow

    Non-goals¶

    • RS-BA1 protocol support — only CI-V over serial
    • Auto-connect — discovery returns info, user still picks radio manually
    • Windows COM port mapping — focus on macOS/Linux first (can add later)

    References¶

    • Current LAN discovery: src/icom_lan/transport.py:discover_radios()
    • CI-V protocol: docs/internals/protocol.md
    • pyserial docs: https://pyserial.readthedocs.io/
    • IC-7610 USB setup guide: docs/guide/ic7610-usb-setup.md

    ✅ #223: feat: Serial port enumeration with filtering (Epic #222 Phase 1)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

    Summary¶

    Implement serial port enumeration with candidate filtering for Icom radio discovery.

    Part of Epic #222 — Phase 1.

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Implement enumerate_serial_ports() in new src/icom_lan/discovery.py
    • [ ] Filter logic:
    • Include: USB serial devices
    • Exclude: Bluetooth (/dev/cu.Bluetooth-*)
    • Exclude: virtual ports (/dev/cu.debug-*, /dev/cu.wlan-*)
    • Exclude: audio-only devices (macOS IC-7610 creates 2 ports — skip audio)
    • [ ] Return list of SerialPortCandidate (path, description, hwid)
    • [ ] Unit tests with mocked list_ports.comports()

    Design¶

    @dataclass
class SerialPortCandidate:
    device: str           # /dev/cu.usbserial-11320
    description: str      # "USB Serial"
    hwid: str | None      # Hardware ID

def enumerate_serial_ports() -> list[SerialPortCandidate]:
    """Enumerate candidate USB serial ports for Icom radios."""
    from serial.tools import list_ports

    candidates = []
    for port in list_ports.comports():
        if _is_candidate(port):
            candidates.append(SerialPortCandidate(
                device=port.device,
                description=port.description,
                hwid=port.hwid,
            ))
    return candidates

def _is_candidate(port) -> bool:
    """Filter out non-USB, Bluetooth, virtual ports."""
    # Exclude Bluetooth
    if "Bluetooth" in port.device:
        return False
    # Exclude virtual
    if any(x in port.device for x in ["debug", "wlan", "spi"]):
        return False
    # Include USB serial
    if "usb" in port.device.lower():
        return True
    return False

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/discovery.py (new) — enumeration logic
    • tests/test_discovery.py (new) — unit tests

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Function returns USB serial ports only
    • [ ] Filters out Bluetooth, virtual, audio ports
    • [ ] Unit tests cover macOS, Linux port patterns
    • [ ] No dependencies on radio hardware (pure filtering)

    References¶

    • pyserial docs: https://pyserial.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tools.html
    • macOS port naming: /dev/cu.* vs /dev/tty.*

    ✅ #224: feat: CI-V probing with auto baud detection (Epic #222 Phase 2)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

    Summary¶

    Implement CI-V probing over serial with auto baud-rate detection.

    Part of Epic #222 — Phase 2.

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Implement probe_serial_civ(port: str, baud_rates: list[int]) → CivProbeResult | None
    • [ ] CI-V frame construction: FE FE 00 E0 19 00 FD (Read Transceiver ID, broadcast)
    • [ ] Response parsing: FE FE E0 <addr> 19 00 <model_id> FD
    • [ ] Baud rate iteration: [19200, 9600, 115200, 4800]
    • [ ] Timeout: 1s per baud rate attempt
    • [ ] Unit tests with mock serial transport

    Design¶

    @dataclass
class CivProbeResult:
    port: str
    baud: int
    address: int       # CI-V address (0x98, 0xA4, etc.)
    model_id: bytes    # 2 bytes BCD

async def probe_serial_civ(
    port: str,
    baud_rates: list[int] = [19200, 9600, 115200, 4800],
    timeout: float = 1.0,
) -> CivProbeResult | None:
    """Probe serial port for CI-V radio."""
    for baud in baud_rates:
        try:
            result = await _try_baud(port, baud, timeout)
            if result:
                return result
        except SerialException:
            continue
    return None

async def _try_baud(port: str, baud: int, timeout: float) -> CivProbeResult | None:
    """Open serial, send CI-V probe, parse response."""
    # Open serial port
    reader, writer = await serial_asyncio.open_serial_connection(
        url=port, baudrate=baud, timeout=timeout
    )

    # Send: FE FE 00 E0 19 00 FD (Read Transceiver ID, broadcast)
    cmd = bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, 0x00, 0xE0, 0x19, 0x00, 0xFD])
    writer.write(cmd)
    await writer.drain()

    # Read response (max 64 bytes, timeout 1s)
    try:
        data = await asyncio.wait_for(reader.read(64), timeout=timeout)
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        writer.close()
        await writer.wait_closed()
        return None

    # Parse: FE FE E0 <addr> 19 00 <model_id> FD
    if len(data) < 9 or data[0:2] != b'\xFE\xFE':
        writer.close()
        await writer.wait_closed()
        return None

    address = data[3]
    model_id = data[6:8]  # 2 bytes BCD

    writer.close()
    await writer.wait_closed()

    return CivProbeResult(
        port=port,
        baud=baud,
        address=address,
        model_id=model_id,
    )

    Protocol¶

    CI-V Frame Structure¶

    ┌──────┬──────┬──────┬──────┬─────┬─────┬──────┐
│ FE   │ FE   │ To   │ From │ Cmd │ Sub │ FD   │
├──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼─────┼─────┼──────┤
│ 0xFE │ 0xFE │ 0x00 │ 0xE0 │ 0x19│ 0x00│ 0xFD │
└──────┴──────┴──────┴──────┴─────┴─────┴──────┘
         Preamble  Broadcast Controller

    Response¶

    ┌──────┬──────┬──────┬──────┬─────┬─────┬──────────┬──────┐
│ FE   │ FE   │ To   │ From │ Cmd │ Sub │ Model ID │ FD   │
├──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼─────┼─────┼──────────┼──────┤
│ 0xFE │ 0xFE │ 0xE0 │ 0x98 │ 0x19│ 0x00│ 0x01 0x06│ 0xFD │
└──────┴──────┴──────┴──────┴─────┴─────┴──────────┴──────┘
                     IC-7610        IC-7610 model

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/discovery.py — probe logic
    • tests/test_discovery.py — unit tests with mock serial

    Dependencies¶

    • Depends on #223 (enumeration) — uses SerialPortCandidate
    • pyserial-asyncio — already in pyproject.toml optional deps

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Function returns CivProbeResult on success, None on timeout/failure
    • [ ] Tries baud rates in order, stops on first success
    • [ ] Parses CI-V response correctly (address + model ID)
    • [ ] Unit tests mock serial transport (no hardware)
    • [ ] Graceful error handling (port busy, permission denied)

    References¶

    • CI-V protocol: docs/internals/protocol.md
    • IC-7610 model ID: 0x01 0x06 (from wfview reference)
    • pyserial-asyncio: https://pyserial-asyncio.readthedocs.io/

    ✅ #225: feat: Radio model identification from CI-V data (Epic #222 Phase 3)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

    Summary¶

    Radio model identification from CI-V address and model ID bytes.

    Part of Epic #222 — Phase 3.

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Add SERIAL_RADIO_MAP to src/icom_lan/radios.py
    • [ ] Implement identify_radio(address: int, model_id: bytes) -> str | None
    • [ ] Support IC-7610, IC-705, IC-7300, IC-9700 (MVP)
    • [ ] Fallback: return f"Unknown (0x{address:02X})" if not in map
    • [ ] Unit tests for all supported models

    Design¶

    # src/icom_lan/radios.py

SERIAL_RADIO_MAP: dict[int, tuple[str, bytes]] = {
    # address: (name, model_id_bcd)
    0x98: ("IC-7610", b"\x01\x06"),
    0xA4: ("IC-705", b"\x01\x05"),
    0x94: ("IC-7300", b"\x01\x01"),
    0xA2: ("IC-9700", b"\x01\x20"),
    0x8E: ("IC-7851", b"\x01\x35"),
    0x96: ("IC-R8600", b"\x01\x26"),
}

def identify_radio(address: int, model_id: bytes) -> str:
    """Identify radio from CI-V address and model ID.

    Args:
        address: CI-V address (e.g. 0x98 for IC-7610)
        model_id: 2-byte BCD model ID from 0x19 0x00 response

    Returns:
        Radio name (e.g. "IC-7610") or "Unknown (0xXX)"
    """
    entry = SERIAL_RADIO_MAP.get(address)
    if not entry:
        return f"Unknown (0x{address:02X})"

    expected_name, expected_id = entry

    # Verify model ID matches (optional strict check)
    if model_id != expected_id:
        logger.warning(
            f"CI-V address {address:02X} maps to {expected_name}, "
            f"but model ID {model_id.hex()} != {expected_id.hex()}"
        )

    return expected_name

    Model ID Reference¶

    Radio CI-V Addr Model ID (BCD) Hex
    IC-7610 0x98 01 06 \x01\x06
    IC-705 0xA4 01 05 \x01\x05
    IC-7300 0x94 01 01 \x01\x01
    IC-9700 0xA2 01 20 \x01\x20
    IC-7851 0x8E 01 35 \x01\x35
    IC-R8600 0x96 01 26 \x01\x26

    Source: wfview rigctrl.cpp model ID mapping

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/radios.py — add SERIAL_RADIO_MAP and identify_radio()
    • tests/test_radios.py — unit tests

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] All MVP radios (7610/705/7300/9700) identified correctly
    • [ ] Unknown address → fallback "Unknown (0xXX)"
    • [ ] Warning logged if model ID mismatch (address maps but ID differs)
    • [ ] Unit tests cover all supported models + unknown

    References¶

    • wfview source: rigidentities.h (model ID list)
    • CI-V address list: IC-7610 manual Appendix (CI-V command reference)

    ✅ #226: feat: Integrate serial discovery into CLI (Epic #222 Phase 4)¶

    Labels: enhancement, area:cli, priority:P1, area:serial

    Summary¶

    Integrate serial discovery into icom-lan discover command with unified LAN + serial output.

    Part of Epic #222 — Phase 4 (final integration).

    Tasks¶

    • [ ] Update cli.py discover command to call both LAN and serial discovery
    • [ ] Unified output format (table showing both LAN and serial radios)
    • [ ] Add optional flags: --serial-only, --lan-only (for debugging)
    • [ ] Parallel execution: LAN + serial discovery run concurrently
    • [ ] Update documentation:
    • docs/guide/quickstart.md — add serial discovery example
    • docs/guide/cli.md — document discover command
    • README.md — Quick Start section
    • [ ] Integration test (requires USB radio plugged in)

    Design¶

    CLI Command Update¶

    # src/icom_lan/cli.py

@app.command()
def discover(
    serial_only: bool = typer.Option(False, "--serial-only", help="Only scan serial ports"),
    lan_only: bool = typer.Option(False, "--lan-only", help="Only scan LAN"),
    timeout: float = typer.Option(5.0, "--timeout", help="Discovery timeout (seconds)"),
):
    """Discover Icom radios on LAN and serial ports."""
    import asyncio
    from icom_lan.discovery import discover_lan_radios, discover_serial_radios

    async def _run():
        tasks = []
        if not serial_only:
            tasks.append(discover_lan_radios(timeout=timeout))
        if not lan_only:
            tasks.append(discover_serial_radios())

        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

        lan_radios = results[0] if not serial_only else []
        serial_radios = results[1] if not lan_only else []

        # Print results
        if lan_radios:
            typer.echo("LAN radios:")
            for radio in lan_radios:
                typer.echo(f"  {radio.model} @ {radio.host} (LAN)")

        if serial_radios:
            typer.echo("\nSerial radios:")
            for radio in serial_radios:
                typer.echo(f"  {radio.model} (0x{radio.address:02X}) @ {radio.port} ({radio.baud} baud)")

        if not lan_radios and not serial_radios:
            typer.echo("No radios found.")

    asyncio.run(_run())

    Output Format¶

    $ icom-lan discover

LAN radios:
  IC-7610 @ 192.168.55.40 (LAN)

Serial radios:
  IC-7610 (0x98) @ /dev/cu.usbserial-11320 (19200 baud)
  IC-705 (0xA4) @ /dev/cu.usbserial-54321 (115200 baud)

Found 3 radios (1 LAN, 2 serial)

    JSON Output (optional)¶

    $ icom-lan discover --json
{
  "lan": [
    {"model": "IC-7610", "host": "192.168.55.40", "port": 50001}
  ],
  "serial": [
    {"model": "IC-7610", "address": 152, "port": "/dev/cu.usbserial-11320", "baud": 19200},
    {"model": "IC-705", "address": 164, "port": "/dev/cu.usbserial-54321", "baud": 115200}
  ]
}

    Files¶

    • src/icom_lan/cli.py — update discover command
    • src/icom_lan/discovery.py — add discover_serial_radios() wrapper
    • docs/guide/quickstart.md — add serial discovery example
    • docs/guide/cli.md — document flags
    • README.md — update Quick Start
    • tests/integration/test_discovery.py (new) — integration test

    Dependencies¶

    • Depends on #223 (enumeration)
    • Depends on #224 (probe)
    • Depends on #225 (model ID)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] icom-lan discover finds both LAN and serial radios
    • [ ] Output clearly shows connection type (LAN vs serial)
    • [ ] --serial-only and --lan-only flags work
    • [ ] Discovery completes in < 5s (parallel LAN + serial)
    • [ ] Docs updated with examples
    • [ ] Integration test passes (requires USB radio)

    Documentation Updates¶

    docs/guide/quickstart.md¶

    ## Discovery

### Automatic Discovery

Find radios on your network and USB:

\`\`\`bash
icom-lan discover
\`\`\`

Output:

\`\`\`
LAN radios:
  IC-7610 @ 192.168.55.40 (LAN)

Serial radios:
  IC-7610 (0x98) @ /dev/cu.usbserial-11320 (19200 baud)
\`\`\`

### Serial-only Discovery

\`\`\`bash
icom-lan discover --serial-only
\`\`\`

    ⬜ #227: lint: Clean up 94 unused imports in init.py (F401)¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Fix 94 ruff F401 (unused-import) warnings in src/icom_lan/__init__.py.

    Problem¶

    __init__.py imports 94 symbols that are not used in the file itself, causing ruff to flag them as unused:

    F401 .auth.AuthResponse imported but unused; consider removing, adding to __all__, or using a redundant alias
F401 .auth.StatusResponse imported but unused; ...
... (94 total)

    Root cause: These symbols are imported for public API exposure but are not in __all__ (which currently has 44 symbols).

    Verification¶

    uv run ruff check src/icom_lan/__init__.py --select F401

    Expected: 94 F401 errors.

    Solution¶

    Option A (recommended): Add all 94 symbols to __all__ if they are part of public API:

    __all__ = [
    # Existing 44 symbols
    "IcomRadio",
    "create_radio",
    # ...

    # Add these 94:
    "AuthResponse",
    "StatusResponse",
    "build_conninfo_packet",
    # ... etc
]

    Option B: Remove imports that are not part of public API (internal helpers only).

    Option C: Add redundant aliases (AuthResponse as AuthResponse) — but this is noisy.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] uv run ruff check src/icom_lan/__init__.py --select F401 → 0 errors
    • [ ] Public API surface unchanged (or explicitly documented if reduced)
    • [ ] __all__ accurately reflects exported symbols
    • [ ] All tests pass

    Priority¶

    P2 — Quality improvement, not blocking functionality.

    Labels¶

    type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    ⬜ #228: lint: Fix 6 module import order warnings (E402)¶

    Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    Summary¶

    Fix 6 ruff E402 (module-import-not-at-top-of-file) warnings in sync.py and _shared_state_runtime.py.

    Problem¶

    Imports are placed after module-level code (TypeVar definitions, docstrings), causing ruff to flag them:

    E402 Module level import not at top of file
  --> src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py:19:1
E402 Module level import not at top of file
  --> src/icom_lan/sync.py:21:1
... (6 total)

    Affected files: - src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py (2 warnings) — imports after docstring - src/icom_lan/sync.py (4 warnings) — imports after TypeVar("T") definition

    Root Cause¶

    Intentional pattern to avoid: 1. Circular import issues (sync.py needs TypeVar defined before importing classes that use it) 2. PEP 257 compliance (docstring before imports in _shared_state_runtime.py)

    Verification¶

    uv run ruff check src/ --select E402

    Expected: 6 E402 errors.

    Solution¶

    Option A (quick fix): Add # noqa: E402 to suppress warnings:

    # src/icom_lan/sync.py
T = TypeVar("T")

from .audio import AudioPacket  # noqa: E402
from .radio import IcomRadio as _AsyncIcomRadio  # noqa: E402
# ... etc

    Option B (refactor): Restructure to move TypeVar/docstring logic: - Move TypeVar to separate _types.py module - Move docstring to end of file (non-standard)

    Recommendation: Option A — cleanest, preserves working circular-import avoidance.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] uv run ruff check src/ --select E402 → 0 errors
    • [ ] No circular import regressions
    • [ ] All tests pass

    Priority¶

    P2 — Quality improvement, not blocking functionality.

    Labels¶

    type:tech-debt, priority:P2

    ⬜ #229: Fix flaky test: test_soft_reconnect_handles_connect_failure¶

    Labels: bug

    Problem¶

    test_soft_reconnect_handles_connect_failure is currently skipped in CI due to mock not preventing real IcomTransport.connect() execution.

    Symptom: Test hangs in CI with 10 retry attempts (~50 seconds), then fails with TimeoutError. Status: Passes locally, fails in CI.

    Root Cause¶

    Mock patch on icom_lan.radio.IcomTransport doesn't intercept transport creation inside soft_reconnect() in CI environment. Possible async timing or import caching issue.

    Current Workaround¶

    Test is skipped with @pytest.mark.skip to achieve green CI.

    Fix Options¶

    1. Patch at a different level (e.g., patch IcomTransport.__init__)
    2. Use fixture-level mock instead of inline patch
    3. Refactor soft_reconnect() to make transport creation injectable
    4. Accept as integration test and run only locally

    Location¶

    tests/test_radio_coverage.py:1402

    Related¶

    • CI fix commits: 5f0b23a, 7c4e4d2, 0789ebe
    • Original issue: Epic #222 (serial discovery)

    ⬜ #230: Fix flaky test: test_audio_bridge_smoke_with_serial_backend_audio_driver¶

    Labels: bug

    Problem¶

    test_audio_bridge_smoke_with_serial_backend_audio_driver is currently skipped in CI due to race condition in audio frame propagation timing.

    Symptom: Test assertion assert fake_sd.output_stream.writes fails (empty list) in CI. Status: Passes locally, fails in CI.

    Root Cause¶

    Audio frames don't propagate through the bridge within the 1-second deadline in CI environment. Likely due to: - Slower async event loop scheduling in CI - Different timing characteristics of CI runners - Potential sounddevice mock setup delay

    Current Workaround¶

    Test is skipped with @pytest.mark.skip to achieve green CI.

    Fix Options¶

    1. Increase deadline from 1.0s to 5.0s
    2. Add explicit frame flush/pump mechanism
    3. Use more reliable synchronization (e.g., asyncio.Event instead of polling)
    4. Mock at a higher level to avoid timing dependency
    5. Accept as integration test requiring real audio device

    Location¶

    tests/test_serial_backend_smoke.py:363

    Related¶

    • CI fix commit: 04d87ec
    • Audio bridge implementation: src/icom_lan/audio_bridge.py

    ✅ #233: bug: Waterfall double-rendering from duplicate scope subscriptions¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem\nTwo separate scope WS subscriptions → waterfall renders twice per frame.\n\n## Location\nSpectrumPanel.svelte + WaterfallCanvas.svelte\n\n## Fix\nConsolidate to single scope subscription, share data via store.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Critical Bugs)¶

    ✅ #234: bug: WS command responses not handled → silent failures¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem\nBackend sends {"status":"error"} but frontend ignores → no user feedback on failures.\n\n## Location\nws-client.ts message handler\n\n## Fix\nAdd response handling, show toast/console error on failures.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Critical Bugs)¶

    ✅ #235: bug: BottomBar audioManager.onChange listener leak¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem\nonChange listener registered but never cleaned up → memory leak on component unmount.\n\n## Location\nBottomBar.svelte\n\n## Fix\nUse $effect cleanup or explicit onDestroy.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Critical Bugs)¶

    ✅ #236: arch: Replace triple HTTP polling with WS state push¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem\n3 separate XHR polls (/state, /capabilities, /bridge) every 200-500ms → inefficient, high latency.\n\n## Solution\nPush state updates via control WebSocket.\n\n## Dependencies\nBlocks #176 (reduce WS conns), enables #1710 (state delta).\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Architecture)¶

    ✅ #237: arch: Reduce WebSocket connections (iOS 6-conn limit)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem\niOS Safari has 6 HTTP connection limit. 4 WS (control/scope/meters/audio) + keepalive = problems.\n\n## Solution\nMerge control/meters into one WS, or use HTTP/2.\n\n## Dependencies\nRequires #175 (WS state push).\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Architecture)¶

    ✅ #238: arch: Remove Connection:close, enable HTTP keepalive¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem\nBackend forces Connection: close → every request = new TCP handshake.\n\n## Solution\nRemove forced close, let browser reuse connections.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Architecture)¶

    ✅ #239: bug: Optimistic update frequency snap mismatch¶

    Labels: bug, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem\nOptimistic freq updates snap to different precision than server → visual flicker.\n\n## Solution\nMatch client-side rounding to server logic.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Architecture)¶

    ✅ #240: resilience: XHR polling error backoff + stale indicator¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem\nXHR poll failures retry immediately → hammers server, no user feedback.\n\n## Solution\nExponential backoff (200ms → 5s), show "reconnecting" indicator.\n\n## Dependencies\nEnables #1703 (stale state detection).\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Resilience)¶

    ✅ #241: resilience: PTT button timeout protection¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P0, area:web-ui

    Problem\nPTT stuck = transmit forever if WS drops.\n\n## Solution\nClient-side timeout (30s max), auto-release on WS disconnect.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Resilience)¶

    ✅ #242: resilience: Stale state detection and warning¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem\nNo indication when state is outdated (poll stopped, WS dead).\n\n## Solution\nTimestamp checks, show "stale data" warning after 5s.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Resilience)¶

    ✅ #243: resilience: Coordinate Audio/Control WS state¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem\nAudio WS can be alive while control WS is dead → confusing state.\n\n## Solution\nUnified connection health store, disable audio UI when control down.\n\n## Dependencies\nRequires #175 (WS state push).\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Resilience)¶

    ✅ #244: resilience: App.svelte limit reload retries¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem\nInfinite reload loop on persistent errors.\n\n## Solution\nMax 3 retries, then show error message.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Resilience)¶

    ✅ #245: improve: Set Vite build.target for Safari 14+ compatibility¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem\nDefault target may break on older iOS.\n\n## Solution\nSet build.target: [es2020, safari14] in vite.config.ts.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Improvements)¶

    ✅ #246: improve: Pause RAF loop when tab hidden (Page Visibility)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem\nWaterfall/spectrum render when tab inactive → wasted CPU.\n\n## Solution\nUse Page Visibility API to pause RAF loop.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Improvements)¶

    ⬜ #247: improve: Add basic LAN authentication (token/PIN)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P3, area:web-ui

    Problem\nNo auth → anyone on LAN can control radio.\n\n## Solution\nOptional token-based auth, configured via env var.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Improvements)¶

    ⬜ #248: improve: State delta / ETag 304 instead of full JSON snapshot¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P3, area:web-ui

    Problem\n/api/v1/state returns full 2KB JSON every poll.\n\n## Solution\nETag + 304 Not Modified, or delta updates.\n\n## Dependencies\nRequires #175 (WS state push) for full benefit.\n\n## Epic\nPart of #169 (Improvements)¶

    ✅ #249: arch: Eliminate hardcoded IC-7610 assumptions¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    UI hardcodes IC-7610 modes/filters/features - won't work with IC-705/7300.

    Solution¶

    Drive all UI options from /api/v1/capabilities response.

    Epic¶

    Part of #169 (Architecture)

    ✅ #250: improve: Wire up missing UI panels¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

    Problem¶

    5 UI panels exist but aren't connected to backend commands: Settings, Antenna, Tuner, Split/RIT, AGC.

    Solution¶

    Implement handlers for each panel.

    Dependencies¶

    Requires capabilities-driven UI.

    Epic¶

    Part of #169 (Improvements)

    ⬜ #251: epic: Data-driven rig profiles — TOML config + IC-7300 enablement¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Summary¶

    Replace hardcoded radio profiles and command wire bytes with data-driven TOML rig files. Adding a new radio model ≈ adding a new .toml file.

    Sub-Issues¶

    • [x] #252 Phase 0: Schema design (rigs/_schema.md, _template.toml) ✅
    • [x] #253 Phase 1: rig_loader.py + command_map.py ✅
    • [x] #254 Phase 2: ic7610.toml migration ✅
    • [x] #255 Phase 3: Wire commands.py to CommandMap (223/223 functions) ✅
    • [x] #256 Phase 4: ic7300.toml (14 command overrides) ✅
    • [x] #257 Phase 5: IC-7300 hardware smoke test ✅ (14/14 commands verified)
    • [x] #258 Phase 6: Web UI capability guards ✅
    • [ ] #259 Documentation update ⏳

    Stats¶

    • +3497 lines / -85 lines across 20 files
    • 236 new tests, 3298 total (zero regressions)
    • 7 commits merged to main via feature/rig-schema
    • Hardware verified: IC-7300 over USB serial, 14/14 CI-V commands pass

    ✅ #252: feat: Rig TOML schema design + format spec (#251 Phase 0)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Parent Epic: #251¶

    Goal¶

    Design the TOML schema for rig configuration files and document it in rigs/_schema.md.

    Deliverables¶

    1. rigs/_schema.md — human-readable format specification
    2. Example snippet showing all sections with comments
    3. Validation rules (required vs optional fields, value constraints)

    Schema Sections¶

    [radio]           # Model identity, CIV address, receiver count
[spectrum]        # Scope parameters (seq_max, amp_max, len_max)
[capabilities]    # Feature flags (list of capability strings)
[freq_ranges]     # Supported frequency ranges with band plans
[modes]           # Supported operating modes
[filters]         # Available IF filters
[vfo]             # VFO scheme (ab vs main_sub) + wire bytes
[commands]        # Default CI-V command wire bytes
[commands.overrides]  # Model-specific wire byte overrides

    Design Constraints¶

    • Use Python stdlib tomllib (3.11+) — no external deps
    • All wire bytes as hex integer arrays: [0x14, 0x01]
    • Capabilities as string lists matching existing RadioProfile.capabilities
    • Must support inheritance/defaults: commands shared across models defined once
    • Schema must be self-documenting (comments in TOML)

    Agent Instructions¶

    1. Read current src/icom_lan/profiles.py for existing RadioProfile fields
    2. Read references/wfview/rigs/IC-7610.rig and IC-7300.rig for reference data
    3. Read src/icom_lan/commands.py for all command wire bytes
    4. Design schema that captures ALL data currently in profiles.py + commands.py
    5. Write rigs/_schema.md with full specification
    6. Create example rigs/_template.toml with all fields commented

    TDD¶

    • Write tests/test_rig_schema.py with schema validation tests FIRST
    • Test: valid TOML parses without errors
    • Test: missing required fields raise clear errors
    • Test: invalid capability names rejected
    • Test: wire byte arrays validated (non-empty, valid hex range 0x00-0xFF)

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] rigs/_schema.md documents every field with type, required/optional, description
    • [ ] rigs/_template.toml is valid TOML and parseable
    • [ ] Schema validation tests pass
    • [ ] Schema covers 100% of RadioProfile fields
    • [ ] Schema covers 100% of command wire bytes in commands.py

    Files to Read (context)¶

    • src/icom_lan/profiles.py — RadioProfile dataclass
    • src/icom_lan/commands.py — all CI-V command builders
    • src/icom_lan/radios.py — model registry
    • references/wfview/rigs/IC-7610.rig — wfview reference (for data, not format)
    • references/wfview/rigs/IC-7300.rig — wfview reference

    Effort: S (1-2 hours)¶

    Dependencies: none¶

    ✅ #253: feat: rig_loader.py — TOML parser + validator + RadioProfile builder (#251 Phase 1)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Parent Epic: #251¶

    Goal¶

    Implement src/icom_lan/rig_loader.py — the runtime engine that loads .toml rig files and produces RadioProfile + CommandMap objects.

    Deliverables¶

    1. src/icom_lan/rig_loader.py with:
    2. load_rig(path: Path) -> RigConfig — parse + validate single TOML file
    3. discover_rigs(rigs_dir: Path) -> dict[str, RigConfig] — load all rigs from directory
    4. RigConfig dataclass holding parsed data
    5. build_profile(rig: RigConfig) -> RadioProfile — convert to existing RadioProfile
    6. build_command_map(rig: RigConfig) -> CommandMap — build wire byte lookup

    7. Validation with clear error messages (missing fields, invalid bytes, etc.)

    8. src/icom_lan/command_map.py with:

    9. CommandMap class — get(command_name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]
    10. Default commands (shared across all Icom radios) + per-model overrides
    11. __contains__ for capability checks

    Design¶

    @dataclass(frozen=True)
class CommandMap:
    """Rig-specific CI-V command wire byte mapping."""
    _commands: dict[str, tuple[int, ...]]

    def get(self, name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
        """Get wire bytes for command name. Raises KeyError if unsupported."""
        ...

    def has(self, name: str) -> bool:
        """Check if command is supported by this rig."""
        ...

    TDD — Write Tests FIRST¶

    tests/test_rig_loader.py:

    # Test: load valid TOML → RigConfig with all fields populated
# Test: load TOML with missing required field → RigLoadError with field name
# Test: load TOML with invalid CIV address (>0xFF) → validation error
# Test: load TOML with empty wire bytes → validation error
# Test: build_profile produces RadioProfile matching current IC-7610 hardcoded profile
# Test: build_command_map produces CommandMap with all expected commands
# Test: CommandMap.get returns correct wire bytes
# Test: CommandMap.has returns False for unsupported commands
# Test: discover_rigs loads all .toml files from directory
# Test: discover_rigs ignores files starting with underscore (_schema.md, _template.toml)

    Agent Instructions¶

    1. Read rigs/_schema.md (from #252) for field definitions
    2. Read src/icom_lan/profiles.py for RadioProfile dataclass
    3. Write tests FIRST in tests/test_rig_loader.py
    4. Implement src/icom_lan/rig_loader.py to pass tests
    5. Implement src/icom_lan/command_map.py to pass tests
    6. Run full test suite — zero regressions

    Constraints¶

    • Python stdlib only (tomllib, dataclasses, pathlib)
    • Frozen dataclasses (immutable after load)
    • Thread-safe (load once at startup, read-only after)
    • Clear error messages: RigLoadError("ic7300.toml: missing required field [radio].civ_address")

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] load_rig() successfully parses ic7610.toml and ic7300.toml
    • [ ] build_profile() output matches current hardcoded RadioProfile for IC-7610
    • [ ] CommandMap.get() returns correct wire bytes for all commands
    • [ ] Validation catches all malformed inputs with clear messages
    • [ ] All existing tests pass (zero regressions)
    • [ ] New tests: ≥15 unit tests

    Files to Create¶

    • src/icom_lan/rig_loader.py
    • src/icom_lan/command_map.py
    • tests/test_rig_loader.py

    Files to Read (context)¶

    • rigs/_schema.md (from #252)
    • src/icom_lan/profiles.py
    • src/icom_lan/commands.py

    Effort: M (2-4 hours)¶

    Dependencies: #252¶

    ✅ #254: feat: ic7610.toml — migrate IC-7610 profile from Python to TOML (#251 Phase 2)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Parent Epic: #251¶

    Goal¶

    Create rigs/ic7610.toml — the reference rig file for IC-7610, migrating all hardcoded data from profiles.py and commands.py into TOML format.

    This is the most critical sub-issue: IC-7610 is our primary radio and the file must be byte-for-byte accurate.

    Deliverables¶

    1. rigs/ic7610.toml — complete IC-7610 rig configuration
    2. Verification test that loaded TOML matches current hardcoded data exactly

    Data Sources (authoritative)¶

    1. src/icom_lan/profiles.py → _PROFILES["IC-7610"] (capabilities, civ_addr, cmd29_routes, vfo codes)
    2. src/icom_lan/commands.py → all build_frame() calls (wire bytes for every command)
    3. references/wfview/rigs/IC-7610.rig → cross-reference (134 commands, spectrum params)
    4. docs/parity/ic7610_command_matrix.json → parity status for all 134 commands

    Key IC-7610 Properties¶

    CIV address: 0x98
Receivers: 2 (Main + Sub)
Command29: true (selective, see cmd29_routes)
Spectrum: true (via LAN UDP, not CI-V)
LAN: true
VFO scheme: main_sub (0xD0=Main, 0xD1=Sub, 0xB0=Swap)

    TDD — Write Tests FIRST¶

    tests/test_rig_ic7610.py:

    # Test: ic7610.toml loads without validation errors
# Test: loaded profile.civ_addr == 0x98
# Test: loaded profile.receiver_count == 2
# Test: loaded profile.capabilities == current _DUAL_CAPS
# Test: loaded cmd29_routes match current _CMD29_7610 exactly
# Test: loaded vfo_main_code == 0xD0, vfo_sub_code == 0xD1, vfo_swap_code == 0xB0
# Test: CommandMap has ALL commands from commands.py (complete parity)
# Test: CommandMap wire bytes match hardcoded values for EVERY command
# Test: spectrum params match wfview reference (seq_max, amp_max, len_max)
# Test: freq_ranges cover 30kHz-60MHz
# Test: modes include all 8 HF modes

    Agent Instructions¶

    1. Read src/icom_lan/profiles.py — extract ALL IC-7610 data
    2. Read src/icom_lan/commands.py — extract ALL wire bytes (grep for build_frame, bytes([0x)
    3. Cross-reference with references/wfview/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    4. Cross-reference with docs/parity/ic7610_command_matrix.json
    5. Write tests FIRST
    6. Create rigs/ic7610.toml to pass tests
    7. Run full suite — zero regressions

    Verification Strategy¶

    The key test is round-trip parity: load ic7610.toml → build RadioProfile + CommandMap → compare field-by-field with current hardcoded values. Any mismatch = test failure.

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] rigs/ic7610.toml is valid TOML, loads via rig_loader
    • [ ] 100% field parity with current _PROFILES["IC-7610"]
    • [ ] 100% wire byte parity with current commands.py for all implemented commands
    • [ ] cmd29_routes match exactly
    • [ ] All existing tests pass (zero regressions)
    • [ ] New tests: ≥10 parity verification tests

    Files to Create¶

    • rigs/ic7610.toml
    • tests/test_rig_ic7610.py

    Files to Read (context)¶

    • src/icom_lan/profiles.py
    • src/icom_lan/commands.py (ALL of it — ~800 lines)
    • references/wfview/rigs/IC-7610.rig
    • docs/parity/ic7610_command_matrix.json

    Effort: M (3-4 hours — lots of data extraction)¶

    Dependencies: #252, #253¶

    ✅ #255: refactor: Wire commands.py to use CommandMap from rig profiles (#251 Phase 3)¶

    Labels: enhancement, type:tech-debt, priority:P1

    Parent Epic: #251¶

    Goal¶

    Refactor src/icom_lan/commands.py so command builders use CommandMap wire bytes from loaded rig profiles instead of hardcoded byte literals. This is the core refactor that makes multi-radio support work.

    Current State¶

    # commands.py — hardcoded IC-7610 wire bytes
def get_af_gain() -> bytes:
    return bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, ADDR, 0xE0, 0x14, 0x01, 0xFD])

def get_s_meter() -> bytes:
    return bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, ADDR, 0xE0, 0x15, 0x02, 0xFD])

    Target State¶

    # commands.py — rig-driven wire bytes
def get_af_gain(cmd_map: CommandMap) -> bytes:
    return build_frame(cmd_map.get("af_gain"))

def get_s_meter(cmd_map: CommandMap) -> bytes:
    return build_frame(cmd_map.get("s_meter"))

    Strategy: Incremental Migration¶

    Do NOT rewrite commands.py in one pass. Use adapter pattern:

    Step 1: Add CommandMap parameter with backward-compatible default¶

    _DEFAULT_CMD_MAP: CommandMap | None = None  # Set at startup from rig loader

def get_af_gain(cmd_map: CommandMap | None = None) -> bytes:
    cm = cmd_map or _DEFAULT_CMD_MAP
    if cm is not None:
        return build_frame(cm.get("af_gain"))
    # Legacy fallback: hardcoded bytes
    return bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, ADDR, 0xE0, 0x14, 0x01, 0xFD])

    Step 2: Wire rig loader into startup (set _DEFAULT_CMD_MAP)¶

    Step 3: Verify all tests still pass with rig-loaded CommandMap¶

    Step 4: Remove legacy hardcoded fallbacks (once confident)¶

    TDD — Write Tests FIRST¶

    tests/test_command_map_integration.py:

    # Test: every command in commands.py has matching entry in ic7610 CommandMap
# Test: build_frame with CommandMap produces identical bytes as hardcoded version
# Test: CommandMap override (ic7300) produces different bytes for overridden commands
# Test: CommandMap override does NOT affect non-overridden commands
# Test: missing command in CommandMap raises clear error (not silent fallback)
# Test: all callers of commands.py (radio.py, handlers.py, poller) work with CommandMap

    Agent Instructions¶

    1. Read src/icom_lan/commands.py COMPLETELY — understand every function
    2. Read src/icom_lan/command_map.py (from #253) — understand CommandMap API
    3. Count all command functions: grep -c "^def " src/icom_lan/commands.py
    4. Write tests FIRST
    5. Implement Step 1 (add CommandMap param with default)
    6. Run full suite after EACH step — zero regressions at every checkpoint
    7. Do NOT remove legacy fallbacks yet (Step 4 is separate)

    Risk Mitigation¶

    • Highest risk refactor in this epic — touches all command paths
    • Every existing test MUST pass at every intermediate step
    • Backward-compatible default means no caller changes needed initially
    • Radio.py, handlers.py, poller.py callers updated AFTER commands.py is stable

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] All command builders accept optional CommandMap parameter
    • [ ] When CommandMap provided, wire bytes come from map (not hardcoded)
    • [ ] When CommandMap is None, legacy hardcoded bytes used (backward compat)
    • [ ] ic7610.toml CommandMap produces identical output to hardcoded bytes
    • [ ] All 3200+ existing tests pass (zero regressions)
    • [ ] New tests: ≥10 integration tests

    Files to Modify¶

    • src/icom_lan/commands.py (primary)
    • src/icom_lan/radio.py (wire CommandMap through)
    • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py (wire CommandMap through)
    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py (wire CommandMap through)

    Files to Read (context)¶

    • src/icom_lan/command_map.py (from #253)
    • rigs/ic7610.toml (from #254)

    Effort: L (4-6 hours — many callers, high risk)¶

    Dependencies: #253, #254¶

    ✅ #256: feat: ic7300.toml — IC-7300 rig file with command overrides (#251 Phase 4)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Parent Epic: #251¶

    Goal¶

    Create rigs/ic7300.toml — complete IC-7300 rig configuration with command wire byte overrides where they differ from IC-7610.

    Deliverables¶

    1. rigs/ic7300.toml — IC-7300 rig configuration
    2. Tests verifying overrides and unique capabilities

    IC-7300 Key Properties¶

    CIV address: 0x94
Receivers: 1 (single)
Command29: false (none)
Spectrum: true (via CI-V serial, SpectrumSeqMax=11, LenMax=475)
LAN: false
VFO scheme: ab (A=0x07 0x00, B=0x07 0x01, Equal=0x07 0xA0, Swap=0x07 0xB0)

    Command Wire Overrides (14 commands differ from IC-7610)¶

    From wfview reference analysis:

    Command IC-7610 IC-7300
    acc1_mod_level 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x88 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x64
    usb_mod_level 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x89 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x65
    data_off_mod_input 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x91 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x66
    data1_mod_input 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x92 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x67
    civ_transceive 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x12 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x71
    system_date 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x58 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x94
    system_time 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x59 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x95
    utc_offset 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x62 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x96
    quick_split 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x33 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x30
    nb_depth 0x1A 0x05 0x02 0x90 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x89
    nb_width 0x1A 0x05 0x02 0x91 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x90
    s_meter_sql_status 0x15 0x01 0x16 0x43
    civ_output_ant 0x1C 0x04 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x61
    agc_time_constant 0x1A 0x04 0x1A 0x04

    Capabilities (IC-7300 _SINGLE_CAPS + extras)¶

    Core: audio, scope, meters, tx, cw, rf_gain, af_level, squelch DSP: attenuator, preamp, nb, nr Unique: audio_eq (SSB/AM/FM Bass/Treble controls)

    NOT supported: dual_rx, digisel, ip_plus, drive_gain, rx_antenna

    Commands NOT available on IC-7300¶

    These commands from IC-7610 should NOT be in ic7300.toml: - Main/Sub Prefix (0x29), VFO Dual Watch, VFO Main/Sub Select - RX Antenna, DIGI-SEL Shift, Drive Gain, Data Mode Filter - Quick Dual Watch, LAN Mod Level, DATA2/DATA3 Mod Input - XFC Status, Scope Main/Sub

    TDD — Write Tests FIRST¶

    tests/test_rig_ic7300.py:

    # Test: ic7300.toml loads without validation errors
# Test: profile.civ_addr == 0x94
# Test: profile.receiver_count == 1
# Test: profile.capabilities includes attenuator, preamp, nb, nr
# Test: profile.capabilities does NOT include dual_rx, digisel, ip_plus
# Test: CommandMap override: s_meter_sql_status == (0x16, 0x43)
# Test: CommandMap override: system_date == (0x1A, 0x05, 0x00, 0x94)
# Test: CommandMap non-override: get_freq == same as IC-7610
# Test: CommandMap non-override: get_s_meter == same as IC-7610
# Test: CommandMap does NOT have dual_rx commands
# Test: vfo scheme is "ab" with correct wire bytes
# Test: cmd29_routes is empty (no Command29)
# Test: spectrum params match wfview (seq_max=11, amp_max=160, len_max=475)

    Agent Instructions¶

    1. Read rigs/ic7610.toml (from #254) as template
    2. Read references/wfview/rigs/IC-7300.rig for all IC-7300 data
    3. Read the override table above for the 14 differing commands
    4. Write tests FIRST
    5. Create rigs/ic7300.toml modifying only what differs
    6. Run full suite — zero regressions

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] rigs/ic7300.toml loads via rig_loader without errors
    • [ ] All 14 overrides have correct wire bytes
    • [ ] Capabilities are accurate (no dual_rx, has scope)
    • [ ] VFO scheme is ab (not main_sub)
    • [ ] All existing tests pass (zero regressions)
    • [ ] New tests: ≥12 unit tests

    Files to Create¶

    • rigs/ic7300.toml
    • tests/test_rig_ic7300.py

    Files to Read (context)¶

    • rigs/ic7610.toml (from #254)
    • references/wfview/rigs/IC-7300.rig
    • src/icom_lan/profiles.py (current _SINGLE_CAPS)

    Effort: S (1-2 hours)¶

    Dependencies: #252, #253, #254¶

    ✅ #257: test: IC-7300 serial smoke test + hardware validation (#251 Phase 5)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

    Parent Epic: #251¶

    Goal¶

    Validate IC-7300 works end-to-end via USB serial: discovery → connect → freq/mode/PTT/meters/scope.

    Prerequisites¶

    • IC-7300 connected via USB (CI-V serial + USB audio)
    • All previous phases merged (#252-#256)

    Test Plan¶

    1. Discovery¶

    icom-lan discover --serial-only
# Expected: IC-7300 found at /dev/cu.usbserial-XXXX, baud=19200 (or auto), CIV addr=0x94

    2. Basic Commands¶

    icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-XXXX status
icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial freq
icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial mode
icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial meter

    3. Web UI¶

    icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial web --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080
# Verify: Web UI shows single receiver, no SUB controls
# Verify: Frequency/mode display correct
# Verify: Meters (S-meter, power, SWR) updating
# Verify: Scope/waterfall rendering (if supported via CI-V serial)

    4. rigctld Compatibility¶

    icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial serve
# Test with WSJT-X or rigctl client

    5. Audio Bridge (if icom-lan[bridge] installed)¶

    icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial --list-audio-devices
# Verify IC-7300 USB audio device detected
# Test RX audio playback

    What to Document¶

    • Exact USB device path
    • Working baud rate
    • Any commands that fail (log with exact CI-V bytes)
    • Scope behavior (does it work via serial CI-V?)
    • Audio device names
    • Any IC-7300-specific quirks

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] IC-7300 discovered automatically
    • [ ] freq/mode/PTT/meters work via CLI
    • [ ] Web UI renders correctly (single receiver layout)
    • [ ] rigctld serves correct frequency/mode
    • [ ] Any failures documented with exact CI-V trace

    Effort: S-M (1-3 hours, depends on quirks found)¶

    Dependencies: #252-#256 merged, IC-7300 hardware connected¶

    ✅ #258: feat: Web UI capability guards for single-receiver radios (#251 Phase 6)¶

    Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

    Parent Epic: #251¶

    Goal¶

    Ensure the Web UI adapts to rig capabilities, hiding controls not supported by the connected radio.

    Current State¶

    Web UI assumes dual-receiver IC-7610: - SUB receiver panel visible - Dual-watch toggle visible - VFO Main/Sub naming - Scope Main/Sub toggle

    Target State¶

    When rig profile has receiver_count=1: - Hide SUB receiver panel - Hide dual-watch toggle - Show VFO A/B naming (not Main/Sub) - Hide Scope Main/Sub toggle

    When capabilities are missing: - Hide DIGI-SEL controls if digisel not in capabilities - Hide IP+ toggle if ip_plus not in capabilities - Hide RX Antenna selector if no rx_antenna capability

    Implementation¶

    Backend: /api/v1/info response enrichment¶

    {
  "model": "IC-7300",
  "receivers": 1,
  "capabilities": ["audio", "scope", "meters", "tx", "cw", ...],
  "vfo_scheme": "ab",
  "has_spectrum": true,
  "has_lan": false
}

    Frontend: Conditional rendering¶

    {#if radioInfo.receivers > 1}
  <SubReceiverPanel />
{/if}

{#if radioInfo.capabilities.includes("digisel")}
  <DigiSelControl />
{/if}

    TDD¶

    tests/test_web_capability_guards.py:

    # Test: /api/v1/info returns receiver_count from rig profile
# Test: /api/v1/info returns capabilities list from rig profile
# Test: /api/v1/info returns vfo_scheme from rig profile
# Test: state snapshot omits sub_* fields when receiver_count=1
# Test: command for unavailable capability returns error with explanation

    Agent Instructions¶

    1. Read src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — find InfoHandler, state endpoints
    2. Read frontend/src/ — find receiver/capability-dependent components
    3. Read src/icom_lan/profiles.py — RadioProfile capabilities
    4. Write backend tests FIRST
    5. Update InfoHandler to include rig capabilities in response
    6. Update frontend components with conditional rendering
    7. Run full suite — zero regressions

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] /api/v1/info includes full capability data from rig profile
    • [ ] Frontend hides SUB controls for single-receiver rigs
    • [ ] Frontend uses A/B naming for "ab" VFO scheme
    • [ ] Commands for unsupported capabilities return clear error
    • [ ] All existing tests pass (zero regressions)

    Files to Modify¶

    • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py (InfoHandler)
    • frontend/src/components/ (conditional rendering)
    • frontend/src/lib/stores/ (capability state)

    Effort: S-M (2-3 hours)¶

    Dependencies: #253 (rig_loader), #254 or #256 (rig file loaded at runtime)¶

    ✅ #259: docs: Update documentation for Epic #251 — data-driven rig profiles¶

    Labels: documentation

    Summary¶

    Epic #251 introduced major architectural changes (TOML rig profiles, CommandMap, Web UI guards) but no documentation was updated. This issue covers all necessary doc updates.

    New Docs¶

    • docs/guide/rig-profiles.md — Adding a New Radio (flagship guide)
    • docs/api/rig-loader.md — Rig Loader + CommandMap API reference

    Updated Docs¶

    • docs/guide/radios.md — IC-7300 as tested, rig profiles link
    • docs/api/commands.md — cmd_map parameter docs
    • docs/internals/architecture.md — rig profiles architecture section
    • README.md — multi-radio support, IC-7300
    • docs/CHANGELOG.md — v0.12.0 entry
    • docs/api/web.md — enriched API fields
    • docs/guide/web-ui.md — dynamic VFO labels, capability guards

    Scope¶

    • 2 new docs
    • 7 updated docs
    • Cross-links between all related pages

    Related¶

    • Epic #251 (parent)

    • 252-#258 (implementation, all closed)¶

    Full task spec: /tmp/task-docs-251.md

    ✅ #260: feat: CLI --model flag + auto-resolve radio_addr from rig profiles¶

    Labels: enhancement

    Summary¶

    CLI and API should auto-resolve radio_addr and defaults from rig TOML profiles.

    Current Problem¶

    # Works (IC-7610 default):
icom-lan --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-111 status

# Fails (IC-7300 — no way to set radio_addr via CLI):
icom-lan --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-201 status
# → Uses 0x98 (IC-7610) → timeout

    Desired¶

    # Auto-resolve from rig profile:
icom-lan --model IC-7300 --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-201 status
# → radio_addr=0x94, single receiver, VFO A/B

# Manual override (takes priority):
icom-lan --model IC-7300 --radio-addr 0xA0 --backend serial ... status

    Implementation Plan¶

    1. CLI arguments¶

    • --model MODEL — radio model name (matches rigs/*.toml id or model name)
    • --radio-addr ADDR — explicit CI-V address override (hex or decimal)

    2. Resolution order (for radio_addr)¶

    1. --radio-addr (explicit override, highest priority)
    2. --model → lookup rigs/<model>.toml → [radio].civ_addr
    3. Environment var ICOM_RADIO_ADDR (optional)
    4. Default: 0x98 (IC-7610 backward compat)

    3. Config wiring¶

    • _build_config() in cli.py resolves model → rig profile → fills config fields
    • SerialBackendConfig and LanBackendConfig already have radio_addr and model
    • Just need to wire the CLI args through

    4. Web UI model detection¶

    • When --model is given, WebServer uses it for profile resolution
    • /api/v1/info reflects correct model and capabilities

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] --model IC-7300 resolves radio_addr=0x94 from TOML
    • [ ] --radio-addr 0x94 works standalone
    • [ ] --radio-addr overrides profile default
    • [ ] Default (no flags) = IC-7610 backward compat
    • [ ] Works for both serial and LAN backends
    • [ ] All existing tests pass
    • [ ] --model foo gives clear error with available models

    Related¶

    • Epic #251

    • 252-#258 (rig profiles implementation)¶

    ✅ #261: bug: serial backend — scope health + read error flood on IC-7300¶

    Labels: bug

    Problem¶

    When running web UI with IC-7300 over USB serial, two issues cause log flood and high CPU:

    1. Scope health monitor retries infinitely¶

    IC-7300 scope via CI-V serial uses different commands than IC-7610 LAN scope. The health monitor detects all-zero frames, re-enables scope in a loop. On IC-7300 the scope data never comes via this path → infinite retry.

    2. Serial read error has no backoff¶

    Recoverable serial read error: device reports readiness to read but returned no data fires thousands of times per second with no delay → 34% CPU, 78k log lines in 5 seconds.

    Expected Behavior¶

    1. Scope health monitor should check capability/backend type before enabling scope
    2. Serial read errors should have exponential backoff (100ms → 200ms → 400ms → max 5s)

    Reproduction¶

    uv run python3 -c '
# Launch web server for IC-7300 serial
from icom_lan.backends.config import SerialBackendConfig
# ... connect, start web on port 8081
# → check /tmp/ic7300-web.log
'

    Impact¶

    • High CPU usage (34%)
    • Log files grow to GB in minutes
    • Serial port may become unresponsive

    Workaround¶

    Manually disable scope before starting web server: 

    radio._scope_enabled = False

    ✅ #262: refactor: move polling config to TOML rig profiles (data-driven)¶

    Labels: enhancement

    Problem¶

    Hardcoded Python dicts (_PROFILES, _SINGLE_CAPS, _DUAL_CAPS, _CMD29_7610) duplicate what's already in TOML rig profiles. This caused real bugs: - IC-7300 missing nb, nr, attenuator, preamp capabilities (commit 60c01eb) - Polling intervals hardcoded with _is_serial / has_lan checks instead of profile data - State queries gated on supports_cmd29() — but IC-7300 needs plain CI-V fallback

    Single source of truth = TOML. Everything else derives from it.

    What Must Be Data-Driven (from TOML)¶

    1. Capabilities (currently hardcoded _SINGLE_CAPS / _DUAL_CAPS)¶

    [capabilities]
features = ["audio", "scope", "meters", "tx", "cw", "attenuator", "preamp", "nb", "nr", ...]

    2. Polling config (currently hardcoded _GAP_SERIAL, _FAST_INTERVAL_SERIAL)¶

    [polling]
gap_ms = 50
meter_interval_ms = 200
transceive = true

    3. cmd29 routes (currently hardcoded _CMD29_7610)¶

    Already partially in TOML as [cmd29] but not wired to runtime.

    4. Profile resolution¶

    get_radio_profile() / resolve_radio_profile() must load from TOML via rig_loader.py, not from _PROFILES dict.

    Implementation Plan¶

    1. Extend rig_loader.py to parse [polling] section → RigConfig
    2. profiles.py: get_radio_profile() loads TOML via rig_loader, caches result
    3. Remove _PROFILES, _SINGLE_CAPS, _DUAL_CAPS, _CMD29_7610
    4. RadioPoller.__init__ reads intervals from profile
    5. State query builder reads capabilities and cmd29 routes from profile
    6. Update ic7300.toml and ic7610.toml with [polling] section

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • Zero hardcoded radio-specific Python constants
    • Adding a new radio = adding one TOML file (no Python changes)
    • All existing tests pass (zero regressions)

    Related¶

    • Commit 60c01eb (hotfix: _SINGLE_CAPS missing nb/nr/att/preamp)
    • Commit 5ea7915 (hotfix: serial polling intervals)
    • Epic #251 (rig profiles)

    ✅ #263: feat: USB Audio CODEC bridge for serial-connected radios (IC-7300)¶

    Labels: enhancement

    Problem¶

    IC-7300 connected via USB exposes a standard USB Audio Class device ("USB Audio CODEC") for RX/TX audio. Current Web UI audio pipeline only works with LAN UDP audio (IC-7610).

    USB Audio CODEC¶

    macOS sees it as a standard audio input/output: - Input (mic): RX audio from radio - Output (speaker): TX audio to radio

    This is NOT a serial port — it's a proper USB Audio Class 1.0 device.

    Implementation Plan¶

    1. Use sounddevice or pyaudio to capture from "USB Audio CODEC" input
    2. Encode to Opus (or raw PCM 16-bit mono)
    3. Stream to frontend via existing WebSocket audio path
    4. Frontend Web Audio API decodes and plays

    TX path (reverse): 1. Frontend captures mic → Opus encode → WS 2. Backend decodes → plays to "USB Audio CODEC" output

    Configuration (TOML)¶

    [audio]
mode = "usb"  # "lan" | "usb" | "none"
device_name = "USB Audio CODEC"
sample_rate = 48000
channels = 1

    Related¶

    • IC-7610 uses LAN UDP audio (existing implementation)
    • sounddevice is already an optional dependency (pip install icom-lan[bridge])

    ⬜ #265: epic: Web UI redesign — capability-driven radio panel¶

    Labels: enhancement, ui, area:web-ui

    Summary¶

    Complete Web UI redesign based on the Pencil mockup (mockups/web-ui/radio-panel.pen) — a professional, dark-themed radio control interface inspired by Icom styling.

    Key principle: capability-driven rendering. Every UI element maps to a TOML capability. No ATU → no ATU panel. No dual_rx → single VFO. The mockup represents the maximal UI (all capabilities ON); the frontend conditionally renders based on the connected radio's rig profile.

    Architecture¶

    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  rigs/ic7610.toml                               │
│  [capabilities] features = ["dual_rx", ...]     │
└──────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
           │ GET /api/v1/capabilities
           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  capabilities.svelte.ts (existing store)        │
│  hasCapability('dual_rx'), hasTx(), etc.        │
└──────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  RadioLayout.svelte                             │
│  ┌──────┐ ┌────────────────────┐ ┌──────────┐  │
│  │ Left │ │   Center           │ │  Right   │  │
│  │ Side │ │   Spectrum +       │ │  Side    │  │
│  │ bar  │ │   Waterfall +      │ │  bar     │  │
│  │      │ │   Audio Spectrum   │ │          │  │
│  └──────┘ └────────────────────┘ └──────────┘  │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  Bottom: Dual RX + Meters               │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    Design Reference¶

    • Mockup: mockups/web-ui/radio-panel.pen (Pencil format, 15k lines)
    • Screenshot: mockups/web-ui/radio-panel-screenshot.png
    • Design language: Dark operational UI, Roboto Mono, cyan/orange/red accent system
    • Key colors: bg #0B0E12, accent #00D4FF, TX #FF6A00, danger #FF2020, ok #00CC66

    Phases¶

    Phase 1: Atomic Components (no capability deps)¶

    • [ ] LinearSMeter.svelte — horizontal bar S1-S9/+20/+40/+60, animated, CSS/canvas
    • [ ] FrequencyDisplay.svelte — large mono freq with dot separators
    • [ ] SegmentedButton.svelte — reusable multi-option toggle (ATT, AGC, PREAMP, etc.)
    • [ ] StatusBadge.svelte — pill badge with active/inactive states
    • [ ] BarGauge.svelte — segmented meter (Po/SWR/ALC) with color zones
    • [ ] Slider.svelte — horizontal slider with numeric readout
    • [ ] NeedleGauge.svelte — analog needle meter (SWR/Power)

    Phase 2: Molecule Components (capability-gated)¶

    • [ ] VfoPanel.svelte — freq + mode + s-meter + badges + RIT/XIT
    • [ ] VfoOps.svelte — A↔B, A→B, Split, TX routing
    • [ ] RfFrontEnd.svelte — RF Gain, ATT, Preamp (gated: rf_gain, attenuator, preamp)
    • [ ] AgcPanel.svelte — AGC mode + decay slider
    • [ ] FilterPanel.svelte — Width, Shift, PBT (gated: pbt presence in commands)
    • [ ] RitXitPanel.svelte — RIT/XIT on/off + freq offset (gated: RIT commands)
    • [ ] DspPanel.svelte — NR, NB, Notch, CW Auto Tune (gated: nr, nb)
    • [ ] TxPanel.svelte — PTT state, Mic Gain, ATU, VOX, COMP (gated: tx)
    • [ ] MeterPanel.svelte — needle gauge + digital bar gauges (Po/SWR/ALC)
    • [ ] RxAudioPanel.svelte — monitor mode, AF level, browser audio (gated: audio)
    • [ ] BandSelector.svelte — band grid from TOML freq_ranges (existing, restyle)

    Phase 3: Layout & Integration¶

    • [ ] RadioLayout.svelte — 3-column layout, capability-driven panel visibility
    • [ ] Dual VFO header (conditional on dual_rx)
    • [ ] Bottom dual-RX strip with mini-spectrums (conditional on dual_rx)
    • [ ] CSS variables / design tokens extracted from mockup
    • [ ] Dark theme as default (match mockup palette)
    • [ ] Responsive: desktop-first, tablet/mobile degrade gracefully

    Phase 4: Polish¶

    • [ ] Smooth animations (S-meter, gauge transitions)
    • [ ] Keyboard shortcuts integration
    • [ ] Touch-friendly controls for tablet
    • [ ] Mobile layout adaptation

    TOML Capability Extensions (if needed)¶

    May need to add explicit features to [capabilities]: - rit, xit — currently implied by commands presence - tuner — ATU support - pbt — passband tuning - split — split operation - notch — manual/auto notch

    Alternative: derive from [commands] section (if set_rit_status exists → RIT is available).

    Approach¶

    • Each component built via Claude Code ACP session with Pencil MCP access
    • Pencil batch_get / snapshot_layout → extract exact design params (colors, sizes, fonts)
    • Existing frontend/src/ as reference for API/WS/store integration
    • Canvas components (Spectrum, Waterfall) — keep existing, restyle container only
    • One component per PR/commit for reviewability

    Non-goals¶

    • Rewriting backend API
    • Changing WebSocket protocol
    • Rewriting spectrum/waterfall canvas renderers
    • Mobile-first design (desktop-first, mobile is Phase 4)

    Key Files¶

    File Role
    mockups/web-ui/radio-panel.pen Design source of truth
    frontend/src/lib/stores/capabilities.svelte.ts Capability store (existing)
    frontend/src/lib/types/capabilities.ts Capability types (existing)
    rigs/ic7610.toml Full-capability reference profile
    rigs/ic7300.toml Reduced-capability profile
    frontend/src/components/ Target directory for new components

    ⬜ #266: feat(ui): LinearSMeter atomic component¶

    Labels: enhancement, ui, area:web-ui

    Summary¶

    Create a reusable LinearSMeter.svelte atomic component — a horizontal bar meter matching the Pencil mockup design.

    Parent: #265 (Web UI redesign epic)

    Design Reference¶

    From mockups/web-ui/radio-panel.pen → "Component / S-Meter Linear": - Dark background #060A10, rounded container 10px, border #18202A - Scale labels: S1, S3, S5, S7, S9, +20, +40, +60 (Roboto Mono, 12px, bold, white) - S1-S9 zone: cyan/teal gradient bar - +20/+40/+60 zone: orange→red gradient bar - Thin bar style (4-6px height) with dense tick marks - Current value overlay: e.g. "S7 · −67 dBm" - Smooth animation on value changes (CSS transition or requestAnimationFrame)

    Props Interface¶

    interface LinearSMeterProps {
  value: number;        // 0-255 raw S-meter value from CI-V
  label?: string;       // optional label ("MAIN", "SUB")
  compact?: boolean;    // smaller variant for bottom dual-RX strip
}

    S-Meter Scale Mapping¶

    Raw CI-V S-units dBm
    0 S0 −127
    18 S1 −121
    36 S2 −115
    54 S3 −109
    72 S4 −103
    90 S5 −97
    108 S6 −91
    126 S7 −85
    144 S8 −79
    162 S9 −73
    182 S9+10 −63
    202 S9+20 −53
    222 S9+30 −43
    241 S9+40 −33

    Implementation Notes¶

    • Pure UI component — no radio store dependency, value comes via props
    • Use CSS for the bar (gradient + width transition) — no canvas needed
    • Tick marks: thin vertical lines at each S-unit
    • The +20/+40/+60 section uses a different color (orange→red)
    • compact mode: hide labels, thinner bar, for dual-RX footer strip
    • Accessibility: aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax

    Files¶

    • frontend/src/components/meters/LinearSMeter.svelte — new component
    • frontend/src/components/meters/smeter-utils.ts — scale mapping helpers
    • frontend/src/components/meters/__tests__/LinearSMeter.test.ts — tests

    Acceptance Criteria¶

    • [ ] Renders S1-S9/+20/+40/+60 scale with correct labels
    • [ ] Bar fills proportionally to raw value (0-255)
    • [ ] Cyan gradient for S1-S9, orange-red for over-S9
    • [ ] Smooth CSS transition on value change
    • [ ] Compact mode for dual-RX strip
    • [ ] Shows "S7 · −67 dBm" style value readout
    • [ ] Matches mockup colors/fonts within 95%
    • [ ] No radio store dependency (pure props)
    Made with Material for MkDocs
